## PAGE 00001: 2024x Version News

- page_id: `142934699`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/142934699/2024x+Version+News

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

### Magic Collaboration Studio

Released on: November 10, 2023

Version 2024x of Magic Collaboration Studio introduces updates that enhance your user experience. You can now conveniently visualize branch activity, use a new role to manage user group memberships, and select the exact branches to listen for when creating webhooks. You can also filter out users with locked elements in the Users application, track data markings' changes in the Audit report, and use DSLS licensing framework in addition to FlexNet.

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2024x brings a range of exciting enhancements as well. This version empowers you to customize PDF/HTML templates, cross-reference tables and figures within documents, use a new Wiki document viewing mode for a smoother navigation and editing experience, share specific diagram versions, export tables to Excel, and offers additional smaller improvements.

#### NOTE: 2024x Hot Fix 3 available

2024x Hot Fix 3 available

2024x Hot Fix 3 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes, and vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Hot Fix 3 Version News' space='CATIA']>]]>

#### NOTE: 2024x Hot Fix 2 available

2024x Hot Fix 2 available

2024x Hot Fix 2 is now available for CATIA Magic and No Magic portfolios. It includes a number of improvements and bug fixes, and vulnerability fixes in our modeling tools, plugins, and server products . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Hot Fix 2 Version News' space='CATIA']>]]>

###### Magic Collaboration Studio and Teamwork Cloud Admin

##### Visual Branch Representation

We implemented a visual representation of branch activity, which enhances your experience by helping you to better understand and track merges, branches, and associated commit information.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visual representation of branch activity.

##### New User Group Membership Manager Role

To streamline user group management, we introduce the new User Group Membership Manager role. This role allows you to efficiently add or remove users within specific user groups, thus, you no longer need your administrator’s assistance for routine user group management tasks.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### User Group Membership Manager role permissions and scope.

##### Selecting Branch for Webhooks

When creating a webhook, you can select the specific branch or branches in which the webhook will listen for events. This enhanced control capability is available for resource-scoped webhooks of the commit or tagged commit event type.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Choosing branches in which the webhook will listen for events.

##### Case-Insensitive LDAP Authentication

It has come to our attention that most LDAP servers across our customer base are configured to act as case-insensitiveby default. Therefore, fromnowon the authentication process against external users coming from LDAP servers is case insensitive as well. This eliminates the need to remember the exact caseof your username as set in the organization’s LDAP server.

##### Other fixes/improvements

- A new filter is available in the **Users**application, allowing you to view users with locked elements.
- The **Audit**report now additionally tracks and displays changes in data markings.
- In addition to the Flexnet licensing framework, Magic Collaboration Studio now supports the **DSLS licensing framework**.

##### Deployment Changes

- **Web Application Platform:**Apache Tomcat version requirements have changed. Now the minimum supported version is 10.1.
- **Web Application Platform:**the servlet specification has been changed to Jakarta 10.
- Magic Collaboration Studio no longer supports CentOS 7 due to its approaching EOL. Instead, it now supports Oracle Linux 8.

### Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

##### Customizable Templates for Document Export to PDF/HTML

With Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud 2024x, customizing the appearance of the PDF or HTML files generated from Cameo Collaborator documents has never been easier. Now, you can download the default PDF/HTML template directly from a Cameo Collaborator document. After downloading, you can make any desired changes and then upload the modified template back to the document. Moreover, a single document has the capability to hold multiple PDF/HTML templates. Therefore, this exciting feature empowers you to generate different outputs from the same document, allowing you to satisfy the unique requirements of different recipients. You will be able to alter the generated document's cover page, header, footer, and font, and even add a custom logo to the document.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Uploading a custom PDF/HTML template to a Cameo Collaborator document.

##### Adding Cross References in Cameo Collaborator Documents

One crucial aspect of document creation involves cross-referencing different parts of the document. The new version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a convenient and easy way to incorporate references to any part of a Cameo Collaborator document, including figures and tables located within specific sections. We are confident that this functionality will assist you in producing documents that are both easy to read and navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding a hyperlink to a presentation element in a document section.

##### A New Document Viewing Mode

This release introduces a new document viewing mode - the Wiki view. Similar to the Document view, it hides the details of the model while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view. The Wiki view will provide you with a smoother content editing experience when the data needs to be managed in a wiki-style environment.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Switching to the Wiki view.

##### Enhanced Diagram Sharing Capabilities

Besides being able to acquire a dynamic link to the latest version of a diagram in a Cameo Collaborator document, you now have the option to share a link to the current version of the diagram. This enables recipients to access a specific diagram version, even if the document is updated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Getting the link to a specific (current) version of a diagram.

##### Exporting Tables to Excel

This release introduces a new functionality, which allows you to export tables within Cameo Collaborator documents to the Excel format.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Exporting a table to the Excel format.

##### Other Improvements

- In addition to FlexNet, Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud now supports the DSLS licensing server as well.
- The email notification capability has been improved and now works with mailing servers using SSL/TLS encryption.
- Now, Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings inside tables, ensuring consistency between documents and related server projects.
- The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin allows you to publish a new type of resource - **3D**EXPERIENCE Systems Traceability resource (TRA) - which can be used in the Systems Traceability app on the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- With the improved Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin, you can now select which document to update in case several documents are published from the same project.

## PAGE 00002: Accessing Magic Collaboration Studio web applications

- page_id: `137991596`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991596/Accessing+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+web+applications
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:00:47.330+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**5**

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Magic Collaboration Studio license' space=''] and starting Magic Collaboration Studio, you can log in to Magic Collaboration Studio web applications.

To sign in to Magic Collaboration Studio web applications

1. In an internet browser, go to*http(s)://<server_address>:<port>/webapp*. For example, if the machine IP is **10.1.1.123** and the default port is used, the URL will be *[https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp](https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp)*. The default port is **8****443**.
2. When the authentication page opens, enter your username and password to sign in. The default credentials are **Administrator/****Administrator**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The main Web Application Platform page(*[https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp](https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp))*displayed after signing in.

Once you are logged in, you will be redirected to the landing page depending on your role/ permissions. To navigate to a specific web application, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using the app bar' space='']. Depending on their roles and permissions, some users may not be able to access some specific content in the Magic Collaboration Studio system or view its details.

The first page that opens after successful login varies according to the user role or their latest activity in the Teamwork Cloud Admin.

##### Signing out of Magic Collaboration Studio web applications

To sign out of Magic Collaboration Studio web applications

- In the top right corner click [ATTACHMENT filename='sign_out.png'] button and select Sign out .

For example, if the machine IP is <strong>10.1.1.123</strong> and the default port is used, the URL will be <em><a href="https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp">https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp</a></em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76b071df-da46-4bc9-a2d8-1f7a21c1a8b0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default port is <strong>8</strong><strong>443</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the authentication page opens, enter your username and password to sign in.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28ed6574-b4ed-4c9f-b2bd-1bab834dceab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default credentials are <strong>Administrator/</strong><strong>Administrator</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="signing_into_twc.png" /></ac:image><br /><span class="nolink">The main Web Application Platform page</span><span class="nolink"> (<em style="text-align: center;"><span class="nolink"><a href="https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp">https://10.1.1.123:8443/webapp</a>) </span></em></span><span class="nolink">displayed after signing in.</span></h6></li></ol><p>Once you are logged in, you will be redirected to the landing page depending on your role/ permissions. To navigate to a specific web application, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using the app bar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[app bar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Depending on their roles and permissions, some users may not be able to access some specific content in the Magic Collaboration Studio system or view its details.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1799632d-6f6a-4317-ab77-d1f47a6c8738"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The first page that opens after successful login varies according to the user role or their latest activity in the Teamwork Cloud Admin.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Signing out of Magic Collaboration Studio web applications</h3><p><br /></p><p>To sign out of Magic Collaboration Studio web applications</p><hr /><ul><li>In the top right corner click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sign_out.png" /></ac:image> button and select <strong>Sign out</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992076 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00004: Adding and configuring LDAP server

- page_id: `137992076`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992076/Adding+and+configuring+LDAP+server
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-07-03T08:32:00.872+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

As an example of the process of adding and connecting to the LDAP server, the connection timeout is defined in milliseconds (ms) and specifies the period of response waiting time from the LDAP server.

##### Adding LDAP server

To add an LDAP server

1. Go to **Setting** application > **LDAP management** page.
2. In the right bottom corner of the page click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Create LDAP configuration** page opens.
3. Enter all required data and click the **Save**button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding and configuring a new LDAP server.

The table below describes the components of the **Create LDAP configuration** page.

| UI Component | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Configuration name | Enter the connection name of the LDAP server. Duplicate names are allowed. |
| Domain name | This is a short name identifier for the LDAP server.This field value is to be used in cases when a username conflict is detected during login operation. If the conflicting user is an internal Teamwork Cloud user, use the predefined twc domain to solve the conflict and proceed to log in as an internal user. |
| Server address | Enter the server IP address/hostname. This is a mandatory field and is editable once created. You will get an error message if you enter a duplicate server IP address or hostname. |
| Port | If you need to change the default port number. |
| Connect timeout (ms) | The maximum amount of time in milliseconds for the system to system to successfully authenticate a single server (5000 is the default value). If authentication fails, the system will query the next server in the queue. This field is required. |
| Read timeout (ms) | The maximum amount of time in milliseconds for the system to system to successfully query User DN before requiring a similar authentication service (10000 is the default value). This field is required. |
| Bind as | A mode of bind specifying whether a user connects to the LDAP server with a specific username or anonymously for finding the Distinguished Name (DN) of a user corresponding to the user trying to log into the Teamwork Cloud system.If you select Anonymous, the Username and Password are not required and the system username and password will be disabled. |
| LDAP status | The option to enable a connection with the LDAP server. When disabled users within LDAP will not be able to sign in. |
| Username | The DN of a user to connect to the LDAP server and perform queries. |
| Password | The system password to connect to the LDAP server and perform queries. |
|  | Click to save the LDAP server configuration properties. The function of this button is the same as that of the Save button on the Edit LDAP Configuration page. |
| Specific for authentication data |  |
| Search base | The authentication methods. It defines the location in the directory from which the LDAP search begins. |
| Authenticate using | Select LDAP query to search for users by LDAP query. This is the default option. Select User DN Template to search for users by User DN. |
| User DN | To store a template for mapping user authentication with LDAP servers using the LDAP distinguished names. |
| Users query | An LDAP query for searching, retrieving, and importing users, e.g., (&(cn={0})(objectClass=user)). Note that Users query and User groups query must be different. Both queries work only in the Search base scope. |
| User groups query | An LDAP query for searching, retrieving, and importing user groups, e.g., (&(cn={0})(objectClass=group)). Note that Users query and User groups query must be different. Both queries work only in the Search base scope. |
| Specific for encryption data |  |
| Encryption Protocol | The SSL and TLS are data encryption and authentication for a secure connection with the server. You can select None, SSL/TLS. Selecting None indicates you do not need to use an encryption protocol. |
| LDAP server certificate | The option to select a certificate file. The LDAP Server Certificate file is exported from the LDAP server to make a secure connection between the Teamwork Cloud Admin and LDAP server. Only files with the following extensions may be uploaded: crt, pem. |
|  | To select a certificate file (enabled if SSL/TLS is selected). |
|  | To remove the certificate file (enabled if either SSL/TLS is selected). |

****

**Authentication data**

The**LDAP query**authentication method is selected by default. The Active Directory LDAP attribute name and value should be set to**(sAMAccountName={0})**”. Besides**sAMAccountName**, you can use any attribute name, but it must be followed with “**={0}**”. The authentication information group should look like the following figure.

All LDAP users necessary to connect to Teamwork Cloud reside in *CN=Users*. The **Search Base** of this kind of LDAP server should be *CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com*. The pattern for the **Search Base** is {Parent_Of_LDAP_Users},{Grand_Parent_Of_LDAP_Users},…{n}.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

User DN Template

If the LDAP server is **OpenLDAP** or **ApacheDS**, the default attribute name is **uid**. If the LDAP server is set for **LDAP query**, the LDAP query for querying a user DN should be entered into the **Query** box. Click the following link for more information about the LDAP query [https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2254.txt](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2254.txt).

##### **Configuring encryption data**

The LDAP server connection is secured with SSL/TLS protocol (LDAPS) at default port number *636*. The Encryption Protocol must be SSL/TLS, and the LDAP server certificate file must be selected.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>On this page</strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a81f8d59-05b6-4d07-8841-ecebae155770"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>As an example of the process of adding and connecting to the LDAP server, the connection timeout is defined in milliseconds (ms) and specifies the period of response waiting time from the LDAP server. </p><h3>Adding LDAP server</h3><p>To add an LDAP server</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Go to <strong>Setting</strong> application &gt; <strong>LDAP management</strong> page. </p></li><li><p>In the right bottom corner of the page click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="30"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add_LDAP_directory.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>Search Base</strong> of this kind of LDAP server should be <em>CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com</em>. The pattern for the <strong>Search Base</strong> is {Parent_Of_LDAP_Users},{Grand_Parent_Of_LDAP_Users},…{n}.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ec18326-84e7-48d6-81c7-4976536d083c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Only users that are under the  Search Base will be able to log in using the <strong>User DN Template </strong>authentication method. The Encryption Protocol must be SSL/TLS, and the LDAP server certificate file must be selected.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992628 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00005: Adding hyperlinks

- page_id: `137992628`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992628/Adding+hyperlinks
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:53:07.735+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio supports hyperlinks to web pages, model elements, and document sections in all string-type values. You can also add cross-references to any part of a document section, including images and tables.

##### Adding hyperlinks to web pages

To add a hyperlink to a web page

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
2. Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. In the Edit pane on the right side of the screen, click the Full screen icon.
4. In the toolbar of an open screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperilnk_icon.png'] .
5. In the Adress tab of an open dialog, specify the following information:
  1. In the URL box, enter the web page address to which you want to add a hyperlink.
  2. In the Text to display box, optionally type the text you want to use for the hyperlink. [ATTACHMENT filename='adding_hyperlink.png']
6. Click the Add button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.
7. On the top left corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='exit_full_screen.png'] to exit the full-screen mode.
8. On the top right corner of the Edit pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

##### Adding hyperlinks to model elements

In a Cameo Collaborator document, you can hyperlinks to model elements for easier navigation in the document. Hyperlinks can be represented as element names, representation text, or any custom text. In addition, you have the ability to choose whether hyperlinks are updated when element names change.

To add a hyperlink to a model element

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
2. Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. In the Edit pane on the right side of the screen, click the Full screen icon.
4. In the toolbar of an open screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperilnk_icon.png'] .
5. In the open dialog, go to the Model element tab and specify the following information:
  1. In Text to display drop-down box, select one of the following options:
    - Name - the hyperlink is displayed as the element name.
    - Representation Text - the hyperlink is displayed as the element representation text.
    - Icon + Representation Text - the hyperlink is displayed as the element representation text with the element icon.
    - **Custom Text** - the hyperlink is displayed as your custom text. Type the hyperlink text in the additional **Text to display** box, which appears after selecting the **Custom Text** option. If you use custom text for the hyperlink, it cannot be updated when the element name changes. Therefore, the only available update mode (see the next step) is **Do not update**.
  2. In the Update mode drop-down box, select one of the following options:
    - Automatically updated - the element name or representation text used for the hyperlink is automatically updated when the element name changes.
    - Automatically checked - the hyperlink to an element is automatically checked in the related project using modeling tool validation.
    - Manually checked - the hyperlink to an element is manually checked in the related project using modeling tool validation.
    - Do not update - the hyperlink text stays the same regardless of any model changes.
  3. In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the model element to which you want to add a hyperlink. [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperlink_to_model_element.png']
6. Click the Add button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.
7. On the top left corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='exit_full_screen.png'] to exit the full-screen mode.
8. On the top right corner of the Edit pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

##### Adding hyperlinks to document sections

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a convenient way to incorporate references to any section of a Cameo Collaborator document.

To add a hyperlink to a document section or an element inside a section

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
2. Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. In the Edit pane on the right side of the screen, click the Full screen icon.
4. In the toolbar of an open screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperilnk_icon.png'] .
5. In the open dialog, go to the Document section tab and specify the following information:
  1. In the **Text to display** box, optionally type the text to be used for the hyperlink. If you skip this step, the section name is used instead.
  2. In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the document section you want to add a hyperlink to. [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperlink_to_section.png']
6. Click the Add button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.
7. On the top left corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='exit_full_screen.png'] to exit the full-screen mode.
8. On the top right corner of the Edit pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

##### Adding hyperlinks to presentation elements

In addition to being able to cross-reference document sections, you can include hyperlinks to presentation elements within a section, like figures and tables.

To add a hyperlink to a presentation element inside a document section

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
2. Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. In the Edit pane on the right side of the screen, click the Full screen icon.
4. In the toolbar of an open screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperilnk_icon.png'] .
5. In the open dialog, go to the Document section tab and specify the following information:
  1. In the **Text to display** box, optionally type the text to be used for the hyperlink. If you skip this step, the presentation element name will be used instead.
  2. In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the document section containing the presentation element you want to add a hyperlink to.
  3. Click the Next button.
  4. In the second step of the wizard, select the presentation element you want to add a hyperlink to. [ATTACHMENT filename='hyperlink_to_presentation_element.png']
6. Click the Add button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.
7. On the top left corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='exit_full_screen.png'] to exit the full-screen mode.
8. On the top right corner of the Edit pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70bbf500-1177-401e-9447-e8302a94fd9f"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Collaborator for Magic Collaboration Studio supports hyperlinks to web pages, model elements, and document sections in all string-type values. You can also add cross-references to any part of a document section, including images and tables.</span></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />Adding hyperlinks to web pages</span></h3><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To add a hyperlink to a web page</span></p><hr style="" /><ol><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Edit</strong> pane on the right side of the screen, click the <strong>Full screen</strong> icon.</li><li>In the toolbar of an open screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperilnk_icon.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Adress</strong> tab of an open dialog, specify the following information:<ol><li>In the <strong>URL</strong> box, enter the web page address to which you want to add a hyperlink.</li><li>In the <strong>Text to display</strong> box, optionally type the text you want to use for the hyperlink.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="adding_hyperlink.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.</li><li>On the top left corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exit_full_screen.png" /></ac:image> to exit the full-screen mode.</li><li>On the top right corner of the <strong>Edit</strong> pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />Adding hyperlinks to model elements</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In a Cameo Collaborator document, you can hyperlinks to model elements for easier navigation in the document. Hyperlinks can be represented as element names, representation text, or any custom text. In addition, you have the ability to choose whether hyperlinks are updated when element names change.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To add a hyperlink to a model element</span></p><hr style="" /><ol><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Edit</strong> pane on the right side of the screen, click the <strong>Full screen</strong> icon.</li><li>In the toolbar of an open screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperilnk_icon.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the open dialog, go to the <strong>Model element</strong> tab and specify the following information:<ol><li>In <strong>Text to display</strong> drop-down box, select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Name</strong> - the hyperlink is displayed as the element name.</li><li><strong>Representation Text</strong> - the hyperlink is displayed as the element representation text.</li><li><strong>Icon + Representation Text</strong> - the hyperlink is displayed as the element representation text with the element icon.</li><li><p><strong>Custom Text</strong> - the hyperlink is displayed as your custom text. Type the hyperlink text in the additional <strong>Text to display</strong> box, which appears after selecting the <strong>Custom Text</strong> option.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a70d48df-cc52-4cf5-a71e-0d739d4d6773"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you use custom text for the hyperlink, it cannot be updated when the element name changes. Therefore, the only available update mode (see the next step) is <strong>Do not update</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Update mode</strong> drop-down box, select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Automatically updated</strong> - the element name or representation text used for the hyperlink is automatically updated when the element name changes.</li><li><strong>Automatically checked</strong> - the hyperlink to an element is automatically checked in the related project using modeling tool validation.</li><li><strong>Manually checked</strong> - the hyperlink to an element is manually checked in the related project using modeling tool validation.</li><li><strong>Do not update</strong> - the hyperlink text stays the same regardless of any model changes.</li></ul></li><li>In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the model element to which you want to add a hyperlink.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_model_element.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.</li><li>On the top left corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exit_full_screen.png" /></ac:image> to exit the full-screen mode.</li><li>On the top right corner of the <strong>Edit</strong> pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />Adding hyperlinks to document sections</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352C63);">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a convenient way to incorporate references to any section of a Cameo Collaborator document.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352C63);">To add a hyperlink to a document section or an element inside a section</span></span></p><hr style="" /><ol><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Edit</strong> pane on the right side of the screen, click the <strong>Full screen</strong> icon.</li><li>In the toolbar of an open screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperilnk_icon.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the open dialog, go to the <strong>Document section</strong> tab and specify the following information:<ol><li><p>In the <strong>Text to display</strong> box, optionally type the text to be used for the hyperlink. If you skip this step, the section name is used instead.</p></li><li>In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the document section you want to add a hyperlink to.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_section.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.</li><li>On the top left corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exit_full_screen.png" /></ac:image> to exit the full-screen mode.</li><li>On the top right corner of the <strong>Edit</strong> pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Adding hyperlinks to presentation elements</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352C63);">In addition to being able to cross-reference document sections, you can include hyperlinks to presentation elements within a section, like figures and tables.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352C63);">To add a hyperlink to a presentation element inside a document section</span></span></p><hr style="" /><ol><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Select the string-type value or paragraph where you want to add a hyperlink. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.</p></li><li>In the <strong>Edit</strong> pane on the right side of the screen, click the <strong>Full screen</strong> icon.</li><li>In the toolbar of an open screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperilnk_icon.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the open dialog, go to the <strong>Document section</strong> tab and specify the following information:<ol><li><p>In the <strong>Text to display</strong> box, optionally type the text to be used for the hyperlink. If you skip this step, the presentation element name will be used instead.</p></li><li>In the document navigation tree at the bottom of the dialog, select the document section containing the presentation element you want to add a hyperlink to.</li><li>Click the <strong>Next</strong> button.</li><li>In the second step of the wizard, select the presentation element you want to add a hyperlink to.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="hyperlink_to_presentation_element.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol></li><li>Click the <strong>Add</strong> button. The hyperlink will be displayed on the screen.</li><li>On the top left corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exit_full_screen.png" /></ac:image> to exit the full-screen mode.</li><li>On the top right corner of the <strong>Edit</strong> pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992619 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00006: Adding images

- page_id: `137992619`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992619/Adding+images
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:52:30.654+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

If you publish a Cameo Collaborator document for editing, you can decorate it by adding images. Images are added as new document paragraphs, allowing you to update them or change their location anytime.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To add images to a Cameo Collaborator document:

- The document must be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing a model for editing' space=''] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

To add an image to a Cameo Collaborator document

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the **Add image** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to add an image. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Add image pane, do the following:
  1. Optionally enter the image title in the Image title field. If you skip this step, the image file name will be used as the image title.
  2. If needed, change the image number in the Image number field.
  3. Drag the image file to the box at the bottom of the Add image pane, or click the box and select the image file from your computer. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragging_image.png']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the Add image pane.

##### Changing image location

You can change the location of an image added to a Cameo Collaborator document by cutting and pasting it in a new location.

To change an image location

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the Cut icon under the image whose location you want to change. [ATTACHMENT filename='cutting_image.png']
4. Click the **Paste** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to move the image. TipIf you want to move the image from one document section to another, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space=''] to open a different section of the same document view and then paste the image. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991805 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00007: Adding roles

- page_id: `137991805`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991805/Adding+roles
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:50:58.834+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Managing users > Managing user roles and assignments
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a role to a user or users group

1. On the Users application , click the user you want to assign a role to. The User pane will open.
2. Go to the Roles card and click Change button.
3. In the open screen, select the Add role tab and start typing a role name in the search box.
4. Select the role you want to add from the list.
5. When the role appears in the **Assigned roles** list, optionally set a custom scope for the role. Otherwise move to the next step. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a role to a user or users group</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>Users application</strong>, click the user you want to assign a role to. The <strong>User </strong>pane will open.</li><li>Go to the <strong>Roles</strong> card and click <strong>Change</strong> button.</li><li>In the open screen, select the <strong>Add role</strong> tab and start typing a role name in the search box.</li><li>Select the role you want to add from the list.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the role appears in the <strong>Assigned roles</strong> list, optionally set a custom scope for the role. Otherwise move to the next step.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assigning_roles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Adding roles" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_roles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Adding roles" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to save the changes.</p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991819 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00008: Adding user to user groups

- page_id: `137991819`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991819/Adding+user+to+user+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:50:59.277+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Managing users
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Multiple selections are activated when you click these icons. Multiple selections allow you to select several users and add them to a group or copy a role assignment from another user. When using multiple selections in a groups section, you can select more than one group and delete them instantly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Select more than one user to add to the group.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Multiple selections <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="99c3c607-8969-48b1-bcfb-c94a81764be2">are activated when you click these icons. Multiple selections allow you to select several users and add them to a group or copy a role assignment from another user. When using multiple selections in a groups section, you can select more than on</ac:inline-comment-marker>e group and delete them instantly.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Group_users.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Adding user to user groups" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Select more than one user to add to the group.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191939524 space=MCS2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00009: Admin Tools

- page_id: `191939524`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/191939524/Admin+Tools
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2024-11-29T10:11:11.296+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > FAQ and Troubleshooting
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**4**

The Admin Tools package is a set of Linux utility scripts that facilitate server-side tasks. You can use these scripts to manage services (start, stop, check status), archive log and configuration files, and check the installation environment. Furthermore, you can generate shortcuts to log and configuration files in a central location for troubleshooting and monitoring configuration changes.

#### INFO: Where can I find the Admin Tools package?

Where can I find the Admin Tools package?

The Admin Tools package is included with 2024x and newer releases.

You can also download *[ATTACHMENT filename='utilities_2024xHF3.zip']* and use it on older releases (19.0 to 2022x).

###### Creating shortcuts

The **add-twc-shortcuts.sh** script creates symbolic links to key log and configuration files so everything can be accessed in one location. For older versions of Teamwork Cloud, the *Utilities/AdminTools*path is created in the installation path and all utilities are copied over as well. A system path entry is set optionally in /etc/profile.d/twc.sh (applied at the next login session). Elevated permission is required to execute this script.

**Shortcut files mapping for add-twc-shortcuts.sh**

| Shortcut name | Original filename |
| --- | --- |
| conf-auth | authserver.properties |
| conf-twc | application.conf |
| conf-webapp | webappplatform.properties |
| log-auth | authentication.log |
| log-cassandra | system.log |
| log-tomcat | catalina.out |
| log-twc | server.log |
| log-webapp | webapp.log or web-app.log |
| log-webapp-admin | admin.log |

###### Managing services

You can use this set of utility scripts to manage all Teamwork Cloud services. Elevated permission is required to execute these scripts.

Note that if add-twc-shortcuts.sh was executed and system path is set, these scripts can be executed anywhere.

| Script name | Details |
| --- | --- |
| twcstats.sh[network] | Check the status of all services. If service is running, output will display: ACTIVE.When run as root/sudo, service log will be generated for each failed or stopped service.If Cassandra is installed and running on the same server, nodetool status will be used to report on Cassandra mode.Add "network" option to check port bindings of each service. |
| twc-start.sh[db] | Start all Teamwork Cloud services that are installed on the system.Services will only be started if they are installed and not active.Add “db” option to start Cassandra service (only performed if Cassandra is installed on same server). |
| twc-stop.sh[db] | Stop all Teamwork Cloud services that are installed on the system.Only services that are currently running will be stopped.Add “db” option to stop Cassandra service as well. A nodetool drain will be performed first before service is stopped (only performed if Cassandra is installed on same server). |
| cassandra-stop.sh | Perform proper nodetool drain before stopping the Cassandra service. This script is called by twc-stop.sh. |

###### Archiving configuration files

Use the **backup-twc-configs.sh** script to create a backup package of the current *certificate/keystore* and all Teamwork Cloud configuration files. The default backup path is */opt/local/TWC_Backup*. Elevated permission may be required to access files and archive path.

###### Comparing configuration files

Use **compare-twc-config.sh** to compare the current configuration against a reference set of configurations. Reference set can be either be from a backup or the no-install package.

###### Checking installation environment

The **check-env.sh** script is a tool for checking the installation environment on a server. This script does not require elevated permission to execute. See the table below for a list of checks performed.

| check-env.sh |
| --- |
| TWC version installed |
| Java version(s) installed |
| OS and version |
| Execution permissions on /tmp & /home |
| SELinux setting |
| fapolicyd service status |
| FIPS mode setting |
| Cassandra version installed |
| Cassandra /data & /logs mount types |

###### Trimming webapps

Use the**webapp-trim.sh** script to control the number of webapps to run on the server. Run with the minimum set of webapps for troubleshooting. Optimize system resources by running only the webapps that users need.

| Command | Option | Details |
| --- | --- | --- |
| webapp-trim.sh |  | Only run webapps in the $RUN_LIST specified inside the script file. |
| min | For troubleshooting and fast startup, set to minimum webapps needed. |  |
| restore | Restore server to run all the webapps. |  |
| help | Display list of options for script. |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="745b40f6-78a0-4fe6-99ca-a4505a90e7be"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Admin Tools package is a set of Linux utility scripts that facilitate server-side tasks. You can use these scripts to manage services (start, stop, check status), archive log and configuration files, and check the installation environment. Furthermore, you can generate shortcuts to log and configuration files in a central location for troubleshooting and monitoring configuration changes.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a66fa6c3-166e-4982-881d-decff7bb53a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Where can I find the Admin Tools package?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Admin Tools package is included with 2024x and newer releases.</p><p>You can also download <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="utilities_2024xHF3.zip" /></ac:link></em> and use it on older releases (19.0 to 2022x).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="font-weight: 600;letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Creating shortcuts</span></h4><p>The <strong>add-twc-shortcuts.sh</strong> script creates symbolic links to key log and configuration files so everything can be accessed in one location. For older versions of Teamwork Cloud, the <em>Utilities/AdminTools </em>path is created in the installation path and all utilities are copied over as well. A system path entry is set optionally in /etc/profile.d/twc.sh (applied at the next login session). Elevated permission is required to execute this script.</p><p><strong>Shortcut files mapping for add-twc-shortcuts.sh</strong></p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Shortcut name</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Original filename</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>conf-auth</p></td><td><p>authserver.properties</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>conf-twc</p></td><td><p>application.conf</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>conf-webapp</p></td><td><p>webappplatform.properties</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-auth</p></td><td><p>authentication.log</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-cassandra</p></td><td><p>system.log</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-tomcat</p></td><td><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="076746b1-7ee9-460a-8e9f-5626af8ed573">catalina.out</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-twc</p></td><td><p>server.log</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-webapp</p></td><td><p>webapp.log or web-app.log</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>log-webapp-admin</p></td><td><p>admin.log</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color: rgb(23,43,77);">Managing services</h4><p>You can use this set of utility scripts to manage all Teamwork Cloud services. Elevated permission is required to execute these scripts.</p><p>Note that if add-twc-shortcuts.sh was executed and system path is set, these scripts can be executed anywhere.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 921.0px;"><colgroup><col style="width: 145.0px;" /><col style="width: 775.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>Script name</strong></p></th><th><p><strong>Details</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>twcstats.sh<br />[network]</p></td><td><p>Check the status of all services. If service is running, output will display: ACTIVE.</p><p>When run as root/sudo, service log will be generated for each failed or stopped service.</p><p>If Cassandra is installed and running on the same server, nodetool status will be used to report on Cassandra mode.</p><p>Add &quot;network&quot; option to check port bindings of each service.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>twc-start.sh<br />[db]</p></td><td><p>Start all Teamwork Cloud services that are installed on the system.</p><p>Services will only be started if they are installed and not active.</p><p>Add “db” option to start Cassandra service (only performed if Cassandra is installed on same server).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>twc-stop.sh<br />[db]</p></td><td><p>Stop all Teamwork Cloud services that are installed on the system.</p><p>Only services that are currently running will be stopped.</p><p>Add “db” option to stop Cassandra service as well. A nodetool drain will be performed first before service is stopped (only performed if Cassandra is installed on same server).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>cassandra-stop.sh</p></td><td><p>Perform proper nodetool drain before stopping the Cassandra service. This script is called by twc-stop.sh.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color: rgb(23,43,77);">Archiving configuration files</h4><p>Use the <strong>backup-twc-configs.sh</strong> script to create a backup package of the current <em>certificate/keystore</em> and all Teamwork Cloud configuration files. The default backup path is <em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">/opt/local</span>/TWC_Backup</em>. Elevated permission may be required to access files and archive path.</p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color: rgb(23,43,77);">Comparing configuration files</h4><p>Use <strong>compare-twc-config.sh</strong> to compare the current configuration against a reference set of configurations. Reference set can be either be from a backup or the no-install package.</p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color: rgb(23,43,77);">Checking installation environment</h4><p>The <strong>check-env.sh</strong> script is a tool for checking the installation environment on a server. This script does not require elevated permission to execute. See the table below for a list of checks performed.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><p><strong>check-env.sh</strong></p></th></tr><tr><td><p>TWC version installed</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Java version(s) installed</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>OS and version</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Execution permissions on /tmp &amp; /home</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SELinux setting</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>fapolicyd service status</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FIPS mode setting</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cassandra version installed</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cassandra /data &amp; /logs mount types</p></td></tr></tbody></table><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color: rgb(23,43,77);">Trimming webapps</h4><p>Use the<strong> webapp-trim.sh</strong> script to control the number of webapps to run on the server. Run with the minimum set of webapps for troubleshooting. Optimize system resources by running only the webapps that users need.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 717.0px;"><colgroup><col style="width: 127.0px;" /><col style="width: 113.0px;" /><col style="width: 476.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Command</th><th scope="col">Option</th><th scope="col">Details</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4">webapp-trim.sh</td><td><br /></td><td>Only run webapps in the $RUN_LIST specified inside the script file.</td></tr><tr><td>min</td><td>For troubleshooting and fast startup, set to minimum webapps needed.</td></tr><tr><td>restore</td><td>Restore server to run all the webapps.</td></tr><tr><td>help</td><td>Display list of options for script.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991641 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00010: Admin web applications

- page_id: `137991641`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991641/Admin+web+applications
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:02:28.136+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Designed for simplicity, Teamwork Cloud Admin is a web interface that allows you to access web applications and work effectively with Magic Collaboration Studio. You can visually[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Users application' space=''],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Roles application' space=''], and[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assigning users to resources' space=''] via the easy-to-use web browser interface.

The following sections describe system security and access control options to allow or restrict access to resources on the Magic Collaboration Studio system. You can also find step-by-step instructions on using Teamwork Cloud Admin to work with projects and manage users who access Teamwork Cloud Admin to accomplish tasks assigned to them.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The welcome screen of the Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Y<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">ou can visually</span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Users application" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ manage users]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">,</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Roles application" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[roles]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, and</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assigning users to resources" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[resource assignments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> via t<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">he easy-to-use web browser interface.</span></p><p>The following sections describe system security and access control options to allow or restrict access to resources on the Magic Collaboration Studio system. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991506 space=MCS2024x version=10 -->
## PAGE 00012: Advanced Magic Collaboration Studio configuration

- page_id: `137991506`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991506/Advanced+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+configuration
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:41:53.802+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Configuration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

The configuration settings described in this section are optional. You only need to configure them if the default setting is not suitable for your environment. The Magic Collaboration Studio system will load the configuration file every time it starts. Therefore, changes to the configuration file will be applied once you restart it.

To configure Cassandra authentication, please see chapter [Cassandra authentication](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024x/Hardening+Teamwork+Cloud#HardeningTeamworkCloud-CassandraAuthentication).

##### Setting a Magic Collaboration Studio password for authentication with the Authentication Server

Teamwork Cloud Admin uses an Authentication Server to authenticate users. Teamwork Cloud Admin needs a password to access /token endpoint of the Authentication Server. You need to specify the same password that is entered in the authentication server's configuration file **webappplatform.properties**(in the parameter **authentication.client.secret**). The default value is **CHANGE_ME**.

```text

```

##### **Changing a log file location**

You can configure the logging system of Magic Collaboration Studio by using **logback.xml**. This file is located in the folder *configuration* of the Magic Collaboration Studio. You can change the name or location of the file by editing the following line in the file **logback.xml.**

- In Magic Collaboration Studio, look for the following line: logback.xml${user.home}/.twcloud/2021x/server.log]]>

When changing the log file name and location, you need to be sure that the user account to your Operating System (OS) that you will use to start Magic Collaboration Studio has the write permission in the new configured location. To learn more about logback configuration, see [http://logback.qos.ch/manual/configuration.html](http://logback.qos.ch/manual/configuration.html).

##### **Setting a server port**

The default port number of Magic Collaboration Studio for the client to connect to is **3579**. However, the port number is changeable. If you would like to change the default port number, for example, to 3580, you can configure this in the Magic Collaboration Studio configuration file, **application.conf***.* Search for the content shown below, and edit as follows:

```text

```

The configuration file, **application.conf**, is located in the directory *configuration* under the Magic Collaboration Studio program folder. For example, */opt/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf.*The configuration file is in HOCON format ("Human-Optimized Config Object Notation"). For more information about HOCON file format, visit the following link [https://github.com/typesafehub/config/blob/master/HOCON.md#syntax](https://github.com/typesafehub/config/blob/master/HOCON.md#syntax).

Please note that MagicDraw is considered as a client of Magic Collaboration Studio. Consequently, if you change this setting, you have to specify the port number while connecting from MagicDraw.

##### **Setting a public IP**

If Magic Collaboration Studio is deployed in the environment where the client will connect to the server via a public IP (NATed to a private IP), set the parameter **server-broadcast-host** to the public IP address and **host** to the private IP. Search for the content in **application.conf** and edit as follows:

```text

```

##### Configuring secure SSL communication between Magic Collaboration Studio and Cassandra

You can set up secure communication between Magic Collaboration Studio and Cassandra. For this, you need to configure **application.conf** (Magic Collaboration Studio side) and **cassandra.yaml** (Cassandra side) files.

**Configuring application.conf**

1. Locate and open the application.conf file.
2. Search for the following section: /configuration/keystore.p12"
 keystoreType = ""
 keystorePassword = ""
 truststorePath = "/configuration/keystore.p12"
 truststoreType = ""
 truststorePassword = ""
 }
}]]>
3. Configure the properties, as shown in the example above:
  - enabled = true
  - keystorePath = <install_root>/configuration /keystore.p12
  - keystoreType = <keystore_type>
  - keystorePassword = <keystore_password>
  - truststorePath = <install_root>/configuration /keystore.p12
  - truststoreType = <truststore_type>
  - truststorePassword = <truststore_password>
4. Save the changes.

**Configuring cassandra.yaml**

- Locate and open the **cassandra.yaml** file.
- Configure the following properties: 
**client_encryption_options**:
  - enabled: true
  - optional: *false* If*enabled* and *optional* are set to true, encrypted and unencrypted connections are handled.
  - keystore : <path_to_keystore>/keystore.p12
  - keystore_password : <keystore_password>
  - require_client_auth : false
- If r equire_client_auth is set to true , set truststore and truststore_password :
  - truststore : <path_to_truststore>/keystore.p12
  - truststore_password : <truststore_password> The **require_client_auth** setting is optional – it adds an extra layer of security by requiring the client to present its own certificate to the server (mutual TLS). However, this setting does not affect whether encryption is used for communication: if **require_client_auth**is set to *false*, the server does not require the client to present a certificate, but the communication is still encrypted as long as SSL/TLS is enabled.
- Use these advanced defaults:
  - protocol : TLS
  - algorithm : SunX509
  - store_type : PKCS12

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>On this page</strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bdba2b96-5078-4fb8-9902-50970b9f88a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The configuration settings described in this section are optional. You only need to configure them if the default setting is not suitable for your environment. The Magic Collaboration Studio system will load the configuration file every time it starts. Therefore, changes to the configuration file will be applied once you restart it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9b2b143-f82f-4f1d-a043-74b5ef04ca4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To configure Cassandra authentication, please see chapter <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024x/Hardening+Teamwork+Cloud#HardeningTeamworkCloud-CassandraAuthentication">Cassandra authentication</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting a Magic Collaboration Studio password for authentication with the Authentication Server</h3><p>Teamwork Cloud Admin uses an Authentication Server to authenticate users. Teamwork Cloud Admin needs a password to access /token endpoint of the Authentication Server. You need to specify the same password that is entered in the authentication server's configuration file <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">webappplatform.properties</span> </strong>(in the parameter <strong>authentication.client.secret</strong>). The default value is <strong>CHANGE_ME</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d794c9e-fe0f-41d9-bfb2-d50e457ca346"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">authserver.properties</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.console {
....
#
 # The console client password parameter.
 client {
 pswd = "CHANGE_ME"
 }
...
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="Standard"><strong>Changing a log file location</strong></h3><p class="Standard">You can configure the logging system of Magic Collaboration Studio by using <strong>logback.xml</strong>. This file is located in the folder <em>configuration</em> of the Magic Collaboration Studio. You can change the name or location of the file by editing the following line in the file <strong>logback.xml.</strong></p><ul><li class="Standard"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In Magic Collaboration Studio, look for the following line: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="348d22ac-dcbf-4571-917d-1c15e3024323"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">logback.xml</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<file>${user.home}/.twcloud/2021x/server.log</file>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p class="Standard">When changing the log file name and location, you need to be sure that the user account to your Operating System (OS) that you will use to start Magic Collaboration Studio has the write permission in the new configured location. To learn more about logback configuration, see <a href="http://logback.qos.ch/manual/configuration.html">http://logback.qos.ch/manual/configuration.html</a>.</p><h3 class="Standard"><strong>Setting a server port</strong></h3><p class="Standard">The default port number of Magic Collaboration Studio for the client to connect to is <strong>3579</strong>. However, the port number is changeable. If you would like to change the default port number, for example, to 3580, you can configure this in the Magic Collaboration Studio configuration file, <strong>application.conf</strong><em>. </em> Search for the content shown below, and edit as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60d6afce-0f48-4698-92e2-f938c6735f91"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">application.conf</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[net
	{
		connectors = [
			{
				# The ip address to bind to (for client connection).
				# The default value is bind to all IPs.
				host = "0.0.0.0"
				
				# Port for client application to connect to. The default value is 3579.
				# This is corresponding to the port specified when connecting from client such as MagicDraw. 
				port = 3580]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="Standard">The configuration file, <strong>application.conf</strong>, is located in the directory <em>configuration</em> under the Magic Collaboration Studio program folder. For example, <em>/opt/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf. </em>The configuration file is in HOCON format (&quot;Human-Optimized Config Object Notation&quot;). For more information about HOCON file format, visit the following link <a style="text-decoration: underline;" href="https://github.com/typesafehub/config/blob/master/HOCON.md#syntax">https://github.com/typesafehub/config/blob/master/HOCON.md#syntax</a>.</p><p class="Standard">Please note that MagicDraw is considered as a client of Magic Collaboration Studio. Consequently, if you change this setting, you have to specify the port number while connecting from MagicDraw.</p><h3><strong>Setting a public IP</strong></h3><p>If Magic Collaboration Studio is deployed in the environment where the client will connect to the server via a public IP (NATed to a private IP), set the parameter <strong>server-broadcast-host</strong> to the public IP address and <strong>host</strong> to the private IP. Search for the content in <strong>application.conf</strong> and edit as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="651c7dc5-daf9-4c7d-aad4-53e077a72453"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[net
	{
		connectors = [
			{
				# The ip address to bind to (for client connection).
				# The default value is bind to all IPs.
				host = "0.0.0.0"
				
				# Port for client application to connect to. The default value is 3579.
				# This is corresponding to the port specified when connecting from client such as MagicDraw. 
				port = 3579
				
				protocol = "raw"
			}
		]
		
		# To support deploying Magic Collaboration Studio in private network, but client connects from public network,
		# Specify IP address that will be used by client to connect to this Magic Collaboration Studio from public network.
		# This value will be default to esi.net.host if it is empty.
		# server-broadcast-host is not recommended to use.
		server-broadcast-host = "PUBLIC_IP_ADDRESS"
	}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Configuring secure SSL communication between Magic Collaboration Studio and Cassandra</h3><p>You can set up secure communication between Magic Collaboration Studio and Cassandra. For this, you need to configure <strong>application.conf</strong> (Magic Collaboration Studio side) and <strong>cassandra.yaml</strong> (Cassandra side) files. </p><p><strong>Configuring application.conf</strong></p><ol><li>Locate and open the <strong>application.conf</strong> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Search for the following section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c0d712b-9728-492f-bf52-d223edbdda83"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Secure connection with SSL between Cassandra and Magic Collaboration Studio
esi.security {
    cassandra {
        enabled = true
    	keystorePath = "<install_root>/configuration/keystore.p12"
    	keystoreType = "<keystore_type>"
    	keystorePassword = "<keystore_password>"
    	truststorePath = "<install_root>/configuration/keystore.p12"
        truststoreType = "<truststore_type>"
        truststorePassword = "<truststore_password>"
    }
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Configure the properties, as shown in the example above:</p><ul><li><strong>enabled</strong> = <em>true</em></li><li><strong>keystorePath</strong> = <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;/configuration</em><em>/keystore.p12</em></li><li><strong>keystoreType</strong> = <em>&lt;keystore_type&gt;</em></li><li><strong>keystorePassword</strong> = <em>&lt;keystore_password&gt;</em></li><li><strong>truststorePath</strong> = <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;install_root&gt;/configuration</em><em>/keystore.p12</em></li><li><strong>truststoreType</strong> = <em>&lt;truststore_type&gt;</em></li><li><strong>truststorePassword</strong> = <em>&lt;truststore_password&gt;</em></li></ul></li><li>Save the changes. <br /><br /></li></ol><p><strong>Configuring cassandra.yaml<br /></strong></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Locate and open the <strong>cassandra.yaml</strong> file.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Configure the following properties:<br /><strong>client_encryption_options</strong>: </p><ul><li>enabled: <em>true</em></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">optional: <em>false</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ae01f62-5e93-4391-b9cb-ad67953781e2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If<em> enabled</em> and <em>optional</em> are set to true, encrypted and unencrypted connections are handled.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><ul><li><strong>keystore</strong>:  <em>&lt;path_to_keystore&gt;/keystore.p12</em></li><li><strong>keystore_password</strong>: <em>&lt;keystore_password&gt;</em></li><li><strong>require_client_auth</strong>: <em>false</em></li></ul></li><li>If <strong>r</strong><strong>equire_client_auth </strong>is set to <em>true</em><span>, set </span><em>truststore</em><span> and </span><strong>truststore_password</strong><span>:</span><ul><li><strong>truststore</strong>: <em>&lt;path_to_truststore&gt;/keystore.p12</em></li><li><strong>truststore_password</strong>: <em>&lt;truststore_password&gt;<br /></em><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2fe76e1-3632-414e-b4c1-c102a830a739"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>require_client_auth</strong> setting is optional – it adds an extra layer of security by requiring the client to present its own certificate to the server (mutual TLS). However, this setting does not affect whether encryption is used for communication: if <strong>require_client_auth </strong>is set to <em>false</em>, the server does not require the client to present a certificate, but the communication is still encrypted as long as SSL/TLS is enabled.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>Use these advanced defaults:<br /><ul><li><strong>protocol</strong>: <em>TLS</em></li><li><strong>algorithm</strong>: <em>SunX509</em></li><li><strong>store_type</strong>: <em>PKCS12</em></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
