<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991507 space=MCS2024x version=5 -->
## PAGE 00014: Advanced services configuration

- page_id: `137991507`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991507/Advanced+services+configuration
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:42:02.476+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Configuration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

In this section, we will review the various settings which you may have to adjust in order to establish communication between web services and Magic Collaboration Studio.

Change these settings only if you need to change the default installation.

##### Increasing open file limit on Linux

Use this method to increase the maximum number of files that the entire operating system can handle at once before installing Web Application Platform with services.

To increase the open file limit for the operating system

1. Open the /etc/sysctl.conf file with a text editor as a root user: text
2. Add or modify the following line with the new limit: text
3. Save and exit the editor.
4. Apply the changes immediately by running: text
5. To verify the change, run: text

##### Changing properties

To change Authentication server properties

1. Open the webappplatform.properties file at CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.
2. Locate Authentication server properties and change the values as necessary :

```text

```

#### NOTE: Authserver access

Authserver access

If you are accessing via a hostname or FQDN, especially if you are using a signed certificate, use the applicable FQDN or hostname instead of the IP address.

If you have configured authserver to use HTTP or to run on a different port, make sure that the URI reflects the correct values.

To change Magic Collaboration Studio server properties

1. Open the webappplatform.properties file at CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.
2. Locate Magic Collaboration Studio server properties and change the values as necessary :

```text

```

#### NOTE: Magic Collaboration Studio access

Magic Collaboration Studio access

Please ensure these credentials for **twc.admin.username** and **twc.admin.password** match those of a user with administrative privileges.

If you are accessing via a hostname or FQDN, especially if you are using a signed certificate, use the applicable FQDN or hostname instead of the IP address.

If you have configured Magic Collaboration Studio to use HTTP or to run on a different port, make sure that the URI reflects the correct values.

If you change any of the configuration parameters, you will need to restart the WebApp service.

##### Setting server protocol

By default, in order to enforce a higher level of security, web services are accessed via HTTPS. In order to change the mode of operation to HTTP, various configuration changes must be made.

We do not recommend changing HTTPS to HTTP. Perform this only if necessary.

The default port for web services is *8443*. In this example, we will make the changes necessary to run over HTTP on the default port of *8443*.

The WebApp server configuration is located in *CATIANoMagicServices**/WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml.*

The following section:

```text
]]>
```

needs to be edited to:

```text
]]>
```

The changes which we implemented consist of changing the port from *8080* to *8443*, and removing a redirect that would route to the handler on port *8443*.

Since we have configured this connector to listen on port *8443*, we now need to remove the existing connector handler on port *8443*.

The following section:

```text
]]>
```

needs to be commented out as follows:

```text
-->]]>
```

By default, due to security reasons, we have established a security policy requiring access to be encrypted. To disable this, we need to edit *CATIANoMagicServices**/WebAppPlatform/conf/web.xml*. This section is located at the very bottom of the file.

The following section:

```text
webapp
        /*                    
                                  
                              
        CONFIDENTIAL
    
 ]]>
```

needs to be edited as follows:

```text
webapp
        /*                    
                                  
                              
        NONE
    
 ]]>
```

##### Changing port

In the next example, we will configure web services to run HTTPS on a different port (8444).

The following code section:

```text
]]>
```

needs to be edited as follows:

```text
]]>
```

The only change is the definition of the port number, which changed from 8443 to 8444.

If you change the protocol or the port from the default, you need to edit **authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist****located at *CATIANoMagicServices**/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties*accordingly.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="586c01fa-b160-4e70-bcd0-964aebbaf7df"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cat /proc/sys/fs/file-max]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Changing properties</h3><p>To change Authentication server properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>webappplatform.properties </em>file at<em> CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.</em></li><li>Locate <strong>Authentication server properties </strong>and change the values as necessary<em>:</em></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74d5b551-c091-4951-a0ec-d4294e0021f7"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Authentication server properties
#

# Specify the Authentication server location.
# IP address or domain name.
authentication.server.ip=127.0.0.1

# Specify the Authentication server port.
authentication.server.port=8443

# Specify rest protocol (http or https) depending on the Authentication server setup.
authentication.server.protocol=https]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b4e5d58d-bb77-4201-8277-e4d99de866bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Authserver access</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you are accessing via a hostname or FQDN, especially if you are using a signed certificate, use the applicable FQDN or hostname instead of the IP address.</p><p>If you have configured authserver to use HTTP or to run on a different port, make sure that the URI reflects the correct values.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To change Magic Collaboration Studio server properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>webappplatform.properties </em>file at<em> CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties.</em></li><li>Locate <strong>Magic Collaboration Studio server properties </strong>and change the values as necessary<em>:</em></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffbf91db-7613-475f-b07d-fdd0efd03b28"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[# Specify the Magic Collaboration Studio IP address or domain name.
twc.ip=127.0.0.1

# Specify rest protocol (http or https) depending on the Magic Collaboration Studio server setup.
twc.rest.protocol=https

# Specify the Magic Collaboration Studio rest API port.
twc.rest.port=8111]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="653c4e44-7108-4e7b-8ac2-54e7ef68d859"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Magic Collaboration Studio access</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please ensure these credentials for <strong>twc.admin.username</strong> and <strong>twc.admin.password</strong> match those of a user with administrative privileges.</p><p>If you are accessing via a hostname or FQDN, especially if you are using a signed certificate, use the applicable FQDN or hostname instead of the IP address.</p><p>If you have configured Magic Collaboration Studio to use HTTP or to run on a different port, make sure that the URI reflects the correct values.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35302a0b-69a6-46e5-94fb-579dfaca4f3a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change any of the configuration parameters, you will need to restart the WebApp service.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Setting server protocol</h3><p>By default, in order to enforce a higher level of security, web services are accessed via HTTPS. In order to change the mode of operation to HTTP, various configuration changes must be made.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc2b0490-1d59-40e9-9dee-e7d41c1d02e7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We do not recommend changing HTTPS to HTTP. Perform this only if necessary.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The default port for web services is <em>8443</em>. In this example, we will make the changes necessary to run over HTTP on the default port of <em>8443</em>.</p><p>The WebApp server configuration is located in <em>CATIANoMagicServices</em><em>/WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml.</em></p><p>The following section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28c91279-05c0-42a6-ab5d-a6d78728715c"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    <Connector executor="tomcatThreadPool"
               port="8080" protocol="HTTP/1.1"
               connectionTimeout="20000"
               redirectPort="8443" />]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>needs to be edited to:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f523130-67c0-4aaf-b2e5-aca23ca8c2e0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    <Connector executor="tomcatThreadPool"
               port="8443" protocol="HTTP/1.1"
               connectionTimeout="20000" />]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The changes which we implemented consist of changing the port from <em>8080</em> to <em>8443</em>, and removing a redirect that would route to the handler on port <em>8443</em>.</p><p>Since we have configured this connector to listen on port <em>8443</em>, we now need to remove the existing connector handler on port <em>8443</em>.</p><p>The following section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed09dbd7-b87c-4895-9677-156dea500c10"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[    <Connector port="8443" protocol="org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol"
               sslImplementationName="org.apache.tomcat.util.net.jsse.JSSEImplementation"
               maxThreads="150" SSLEnabled="true">
      <SSLHostConfig>
        <Certificate certificateKeystoreFile="../configuration/keystore.p12"
                     certificateKeystorePassword="nomagic"
                     type="RSA" />
      </SSLHostConfig>
    </Connector>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>needs to be commented out as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a34a014-d363-4371-9985-8cee5872d2c2"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<!--   
 	<Connector port="8443" protocol="org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol"
               sslImplementationName="org.apache.tomcat.util.net.jsse.JSSEImplementation"
               maxThreads="150" SSLEnabled="true">
      <SSLHostConfig>
        <Certificate certificateKeystoreFile="../configuration/keystore.p12"
                     certificateKeystorePassword="nomagic"
                     type="RSA" />
      </SSLHostConfig>
    </Connector>
-->]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">By default, due to security reasons, we have established a security policy requiring access to be encrypted. To disable this, we need to edit <em>CATIANoMagicServices</em><em>/WebAppPlatform/conf/web.xml</em>. This section is located at the very bottom of the file.</p><p>The following section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d29b21a1-fb08-4784-bb4d-2f7b9aab23eb"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<security-constraint>                             
    <web-resource-collection>                      
        <web-resource-name>webapp</web-resource-name>
        <url-pattern>/*</url-pattern>                    
    </web-resource-collection>                              
    <user-data-constraint>                          
        <transport-guarantee>CONFIDENTIAL</transport-guarantee>
    </user-data-constraint>
</security-constraint> ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>needs to be edited as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c84fbf3b-4721-44fb-a151-68fdf12878fc"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<security-constraint>                             
    <web-resource-collection>                      
        <web-resource-name>webapp</web-resource-name>
        <url-pattern>/*</url-pattern>                    
    </web-resource-collection>                              
    <user-data-constraint>                          
        <transport-guarantee>NONE</transport-guarantee>
    </user-data-constraint>
</security-constraint> ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Changing port</h3><p>In the next example, we will configure web services to run HTTPS on a different port (8444).</p><p>The following code section:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="252c65e8-436b-43f6-b7dd-d2882bcf0262"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<Connector port="8443" protocol="org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol"
               sslImplementationName="org.apache.tomcat.util.net.jsse.JSSEImplementation"
               maxThreads="150" SSLEnabled="true">
      <SSLHostConfig>
        <Certificate certificateKeystoreFile="../configuration/keystore.p12"
                     certificateKeystorePassword="nomagic"
                     type="RSA" />
      </SSLHostConfig>
    </Connector>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>needs to be edited as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d5b9d11-24ba-406a-8260-c6266019c466"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<Connector port="8444" protocol="org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11NioProtocol"
               sslImplementationName="org.apache.tomcat.util.net.jsse.JSSEImplementation"
               maxThreads="150" SSLEnabled="true">
      <SSLHostConfig>
        <Certificate certificateKeystoreFile="../configuration/keystore.p12"
                     certificateKeystorePassword="nomagic"
                     type="RSA" />
      </SSLHostConfig>
    </Connector>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The only change is the definition of the port number, which changed from 8443 to 8444.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4fb84ef7-56a2-4b9e-8a35-203903049084"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change the protocol or the port from the default, you need to edit <strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</span><em> </em></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-yellow-bolder,#533f04);">located at <em>CATIANoMagicServices</em><em>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties </em>accordingly.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992409 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00015: App bar

- page_id: `137992409`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992409/App+bar
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:21:23.850+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The app bar is located on the top of a published model portal. You can perform the following actions from the app bar:

| App bar icon | Icon description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Click to show or hide the . |
|  | Click and select a Cameo Collaborator document view to open it. This icon appears next to the current document view name if the document has multiple views. |
|  | Click and type a search phrase in the document search box to that matches or includes the entered text (e.g., element names). |
|  | Click to show or hide the . |
|  | Click and select a web app to open it (e.g., Resources, Users, Roles, etc.). |
|  | Click and select Sign out to sign out of the current user account. |
|  | Click and select the action you want to perform:/- to switch from the Model view to the Document view mode or vice versa. - to enable or disable hyperlinks to a modeling tool for all paragraphs in the current document. - to enable or disable email notifications about new comments or comment replies in the current document. - to see detailed Cameo Collaborator document and project information.Help - to open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud documentation. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

style=""><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td style=""><p>Click and select the action you want to perform:</p><ul><li><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Document view modes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>/<strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Document view modes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>- to switch from the Model view to the Document view mode or vice versa.</li><li><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening items in a modeling tool"><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Show/hide paragraph links to model editor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to enable or disable hyperlinks to a modeling tool for all paragraphs in the current document.</li><li><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Subscribing to email notifications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Subscribe to/unsubscribe from email notifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to enable or disable email notifications about new comments or comment replies in the current document.</li><li><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Getting document information" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document information]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> - to see detailed Cameo Collaborator document and project information.</li><li><strong>Help</strong> - to open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud documentation.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991415 space=MCS2024x version=24 -->
## PAGE 00016: Applying Magic Collaboration Studio license

- page_id: `137991415`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991415/Applying+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+license
- version_number: 24
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:45:59.602+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > License management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

##### Using FlexNet license server

Upon successful installation, only the administrator account is available and required for you to apply a Magic Collaboration Studio license for the first time. The license selection page will also open if your license expires.

The license selection page prompts you to specify the address of the FlexNet license server installation where you store your license(s). If you do not specify any port, **1101** will be the default port. If you enter the wrong address, an error message will open for you to report the problem.

- You need to import a floating license key of Magic Collaboration Studio to the FlexNet License Server before you can activate it from Teamwork Cloud Admin.
- An administrator account is required only when applying a license for the first time. In subsequent license updates, any user who has the Server Administrator role can apply a Magic Collaboration Studio license.
- Magic Collaboration Studio will automatically apply the selected license whenever it starts. If the license does not exist in the license server anymore, the server will go into a limited mode, allowing only a user with the Server Administrator role to log in.
- After applying a license, Magic Collaboration Studio permanently consumes one user connection.

To apply Magic Collaboration Studio license using FlexNet license server

1. Open Web Application Platform in a web browser using this address format: https://<machine ip>:<port>/webapp .
2. Sign in to Magic Collaboration Studio using the administrator account credentials (the default credentials are Administrator/Administrator ).
3. Click the Settings application to open it.
4. From the left-side menu select the Server license page. There you will find the License information table.
5. In the FlexNet tab, enter the IP address of the FlexNet server (if it is on the same instance, use 127.0.0.1), and click SHOW LICENSES . If you want to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='FlexNet Three-Server Redundancy - Triad License Server' space='IL2024x'], follow this format:*server-address1:port,server-address2:port,serveraddress3:port*
6. If a valid license is found, it is applied automatically. If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click **Confirm.**
7. After applying the license, in the License information table, you will see the license that is used.

If your license server only has a single license edition for Magic Collaboration Studio, it will be automatically applied.

If your Magic Collaboration Studio is using a multi-node Cassandra cluster, you must have a Business Pro or Enterprise license. Magic Collaboration Studio will deactivate if it detects a multi-node cluster with either a Standard or Business license.

Whenever a Magic Collaboration Studio node connects to the FLEXnet License Server and retrieves (leases) a license, the number of available licenses will decrease. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the node will not be able to launch and you will need to reactivate once a license becomes available.

If your FLEXnet License Server is down, the server capacity shall be set to zero. All currently active users can continue working but they cannot sign in again once they log out as no user can sign into the server at that time. Auser with Configure Server permission (Server Administrator role)can sign into the Teamwork Cloud Admin to select a new license server.

You can see information about your current license on the **Server license** page of Teamwork Cloud Admin. The information includes the license edition and expiration date, the license version. See section [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Changing Magic Collaboration Studio license' space=''] to change or update your license.

##### Using DSLS

You need to have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DSLS installation and licensing' space='IL2024x'].

If you use DSLS, configure the Magic Collaboration Studio license in the *application.conf* file as described below.

To apply the Magic Collaboration Studio license using DSLS

1. Open for editing the <install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio/TeamworkCloud/configuration/ application.conf file.
2. Specify the following set of properties in the**core section of the file*:* OR add and specify the following set of properties at the end of the file: where:
  - 4085 is the default port.
  - The valid values of the mode property are:
    - CUSTOM - This means that the address provided in the **address** property is used to connect to the licensing server.
    - ORGANIZATION_DEFINED - In this case, you do not need to specify the address property since it is already defined in the DSLicSrv.txt file by the organization. The API will check out licenses from servers defined in that file. You can find the file in C:\ProgramData\DassaultSystemes\Licenses on Windows or /var/DassaultSystemes/Licenses on Linux and Mac.
  - The valid value of the edition property is the product trigram used to check out licenses in DSLS. To find the trigram of your product, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Product trigrams' space='IL2024x'] . MCS Academic licenseIf you are using an MCS Academic license, you should set the following:edition = "**MCSEDU**"the connection-packs property does not need to be specified.

If there are multiple licenses available, select the license to use on this server and click <strong>Confirm.</strong></span></li><li data-uuid="3c0ddcdc-a341-49e0-a54e-59b2be954c34">After applying the license, in the <strong>License information</strong> table, you will see the license that is used.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c888cdd-79a0-48f4-98e4-db8a9b50f9a0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your license server only has a single license edition for Magic Collaboration Studio, it will be automatically applied.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ca75d65-3b4d-4368-92a2-2cc1b43cb45a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your Magic Collaboration Studio is using a multi-node Cassandra cluster, you must have a Business Pro or Enterprise license. Magic Collaboration Studio will deactivate if it detects a multi-node cluster with either a Standard or Business license.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">Whenever a Magic Collaboration Studio node connects to the FLEXnet License Server and retrieves (leases) a license, the number of available licenses will decrease. If all available licenses from the server are leased, the node will not be able to launch and you will need to reactivate once a license becomes available.</p><p>If your FLEXnet License Server is down, the server capacity shall be set to zero. All currently active users can continue working but they cannot sign in again once they log out as no user can sign into the server at that time. A<span> user with Configure Server permission (Server Administrator role) </span>can sign into the Teamwork Cloud Admin to select a new license server.</p><p>You can see information about your current license on the <strong>Server license</strong> page of Teamwork Cloud Admin. The information includes the license edition and expiration date, the license version. See section <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Changing Magic Collaboration Studio license" /></ac:link> to change or update your license. </p><p><br /></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target" style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Using DSLS</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="260f0db4-ea4e-4983-abf5-ddcdd091d98c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to have <span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024x" ri:content-title="DSLS installation and licensing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[DSLS (Dassault Systèmes License Server)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>If you use DSLS, configure the Magic Collaboration Studio license in the <em>application.conf</em> file as described below.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To apply the Magic Collaboration Studio license using DSLS</p><hr /><ol><li>Open for editing the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/MagicCollaborationStudio/TeamworkCloud/configuration/</em><em>application.conf</em> file.</li><li><p>Specify the following set of properties in the<em> </em>core section of the file<em>:</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e1d6735-7d61-488e-88b8-cc1043b66020"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[dsls {
	enabled = true
	address = "license-server-address:4085"
	mode = "CUSTOM"
	edition = "MC5"		// possible values: "MCS", "MC5", "MCSEDU"
    connection-packs = 2
		}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>OR add and specify the following set of properties at the end of the file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ad4965e-67a9-4717-a62a-dd4ff5fb39f6"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.server.core.dsls.enabled=true
esi.server.core.dsls.address="license-server-address:4085"
esi.server.core.dsls.mode="CUSTOM"
esi.server.core.dsls.edition="MC5"	    // possible values: "MCS", "MC5", "MCSEDU"
esi.server.core.dsls.connection-packs=2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>where:</p><ul><li><strong>4085 </strong>is the default port.</li><li>The valid values of the <strong>mode</strong> property are:<ul><li>CUSTOM - This means that <span>the address provided in the <strong>address</strong> property is used to connect to the licensing server.</span></li><li><span>ORGANIZATION_DEFINED - In this case, </span>you do not need to specify the <strong>address</strong> property since it is already defined in the <em>DSLicSrv.txt</em> file by the organization. The API will check out licenses from servers defined in that file. You can find the file in <em>C:\ProgramData\DassaultSystemes\Licenses</em> on Windows or <em>/var/DassaultSystemes/Licenses</em> on Linux and Mac.</li></ul></li><li>The valid value of the <strong>edition</strong> property is the product trigram used to check out licenses in DSLS. To find the trigram of your product, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024x" ri:content-title="Product trigrams" /></ac:link>.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be701496-5c38-4901-9ba7-77ed1e3537b9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">MCS Academic license</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you are using an MCS Academic license, you should set the following:</p><ul><li><p>edition = &quot;<strong>MCSEDU</strong>&quot;</p></li><li><p>the connection-packs property does not need to be specified.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991971 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00017: Assigning roles

- page_id: `137991971`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991971/Assigning+roles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-02T16:41:08.836+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Roles application > Managing roles
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

In the Roles application, you can assign a predefined or custom role to users or user groups as described below. When assigning roles, you can also specify the role scope (resources the role applies to) for the users or user groups the role is assigned to.

#### NOTE: Assigning roles

Assigning roles

Only users with the Resource Manager role can assign roles to users and user groups.

##### Assigning a role to a user or user group

To assign a role to a user or user group

1. In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:
  - Select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the Role assignments card. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_role_assignments.png']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to a role name and select Change role assignments .
2. When the role assignments screen opens, select the **Add user/group** tab.
3. In the search bar, start typing a user or user group name and select the desired name from the search result list. The selected user or user group should be added to the role assignments screen. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

If you want to assign multiple roles to a user at once, you can do it in the Users application. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding roles' space=''].

##### Removing users or user groups from a role

You can use the Roles application to remove a user or user group from a role. When you remove a user/group from a role, you take away the permissions associated with the role, which will cause any resources assigned to the role to be removed as well. When you remove a user group from a role on the**Users**application, you cancel the role assigned to all users in the group.

To remove a user/group from a role

1. In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:
  - Select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the Role assignments card.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to a role name and select Change role assignments .
2. When the role assignments screen opens, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the name of the user or user group you want to remove. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If the are a lot of users or user groups assigned to the role, go to the **Find user/group** tab and use the search bar to find the user or user group you need.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the changes.

For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding roles" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removing users or user groups from a role</h3><p>You can use the Roles application to remove a user or user group from a role. When you remove a user/group from a role, you take away the permissions associated with the role, which will cause any resources assigned to the role to be removed as well. When you remove a user group from a role on the<strong> Users </strong>application, you cancel the role assigned to all users in the group.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a user/group from a role</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>In the content pane of the Roles application, do one of the following actions:<ul><li>Select a role and when the <strong>Role</strong> pane opens, click the <strong>Change</strong> button in the <strong>Role assignments</strong> card.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to a role name and select <strong>Change role assignments</strong>.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the role assignments screen opens, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png" /></ac:image> next to the name of the user or user group you want to remove.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_user_from_role.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ce73e6d-eeaa-45cf-817a-ce374e9f0aeb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the are a lot of users or user groups assigned to the role, go to the <strong>Find user/group</strong> tab and use the search bar to find the user or user group you need.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> to save the changes.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992237 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00018: Assigning users to resources

- page_id: `137992237`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992237/Assigning+users+to+resources
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-01-06T14:17:42.432+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Resources application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

In the Resources application, you can assign users to a specific resource by giving them a certain role. As a result, the permissions of that role are applied to the user in the scope of the resource.

##### Assigning users to resources

To assign users to a resource

1. In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to assign users to and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the resource and select Change assignments .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and editing resource details' space=''] and in the Resource assignments card click the Change button. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_assignments_card.png']
2. In the resource assignments screen, use the search box to find and select the user you want to assign. The selected user is added and displayed in the **Assigned users/groups** list.
3. Click the **Add new role** box and select the role you want to add. The assigned role is displayed on the bottom of the user card. Changing roles of assigned usersIf you want to change the roles of the user who is already assigned to the resource, select the **Find user/group** tab and use the search box to find the user whose roles you want to change. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. If needed, repeat steps 2 and 3 to assign more users to the resource.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_users.png'] in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the assigned users. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_assignments_screen.png']

The added users are assigned to the resource and given the selected roles in the resource scope.

##### Removing users from resources

You can use the Resources application to remove users assigned to a specific resource.

To remove users from a resource

1. In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to remove users from and do one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the resource and select Change assignments .
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Viewing and editing resource details' space=''] and in the Resource assignments card click the Change button.
2. In the resource assignments screen, select the Find user/group tab and use the search box to find the user you want to remove.
3. In the bottom right corner of the user card, click [ATTACHMENT filename='delete_user.png'] to remove the user.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_users.png'] in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce9cc659-3f5b-4333-b763-92b09be57e37"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>In the Resources application, you can assign users to a specific resource by giving them a certain role. As a result, the permissions of that role are applied to the user in the scope of the resource.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Assigning users to resources</h3><p>To assign users to a resource</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to assign users to and do one of the following:<ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the resource and select <strong>Change assignments</strong>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Viewing and editing resource details" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the resource details pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and in the <strong>Resource assignments</strong> card click the <strong>Change</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignments_card.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the resource assignments screen, use the search box to find and select the user you want to assign. The selected user is added and displayed in the <strong>Assigned users/groups</strong> list.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Add new role</strong> box and select the role you want to add. The assigned role is displayed on the bottom of the user card.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9976359d-a19c-494c-9fdf-6a3adef6c9b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Changing roles of assigned users</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to change the roles of the user who is already assigned to the resource, select the <strong>Find user/group</strong> tab and use the search box to find the user whose roles you want to change.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="finding_assigned_users.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>If needed, repeat steps 2 and 3 to assign more users to the resource.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_users.png" /></ac:image> in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the assigned users.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_assignments_screen.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br />The added users are assigned to the resource and given the selected roles in the resource scope.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Removing users from resources</h3><p>You can use the Resources application to remove users assigned to a specific resource.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove users from a resource</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>In the Resources application, find the resource that you want to remove users from and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the resource and select <strong>Change assignments</strong>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Viewing and editing resource details" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the resource details pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and in the <strong>Resource assignments</strong> card click the <strong>Change</strong> button.</li></ul></li><li>In the resource assignments screen, select the <strong>Find user/group</strong> tab and use the search box to find the user you want to remove.</li><li>In the bottom right corner of the user card, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_user.png" /></ac:image> to remove the user.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_users.png" /></ac:image> in the top right corner of the resource assignments screen to save the changes.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991476 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00020: Authentication by certificate

- page_id: `137991476`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991476/Authentication+by+certificate
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:40:10.311+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server > Common Access Card
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following parameters are utilized to authenticate the user by using certificates, e.g., certificates from CAC authentication.

| Parameter | Required | Description | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| authentication.certificate.enabled | no | An option that enables or disables certificate authentication. Available values are true and false. | false |
| authentication.certificate.headers.enabled | no | An option that enables or disables certificate authentication when authentication server is under proxy. Available values are true and false. This option by default is hidden and should be used only if authentication server is not accessible without a proxy.Appropriate HTTP headers should be sent by the proxy:X-Issuer-DnX-Subject-DnX-Serial-Number (hex format) | false |
| authentication.certificate.username.source | no | Source of the username. Available values: dn (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or san (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name). | dn |
| authentication.certificate.username.san.type | no | If authentication.certificate.username.source value is san, this parameter specifies the type of SAN to use. Available values:0 - other name,1 - RFC 822 name,2 - DNS name,4 - directory name (in case of this type parameter authentication.certificate.username.template is required). | 4 |
| authentication.certificate.username.template | no | Template that is used to create username from the subject DN (Distinguished Name) or SAN (Subject Alternative Name) of type 4 stored on the certificate. Required if certificate authentication is enabled.The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in round brackets that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN. | (CN) |
| authentication.certificate.displayname.source | no | Source of the display name on login page. Available values: dn (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or san (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name). | dn |
| authentication.certificate.displayname.san.type | no | If authentication.certificate.displayname.source value is san, this parameter specifies the type of SAN to use. Available values:0 - other name,1 - RFC 822 name,2 - DNS name,4 - directory name (in case of this type parameter authentication.certificate.displayname.template is required). | 4 |
| authentication.certificate.displayname.template | no | Template that is used to create a display of the subject DN (Distinguished Name) or SAN (Subject Alternative Name) of type 4 stored on the certificate. Required if certificate authentication is enabled.The template specified the same way as username template, except that display name is used for display purposes only. Display name is shown on authentication button that enables the user to authenticate with the selected certificate. | (CN) |
| authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file | no | The absolute path to the certificate revocation list (CRL) file, if it is stored on the filesystem. Multiple files are available if there are several certificate revocation lists.If there are multiple files, separate each file by a comma (for example, <Path and file name 1>,<Path and file name 2>, <Path and file name 3>). | - |
| authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url | no | The URL of the certificate revocation list (CRL) file, if it is available on the web. Multiple URLs are available if there are several certificate revocation lists.If there are multiple URLs, separate each URL by a comma (for example, <URL1>,<URL2>, <URL3>). | - |

#### PANEL: A configuration example

A configuration example

```text
authentication.certificate.enabled=trueauthentication.certificate.username.source=sanauthentication.certificate.username.san.type=4authentication.certificate.username.template=(O)-(CN)authentication.certificate.displayname.source=dnauthentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)'s Smart Card
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This option by default is hidden and should be used only if authentication server is not accessible without a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="944c181f-7525-4cfc-8603-20e433bc8745">proxy</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p>Appropriate HTTP headers should be sent by the proxy:</p><pre>X-Issuer-Dn<br />X-Subject-Dn<br />X-Serial-Number (hex format)</pre></td><td colspan="1">false</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">authentication.certificate.username.source</td><td colspan="1">no</td><td colspan="1">Source of the username. Available values: <strong>dn</strong> (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or <strong>san</strong> (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name).</td><td colspan="1">dn</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">authentication.certificate.username.san.type</td><td colspan="1">no</td><td colspan="1"><p>If <em>authentication.certificate.username.source</em> value is <strong>san</strong>, this parameter specifies the type of SAN to use. Available values:</p><p><strong>0</strong> - other name,</p><p><strong>1</strong> - RFC 822 name,</p><p><strong>2</strong> - DNS name,</p><p><strong>4</strong> - directory name (in case of this type parameter <em>authentication.certificate.username.template</em> is required).</p></td><td colspan="1">4</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>authentication.certificate.username.template</p></td><td colspan="1">no</td><td colspan="1"><p>Template that is used to create username from the subject DN (Distinguished Name) or SAN (Subject Alternative Name) of type 4 stored on the certificate. Required if certificate authentication is enabled.</p><p>The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in round brackets that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN.</p></td><td colspan="1">(CN)</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">authentication.certificate.displayname.source</td><td colspan="1">no</td><td colspan="1">Source of the display name on login page. Available values: <strong>dn</strong> (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or <strong>san</strong> (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name).</td><td colspan="1">dn</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">authentication.certificate.displayname.san.type</td><td colspan="1">no</td><td colspan="1"><p>If <em>authentication.certificate.displayname.source</em> value is <strong>san</strong>, this parameter specifies the type of SAN to use. Available values:</p><p><strong>0</strong> - other name,</p><p><strong>1</strong> - RFC 822 name,</p><p><strong>2</strong> - DNS name,</p><p><strong>4</strong> - directory name (in case of this type parameter <em>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</em> is required).</p></td><td colspan="1">4</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</p></td><td colspan="1">no</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Template that is used to create a display of the subject DN (Distinguished Name) or SAN (Subject Alternative Name) of type 4 stored on the certificate. Required if certificate authentication is enabled.</p><p>The template specified the same way as username template, except that display name is used for display purposes only. Display name is shown on authentication button that enables the user to authenticate with the selected certificate.</p></div></td><td colspan="1">(CN)</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file</p></td><td colspan="1">no</td><td colspan="1"><p>The absolute path to the certificate revocation list (CRL) file, if it is stored on the filesystem. Multiple files are available if there are several certificate revocation lists.</p><p>If there are multiple files, separate each file by a comma (for example, &lt;Path and file name 1&gt;,&lt;Path and file name 2&gt;, &lt;Path and file name 3&gt;).</p></td><td colspan="1">-</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url</p></td><td colspan="1">no</td><td colspan="1"><p>The URL of the certificate revocation list (CRL) file, if it is available on the web. Multiple URLs are available if there are several certificate revocation lists.</p><p>If there are multiple URLs, separate each URL by a comma (for example, &lt;URL1&gt;,&lt;URL2&gt;, &lt;URL3&gt;).</p></td><td colspan="1">-</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f32a2e3d-f2ca-4ddb-a335-cb267ba9c33b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">A configuration example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>authentication.certificate.enabled=true<br />authentication.certificate.username.source=san<br />authentication.certificate.username.san.type=4<br />authentication.certificate.username.template=(O)-(CN)<br />authentication.certificate.displayname.source=dn<br />authentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)'s Smart Card</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991463 space=MCS2024x version=5 -->
## PAGE 00021: Authentication server

- page_id: `137991463`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991463/Authentication+server
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:47:36.461+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Configuration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Magic Collaboration Studio authentication server is built to facilitate an authentication service for an application to validate and authenticate an entity such as a remote user or a node that attempts to access a network by using account names and passwords. Not only can the authentication server authenticate users, but also create them as external users in the Magic Collaboration Studio system once they have been validated successfully. The option to allow the authentication server and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing LDAP servers' space=''] to automatically create a user as external once they have successfully logged in is provided on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accessing Magic Collaboration Studio web applications' space='']** page.

This section provides the configuration parameters of the authentication server. You can find the parameters in the Teamwork Cloud *dir/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties*file.

The AuthServer installation is changed in comparison to the earlier versions. Now, the Authentication server is placed under Web Application Platform and thus no longer runs as a separate service.

The following sections provide instructions on how to install and configure the authentication server in Magic Collaboration Studio.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Not only can the authentication server authenticate users, but also create them as external users in the Magic Collaboration Studio system once they have been validated successfully. The option to allow the authentication server and the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing LDAP servers" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[LDAP server]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to automatically create a user as external once they have successfully logged in is provided on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Accessing Magic Collaboration Studio web applications" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Server Settings]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="886bb431-9d59-42c0-a24d-d603321d9b5d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This section provides the configuration parameters of the authentication server. You can find the parameters in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2ba2121c-1a36-4120-be72-43368cdc335c">Teamwork C</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2ba2121c-1a36-4120-be72-43368cdc335c">loud <em>dir/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties</em></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2ba2121c-1a36-4120-be72-43368cdc335c"> fil</ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2ba2121c-1a36-4120-be72-43368cdc335c">e</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4b0c730-c810-4d2c-aee3-ee3531af4539"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="bbf7184c-0adb-402c-806b-5cab9a85d7d3">The AuthServer installation is changed in comparison to the earlier versions. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992312 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00022: Available report types

- page_id: `137992312`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992312/Available+report+types
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T11:02:08.722+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Reports application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are 5 types of reports available in the Reports application:

- The **User details** report allows you to export user data, such as their full name, phone number, or email. Also, you can choose to export user permissions, which is helpful for user audit purposes.
- The **Resource access** report shows a timeline of selected users who have accessed various resources in a given time frame.
- The **Resource modifications** report is a list of users who have modified resources in a given time frame with all essential information, such as the commit message.
- The **Failed login attempts** report is self-explanatory; it shows all failed logins. This is an essential report for IT administrators who audit server security.
- Finally, the **Audit report** is a list of users’ actions related to resources. Exported data shows which users were granted or lost access to a resource, the time of the action, and who performed it.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Reports application.
