It is recommended to scale up a cluster in multiples of 3.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>When setting up a Cassandra node, you need to configure a seed that indicates an initial contact point for the new node. So, you can configure the contact point using the IP of any existing active node in the cluster. Use the node's own IP when configuring the first node. For example, if you are configuring a three-node cluster and the IPs of the nodes are <em>10.1.1.101</em>, <em>10.1.1.102,</em> and <em>10.1.1.103,</em> select one of them (e.g. <em>10.1.1.101</em>) as the <strong>seed</strong>. When configuring <strong>cassandra.yaml</strong>, specify <em>10.1.1.101</em> as the contact point value for all of the three nodes.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Machine IP</th><th>10.1.1.101</th><th>10.1.1.102</th><th>10.1.1.103</th></tr><tr><td>seeds</td><td><strong>10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102</strong></td><td><strong>10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102</strong></td><td><strong>10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102</strong></td></tr><tr><td>listen_address</td><td><span>10.1.1.101</span></td><td><span>10.1.1.102</span></td><td><span>10.1.1.103</span></td></tr><tr><td>broadcast_rpc_address</td><td><span>10.1.1.101</span></td><td><span>10.1.1.102</span></td><td><span>10.1.1.103</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56cf9840-07e2-4539-8362-37f25e856605"><ac:rich-text-body><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Note that </span><strong style="text-align: left;">listen_address</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> and </span><strong style="text-align: left;">broadcast_rpc_address</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> are still set to their respective machine’s address</span>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Now that you understand what a seed is and how to configure it, follow the instructions to install and configure Cassandra on Windows or Linux operating system. </p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing Cassandra on Windows" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Windows instructions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024x" ri:content-title="Installing and configuring Cassandra on Linux" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Linux instructions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>After installing and configuring all nodes, start the seed node. Verify that it is operational by issuing the command &quot;nodetool status&quot;.</p><p>Once the seed node is operational, you can proceed to start the other nodes in the cluster. You must allow the node to join the cluster before proceeding to the next node. After all nodes have started, the &quot;nodetool status&quot; command will display results as shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba7bbd5e-a49a-46c3-8349-e4a3910a5243"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Datacenter: datacenter1
````html
=======================
Status=Up/Down
|/ State=Normal/Leaving/Joining/Moving
--   Address     Load      Tokens    Owns (effective)    Host ID                                   Rack
UN   10.1.1.101  6.7 GB    256       100%               b33c603a-95c7-426d-9f7b-ebad2375086a      rack1
UN   10.1.1.102  6.34 GB   256       100%               16f40503-4a65-45fe-9ee7-5d942506aa87      rack1
UN   10.1.1.103  6.15 GB   256       100%               2d90c119-08a4-4799-ac73-0440215d0b18      rack1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07a6a9fe-7183-468b-aa2e-dd0217632e83"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Node Consistency</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In order to ensure that all nodes are consistent, please run the &quot;nodetool repair&quot; command nightly. This can be scheduled as a crontab job or via Windows Task Scheduler. This only needs to be executed on a single node.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><strong>Cassandra configuration parameters </strong></h4><p><em>- </em><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic.contact-points</span></strong></p><p>The value of this parameter is a list of Cassandra node IP addresses. You can find this parameter in <strong>application.conf </strong>by looking for the following.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb80014f-e4c6-416b-891e-4851c286dc34"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">application.conf</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver {
        basic {
                contact-points = ["10.1.1.101:9042", "10.1.1.102:9042", "10.1.1.103:9042"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><em>- </em><strong>esi.persistence.cassandra.keyspace.replication-factor</strong></p><p>This parameter defines the Cassandra replication factor which describes how many copies of your data will be written by Cassandra. For example, replication factor 2 means your data will be written to 2 nodes.</p><p>For a three-node cluster, if you would like the cluster to be able to survive 1 node loss, you will need to set the replication factor to 3. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca7bf66d-9414-408c-ad38-4b3c0ef35458"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[persistence {
         cassandra {
                 keyspace {                                                                                           
                        replication-factor = 3                                         
                 }]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Please note that this configuration will be used only for the first time Magic Collaboration Studio connects to Cassandra and creates a new twc keyspace. Changing the replication factor after the keyspace has already been created is rather a complex task. Read <a href="https://docs.datastax.com/en/cql/3.3/cql/cql_using/useUpdateKeyspaceRF.html">this document</a> if you need to change it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e13764cb-5f63-4445-b3ab-7bba06e12951"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Magic Collaboration Studio uses QUORUM for both write and read consistency levels.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><a href="https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra/3.0/cassandra/dml/dmlAboutDataConsistency.html">Click here</a> for a detailed explanation of data consistency.</p><p><strong>Update Authentication Server parameters</strong></p><p style="text-align: left;">Apply <ac:link ac:anchor="Cassandra configuration parameters" /> to<span> </span><strong>authserver.properties.</strong> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f723bc72-23b0-4fb1-ac20-9f2dd42e272e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">perl</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[cassandra.contactPoints=10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102, 10.1.1.103
cassandra.keyspace.replication.factor=3]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To start up the Magic Collaboration Studio cluster, start the server on the seed machine and wait until you see a message similar to the following in the <strong>server.log</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f276482-11df-41b8-adc2-991a35f44f62"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[INFO  2017-02-15 10:57:08.409 Magic Collaboration Studio cluster with 1 node(s) : [10.1.1.111] [com.nomagic.esi.server.core.actor.ClusterHealthActor, twcloud-esi.actor.other-dispatcher-31]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Then you can start the server on the remaining machines. You should see the following messages in the <strong>server.log</strong> which shows all 3 nodes are forming the cluster.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5609385-9fcf-4ed9-b15b-b7abaee171f1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[INFO  2017-02-15 10:58:23.956 Magic Collaboration Studio cluster with 2 node(s) : [10.1.1.111, 10.1.1.112] [com.nomagic.esi.server.core.actor.ClusterHealthActor, twcloud-esi.actor.other-dispatcher-18]
INFO  2017-02-15 10:58:25.963 Magic Collaboration Studio cluster with 3 node(s) : [10.1.1.111, 10.1.1.112, 10.1.1.113] [com.nomagic.esi.server.core.actor .ClusterHealthActor, twcloud-esi.actor.other-dispatcher-18]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting up a Magic Collaboration Studio cluster</h3><p>Before setting up a Magic Collaboration Studio cluster, you need to determine the following.</p><ul><li>The initial number of nodes in the cluster.</li><li>The IP address of each Magic Collaboration Studio node.</li><li>Prepare a list of IPs of Cassandra nodes.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The instructions below use the following sample environment for ease of understanding.</p><ul><li>3 nodes Magic Collaboration Studio cluster.</li><li>Magic Collaboration Studio node IP addresses are <em>10.1.1.111, 10.1.1.112</em>, and <em>10.1.1.113.</em></li><li>All nodes will be used as seed nodes.</li><li>IP addresses of Cassandra nodes are <em>10.1.1.101, 10.1.1.102, 10.1.1.103.</em></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>Setting up the Magic Collaboration Studio cluster involves 2 groups of parameters in <strong>application.conf</strong>.</p><h4><strong>Magic Collaboration Studio clustering parameter</strong></h4><p><em>- </em><strong>akka.cluster.seed-nodes</strong></p><p>This parameter indicates the initial seed for the cluster.</p><p>If you install Magic Collaboration Studio using the installer file, you will be asked to provide the seed node IP during installation process and the value will be configured in <strong>application.conf</strong>.</p><p>If you install using the zip file, you will need to manually configure the parameter in<em> </em><strong>application.conf</strong>. Search for the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8352ec9-5ab7-4507-9aac-c749a7d3aa57"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">application.conf</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[seed-nodes = ["akka://twcloud@${seed-node.ip}:2552"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Replace <em>${seed-node.ip}</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">with the IP addresses of the seed nodes, so it should look similar to the following:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b0f896d-e1ba-4460-b63c-990b3a372a79"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">application.conf</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[seed-nodes = ["akka://twcloud@10.1.1.111:2552","akka://twcloud@10.1.1.112:2552","akka://twcloud@10.1.1.113:2552"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991482 space=MCS2024x version=5 -->
## PAGE 00031: Cassandra connection parameters

- page_id: `137991482`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991482/Cassandra+connection+parameters
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:41:04.171+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following data source parameters are used for integration with and connection to Cassandra.

| Parameter | Description | Default value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| cassandra.contactPoints | Cassandra hosts or IP addresses, separated by commas. | localhost |
| cassandra.port | Cassandra port for CQL clients. | 9042 |
| cassandra.keyspace.replication.factor | Cassandra replication factor for “auth” keyspace which is used by the authentication server. The replication factor should be the same as in the Teamwork Cloud application.conf file. | 1 |
| cassandra.connection.max.attempts | Maximum number of attempts to connect to Cassandra on server startup. | 10 |
| cassandra.connection.sleep.before.attempt | Time interval before connection attempts in milliseconds. | 30000 |
| cassandra.username | The user name used for connecting to Cassandra. | cassandra |
| cassandra.password | The password used for connecting to Cassandra. | cassandra |
| cassandra.ssl.enabled | Enable SSL authentication for Cassandra. | false |
| cassandra.ssl.ignore.certificate | If SSL certificate check should be ignored while connecting to Cassandra. | true |

To enable SSL authentication for Cassandra, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hardening Cassandra' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>The following data source parameters are used for integration with and connection to Cassandra.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Parameter</th><th>Description</th><th>Default value</th></tr><tr><td>cassandra.contactPoints</td><td>Cassandra hosts or IP addresses, separated by commas.</td><td>localhost</td></tr><tr><td>cassandra.port</td><td>Cassandra port for CQL clients.</td><td>9042</td></tr><tr><td>cassandra.keyspace.replication.factor</td><td>Cassandra replication factor for “auth” keyspace which is used by the authentication server. The r<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">eplication factor should be the same as in the Teamwork Cloud <strong>application.conf</strong> file.</span></td><td>1</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">cassandra.connection.max.attempts</td><td colspan="1">Maximum number of attempts to connect to Cassandra on server startup<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></span></td><td colspan="1">10</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">cassandra.connection.sleep.before.attempt</td><td colspan="1">Time interval before connection attempts in milliseconds<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></span></td><td colspan="1">30000</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>cassandra.username</p></td><td colspan="1">The user name used for connecting to Cassandra.</td><td colspan="1">cassandra</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>cassandra.password</p></td><td colspan="1">The password used for connecting to Cassandra.</td><td colspan="1">cassandra</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">cassandra.ssl.enabled</td><td colspan="1">Enable SSL authentication for Cassandra.</td><td colspan="1">false</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">cassandra.ssl.ignore.certificate</td><td colspan="1">If SSL certificate check should be ignored while connecting to Cassandra.</td><td colspan="1">true</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To enable SSL authentication for Cassandra, see <ac:link ac:anchor="SSLauthenticationforCassandra"><ri:page ri:content-title="Hardening Cassandra" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SSL authentication for Cassandra]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991428 space=MCS2024x version=26 -->
## PAGE 00032: Changing Magic Collaboration Studio license

- page_id: `137991428`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991428/Changing+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+license
- version_number: 26
- version_date: `2026-01-05T10:07:40.503+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > License management
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

##### Using the FlexNet license server

You need to have the Server Administrator role to change the license.

To change the Magic Collaboration Studio license using the FlexNet license server

1. In a web browser, go to http(s)://<domain_name>:<port>/webapp to open the authentication page.
2. Sign in to the Administrator account or a user account with the Server Administrator ****role
3. Open the Settings application .
4. In the left-side menu select the Server license .
5. In the License information card, click the Change License button. A d ialog informing tha t you are about to delete the license will open.
6. Click the **Change** button. You cannot undo your action after clicking the **Change** button.
7. If you want to apply a license from another license server, change the license server address in the Server license field.
8. Click the **Show Licenses**button.
9. Select a new license key and click the Confirm button.

After changing the license, a new license is displayed on the **License information** card.

##### Using DSLS

If you use DSLS, follow the steps for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Magic Collaboration Studio license' space=''] and change the appropriate property values to update the license.

A d<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f8e0901c-a59b-48fa-a6ef-636e9a823ffd">ialog informing tha</ac:inline-comment-marker>t you are about to delete the license will open.</li><li><p>Click the <strong>Change</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="734b636d-2694-4b4f-8a3a-d0deb3afc9bd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot undo your action after clicking the <strong>Change</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>If you want to apply a license from another license server, change the license server address in the <strong> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Server license</span> </strong> field.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cf755e04-79aa-498d-86b3-8deff991fce3">Click the <strong>Show Licenses </strong>button.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a765d74a-c7eb-4937-9602-f6cd3e1a9608">Select</ac:inline-comment-marker> a new license key and click the <strong>Confirm </strong>button.</li></ol><p><br />After changing the license, a new license is displayed on the <strong>License information</strong> card.</p><h3><br />Using DSLS</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">If you use DSLS, follow the steps for</span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <ac:link ac:anchor="Using DSLS"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Magic Collaboration Studio license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applying the license]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and change the appropriate property values to update the license.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991588 space=MCS2024x version=6 -->
## PAGE 00033: Changing SSL certificates

- page_id: `137991588`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991588/Changing+SSL+certificates
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:45:28.474+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.

##### Changing the SSL certificate file

You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.

To change the SSL certificate file

1. Go to the <web_app_platform_installation_directory>\conf directory and open to edit the server.xml file.
2. In the server.xml file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password: ImportantIf you have created the SSL certificate file as described in the above section, the **certificateKeystorePassword** property value must be the same as the **-storepass** and **-keypass** property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.
  - certificateKeystoreFile="<path_to_certificate_file>"
  - certificateKeystorePassword="<password>"
3. Save and close the server.xml file.
4. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f84b41de-8fb8-49a9-824d-f87049f135cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>By default, Web Application Platform is installed with SSL certificates. If needed, you can change the existing SSL certificate file with a new one as described below.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Changing the SSL certificate file</h3><p>You can change the default SSL certificate file that comes with Web Application Platform installation as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the SSL certificate file</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;web_app_platform_installation_directory&gt;\conf</em> directory and open to edit the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>In the <em>server.xml</em> file, change the default values of the following properties to the actual path to the certificate file and password:<br /><ul><li><pre>certificateKeystoreFile=&quot;&lt;path_to_certificate_file&gt;&quot;</pre></li><li><pre>certificateKeystorePassword=&quot;&lt;password&gt;&quot;<br /><br /></pre></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="027c18b8-3066-4e11-9adb-e8dab5278eae"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have created the SSL certificate file as described in the above section, the <strong>certificateKeystorePassword</strong> property value must be the same as the <strong>-storepass</strong> and <strong>-keypass</strong> property values that you specified when creating the SSL certificate.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the <em>server.xml</em> file.</li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991583 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00034: Changing Web Application Platform property values

- page_id: `137991583`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991583/Changing+Web+Application+Platform+property+values
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:45:20.652+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the *webappplatform.properties* file. Click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using no-installer' space=''], to learn more about each property.

To change Web Application Platform property values

1. Go to the *<* install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open for editing the webappplatform.properties file.
2. Change the desired property values.
3. Restart the Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After installing the Web Application Platform, you can change its property values in the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file. Click <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using no-installer" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, to learn more about each property.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change Web Application Platform property values</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><em>&lt;</em></span><em style="text-align: left;">install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory and open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li>Change the desired property values.</li><li>Restart the Web Application Platform.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991593 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00035: Checking current version and user count

- page_id: `137991593`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991593/Checking+current+version+and+user+count
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-02-03T10:13:15.133+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.

To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information

1. Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.
2. Go to http(s)://<cc_for_twc_host>:<cc_for_twc_port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info .

The website displays all licence information, such as licence type, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can check the version, maximum user count and other licence information of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud installed in your environment.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check Cameo Collaborator for TWC licence information</p><hr /><ol><li>Sign in to Cameo Collaborator for TWC.</li><li>Go to <em>http(s)://&lt;cc_for_twc_host&gt;:&lt;cc_for_twc_port&gt;/collaborator/api/collaborator/license/info</em>.</li></ol><p><br />The website displays all licence information, such as licence <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d4430fa4-f2e4-49a6-a0d3-6698f17baa5b">type</ac:inline-comment-marker>, expiration date, maximum user count and product version.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992120 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00036: Checking LDAP connection

- page_id: `137992120`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992120/Checking+LDAP+connection
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:51:09.884+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.

To check the connection to a LDAP server

1. On the **LDAP Management** page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
2. Select **Check connection**. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messages will appear.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Upon adding and configuring the LDAP server, you may want to test whether your connection to the LDAP server works. If the test is not successful, you need to make necessary changes to the settings, save the changes, and retest the connection.</p><p><br /></p><p>To check the connection to a LDAP server</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the <strong>LDAP Management</strong> page, select an LDAP server which connection you want to check and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Managing users" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Select <strong>Check connection</strong>. If the connection works, you will get a success message, otherwise connection-related error messag<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8d9608cd-f9a4-45af-af63-ab4980ff04c2">es will appear.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992344 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00037: Collaborator View Diagram

- page_id: `137992344`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992344/Collaborator+View+Diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:08:22.802+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with custom document templates > Creating a custom template
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.

##### The Document element

A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog when [publishing a project](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024x/Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface).

##### The View element

A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling the Single Tree View' space=''].

The main properties of a View element are:

- Skip if Empty - if set as true , a published project does not display views without data.
- Document View - if set as true , this particular view of a document is presented as in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling the Document View' space=''].
- **Allow To Switch Property View Mode** - if set as *true*, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. To change the mode when browsing a document, open the view, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select **Model View** or **Document View**.

##### The Viewpoint element

A Viewpoint is used to define the rules that dictate how information is collected from the model and displayed on the web. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram' space=''].

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adbd07e7-0846-427e-bd25-ba64d756983c"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Cameo Collaborator View Diagram defines the structure of a Cameo Collaborator document. The essential components of this diagram are Document, View, and ViewPoint. You can create them by using the diagram palette of the Collaborator View Diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper image-center-wrapper"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_template_example.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Basic View Diagram containing Document, View, and Viewpoint elements with Directed Composition and Conform relations.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The Document element</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A Document is the root element of a template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template. You can select the template by this name this name in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog when</span> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024x/Publishing+from+graphical+user+interface">publishing a project</a>.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />The View element</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A View is a standalone description of the model within your template. Each view is used to display a certain type of information. You can create as many views as required. Different views not only display different types of information, e.g., requirements or use cases, but also create separate navigation trees (views) in a published project. Note that you can choose to have all of your views displayed in a single tree by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling the Single Tree View" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[enabling the Single Tree View mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p>The main properties of a View element are:</p><ul><li><strong>Skip if Empty</strong> -<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, a published project does not display views without data.</span></li><li><strong>Document View</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> - </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">if set as </span><em>true</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">, this particular view of a document is presented as in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling the Document View" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Document view mode]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Allow To Switch Property View Mode</strong> - if set as <em>true</em>, the mode of this particular view can be switched from Model View to Document View and vice versa. To change the mode when browsing a document, open the view, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image>, and select <strong>Model View</strong> or <strong>Document View</strong>.</span></li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br />The Viewpoint element<br /></span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">A Viewpoint is used to define the rules that dictate how information is collected from the model and displayed on the web. Viewpoints are related to views through a Conform relationship. The rules contained in a viewpoint are modeled in an activity-like form utilizing a special <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992339 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00038: Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram

- page_id: `137992339`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992339/Collaborator+Viewpoint+Method+Diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:06:42.537+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with custom document templates > Creating a custom template
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

The Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram is a specialized activity diagram that defines a Viewpoint. This diagram allows you to obtain model data using several types of operators and present the obtained data as tables, images, paragraphs, lists, and sections in a web portal.

Once a Viewpoint is applied to a View, exposed elements can be operated by three types of operators: Collect, Sort, and Filter. You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.

##### Collect operators

In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.

##### Filter operators

Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the "FilterBy..." operator needs to have the "CollectBy..." operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.

##### Sort operators

Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.

##### Table Structure operators

Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.

##### Template operators

After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed. Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

