You can use these operators to expand or narrow the collection of elements that are used in a Viewpoint method. After the data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, use the Table Structure and Template operators to adjust how the data is displayed. The figure below shows how available operators are grouped in the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><h3><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="viewpoint_method_diagram_pallet.png" /></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>The diagram palette containing operators available in a Viewpoint Method Diagram.</span></h6><h3>Collect operators</h3><p>In a Viewpoint Method Diagram, Collect operators are used to divide the elements in an exposed Package into the elements that will be presented in a web portal and the elements that will be ignored. This way you can expand or narrow the collection of elements used in a Viewpoint method. There are a number of collection methods, each of which have their own Call Behavior Action.</p><h3><br />Filter operators</h3><p>Filter operators allow you to narrow the scope of data to the data of interest according to one or multiple filter criteria of various types (such as stereotype, name, metaclasse, etc). In most cases, the &quot;FilterBy...&quot; operator needs to have the &quot;CollectBy...&quot; operator preceding it so that the filter operator would have the data set to look through and filter.</p><h3><br />Sort operators</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Sort operators allow you to sort a data set according to the desired property. Data can be sorted by attribute, property, or expression. Note that first you have to collect elements in order to have a data set to sort.</span></p><h3><br />Table Structure operators</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Table Structure operators allow you to create custom tables by using various table components. The Viewpoint Method Diagram below shows the example of a simple table with only two columns,</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table_structure_example.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of a simple table with two columns: Animal Name and Description.</h6><h3>Template operators</h3><p>After data is collected, filtered, and/or sorted, several operators can be used to adjust how the data will be displayed.  Template operators are used in a Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram to determine in what format data will be displayed in the document views - as paragraphs, properties tables, images, bulleted lists, etc.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Additional resources</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/NAoYVUJhT2A">Webinar <em>Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator</em></a></li><li><a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992690 space=MCS2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00039: Command Line Interface (CLI) for user administration

- page_id: `137992690`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992690/Command+Line+Interface+CLI+for+user+administration
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:57:14.067+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Developer Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

Besides using Teamwork Cloud Admin for administrative tasks, you can also use the Command Line Interface (CLI) to create and manage users, roles, and permissions. All of the CLI supported in Magic Collaboration Studio are properly defined on this page. You can also find them via Swagger where you can find the command-line description and issue it easily. We recommend you use CLI via [Swagger UI](https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator) because executing the command via Swagger also shows you the correct curl command. This link [https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator](https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator) takes you to the Magic Collaboration Studio CLI documentation in Swagger.

###### Assign a project role to a particular user

Sending POST to */osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resouceId}/roles/{roleId}/users* assigns a project role to a specified user.

```text

```

```text

```

**HTTP Status**

201 with empty body.

###### List all permissions

The */osmc/admin/permissions* lists all permissions in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### createroleCreate a role

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/roles* creates a row in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### Edit a role

Sending PATCH to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}* edits a row in the server. For the format of the request and the response, see .

###### Delete a role

DELETE to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}* deletes a row in the server.

###### Create a user

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/users* creates a new user. The user must not be an LDAP user.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### Edit a user

Sending PATCH to */osmc/admin/users/{username}* edits a user.

```text

```

The key *userName* is ignored. By default, new **otherAttributes** are merged into the server. If the value of a key in **otherAttributes** is empty, the key will be removed from the server.

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

###### List all LDAP configurations

The */osmc/admin/ldaps* returns a JSON array containing all LDAP configurations in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Import a user from LDAP

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/import{username}* imports an LDAP user into Magic Collaboration Studio.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Search a user in an LDAP server

Sending GET to */osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/search?<query string>* searches users from a specified LDAP server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### List all users

The */osmc/admin/users* returns a JSON array containing usernames in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Get user information

The */osmc/admin/users/{username}* returns a JSON object containing information of a specified user.

```text

```

```text

```

###### LIst all roles

The */osmc/admin/roles* lists all rows in the server.

```text

```

```text

```

###### List users assigned to a particular role

The *_/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users* lists users assigned to a particular role.

```text

```

```text

```

###### Unassign a role from a particular user

Sending HTTP DELETE to */admin/roles/{roleId}/users/{username}* unassigns a particular role from a specified user.

**HTTP Response**

204 with empty body.

###### Unassign a project role from a particular user

Sending DELETE to *_/osmc/workspaces/\{workspaceId\}/resources/\{resourceId\}/roles/\{roleId\}/users/\{username\}_* unassigns a project role from a particular user.

**HTTP Status**

204 with empty body.

###### Assign a role to a particular user

Sending POST to */osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users* assigns a role to a user.

```text

```

```text

```

**HTTP Status**

201 with empty body

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26b55dcc-9a3e-4692-bb20-2b1d3572a5fa" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Besides using Teamwork Cloud Admin for administrative tasks, you can also use the Command Line Interface (CLI) to create and manage users, roles, and permissions. All of the CLI supported in Magic Collaboration Studio are properly defined on this page. You can also find them via Swagger where you can find the command-line description and issue it easily<span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-gray,#44546f);">.</span> We recommend you use CLI via <a href="https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator" style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-blue-bolder,#0c66e4);">Swagger UI</a> because executing the command via Swagger also shows you the correct curl command. This link <a href="https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator" style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-blue-bolder,#0c66e4);">https://osmc.nomagic.com/#/Administrator</a> takes you to the Magic Collaboration Studio CLI documentation in Swagger.</p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Assign a project role to a particular user</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/workspaces/{workspaceId}/resources/{resouceId}/roles/{roleId}/users</em>  assigns a project role to a specified user.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2523ce80-55ce-4ac6-9056-6ba251a64e4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: text/plain]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1db6103d-a876-4271-b03b-5057b71ef037"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[user1,user2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>HTTP Status</strong></p><p>201 with empty body.</p><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">List all permissions</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/permissions</em> lists all permissions in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6fc347bf-a120-44da-90ec-90763f9fa0a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15ac7364-2bca-4ad0-aa16-c234df537991"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "operationAssignableAccessScope": "GLOBAL_ONLY",
    "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
    "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_list.all.resources",
    "operationName": "list.all.resources",
    "ID": "d91a7ba9-a017-44ac-9ff8-4b35635cb7b9",
    "operationDisplayName": "List All Projects",
    "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
}, {
    "operationAssignableAccessScope": "GLOBAL_OR_OBJECT",
    "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
    "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_read.resource",
    "operationName": "read.resource",
    "ID": "9649cb30-6933-49f1-b309-7aade63340cc",
    "operationDisplayName": "Read Projects",
    "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
}, {
    "operationAssignableAccessScope": "GLOBAL_ONLY",
    "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.server",
    "name": "com.nomagic.esi.server_manage.user.permissions",
    "operationName": "manage.user.permissions",
    "ID": "8d7423b8-4e8c-4d32-8d3c-783504bef044",
    "operationDisplayName": "Manage User Permissions",
    "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Server"
}]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d3816a9d-d7ec-45df-bc9e-d4856305e215"><ac:parameter ac:name="">createrole</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a role</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/admin/roles</em> creates a row in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6958de4d-ca96-447f-a5b1-ba72acf26c65"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca05d13f-084c-4758-a3c2-83c9f9f7dcdd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "permissions": ["9649cb30-6933-49f1-b309-7aade63340cc"],
    "name": "new role name",
    "description": "new row description",
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e54d0b6d-ac93-4170-be4a-c09152697cf2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="649f8efc-2bcd-4e90-909c-0efd7d5b7572"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "permissions": [{
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_edit.resource",
        "operationName": "edit.resource",
        "ID": "0b972f77-368c-4511-9285-0069a1a8bf07",
        "operationDisplayName": "Edit Projects",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
    }],
    "name": "Project Contributor",
    "description": "Project-specific role. Users who are assigned to this role can modify content of selected project.",
    "ID": "417494bc-d0e8-449a-a8ac-5476dc2e6537"
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Edit a role</h4><p>Sending PATCH to <em>/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}</em> edits a row in the server. For the format of the request and the response, see <ac:link ac:anchor="createrole"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a role]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Delete a role</h4><p>DELETE to <em>/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}</em> deletes a row in the server.</p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Create a user</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/admin/users</em> creates a new user. The user must not be an LDAP user.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b0c42859-2137-4b3d-9f45-b27d12ef19bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e27577da-6df9-4ce8-b122-089b29f81f77"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body (note that the otherAttributes map must not contain realmid key)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
	"password": "a passward",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1618f2a1-9cec-4d4d-9bb5-34b9119876c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="31827ac7-726d-4d71-9ab9-ecbc613b087b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Edit a user</h4><p>Sending PATCH to <em>/osmc/admin/users/{username}</em> edits a user. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e41558a-2f85-4837-a331-b87ab13f241d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The key <em>userName</em> is ignored. By default, new <strong>otherAttributes</strong> are merged into the server. If the value of a key in <strong>otherAttributes</strong> is empty, the key will be removed from the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e165216e-796b-4d06-b165-428df6004704"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
	"password": "a passward",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f73afe86-a8fe-4f51-b4ea-2702acdb91f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a204ef23-3565-4f79-a13c-265a4130f764"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">List all LDAP configurations</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps</em> returns a JSON array containing all LDAP configurations in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81793cdb-7e88-4bae-9ff0-3be38292c4a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95405d87-0c87-4d9a-af74-8a86524cc5d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "environment": {
        "authentype": "simple",
        "searchbase": "ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
        "authen_dntype": "template",
        "anoymousbind": "false",
        "ldap_realm_name": "ldap0",
        "java.naming.factory.initial": "com.sun.jndi.ldap.LdapCtxFactory",
        "java.naming.security.protocol": "none",
        "userDNTemplate": "uid={0},ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
        "com.sun.jndi.ldap.read.timeout": "500",
        "java.naming.provider.url": "ldap://ldap.th.nomagic.com:389",
        "weight": "1073741823",
        "enabled": "true"
    },
    "protocol": "ldap",
    "port": "389",
    "IP": "ldap.th.nomagic.com",
    "ID": "36f8b736-f51c-43a2-a9a6-63905419838a",
    "authen": "simple",
    "userName": "cn=admin,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com",
    "url": "ldap://ldap.th.nomagic.com:389"
}]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Import a user from LDAP</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/import{username}</em> imports an LDAP user into Magic Collaboration Studio.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e4039fe-5cc2-4fba-b8f3-4f59dfa3eef1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="633ae014-fa29-4246-8bd2-aad4e767890f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "userName": "test2",
    "password": "a passward",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "mobile": "456",
        "realmid": "36f8b736-f56c-43a2-a9a6-54128919838a",
        "name": "a full name",
        "department": "department",
        "email": "user1@notset.com"
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Search a user in an LDAP server</h4><p>Sending GET to <em>/osmc/admin/ldaps/{ldapId}/search?&lt;query string&gt;</em> searches users from a specified LDAP server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="716cf3c7-d0a7-497e-9c85-e858fbaea137"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="833c82a9-15ff-4d4a-830a-a64b297e4c18"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "mobile": "0123456789",
    "fullName": "Bat",
    "department": "production",
    "userName": "bat",
    "email": "bat@nma.com",
    "userDN": "uid=bat,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}, {
    "mobile": "",
    "fullName": "abc",
    "department": "",
    "userName": "abc",
    "email": "",
    "userDN": "uid=abc,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}, {
    "mobile": "",
    "fullName": "aaaa",
    "department": "",
    "userName": "aaa",
    "email": "",
    "userDN": "uid=aaa,ou=Users,dc=nomagicasia,dc=com"
}]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">List all users</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/users</em> returns a JSON array containing usernames in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0eedc27c-44a6-49ae-87c3-b4cbb4d56698"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="021da6af-cb78-46e6-90dc-2d0a3b4b6d14"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[["admin", "observer1", "user1", "user2", "worker1"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Get user information</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/users/{username}</em> returns a JSON object containing information of a specified user.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb1b2b61-98a6-4e4c-b20f-204926726d51"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aff6d549-2ed1-4bbc-ac71-3c3da655be30"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[{
    "roleAssignments": [{
        "roleID": "3e4696d5-1937-4741-aacb-062378e0cda2",
        "protectedObjects": [{
            "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
            "ID": "cda926be-58c7-4d3f-acb2-e9cdb57d8aaf"
        }],
        "ID": "3871e058-65e6-40d9-bd23-f80bdc074394"
    }, {
        "roleID": "417494bc-d0e8-449a-a8ac-5476dc2e6537",
        "protectedObjects": [{
            "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
            "ID": "cda926be-58c7-4d3f-acb2-e9cdb57d8aaf"
        }],
        "ID": "54b8ef7f-f60c-410f-8a09-93a15c51bbaa"
    }, {
        "roleID": "a020ca32-22a2-4c90-8513-f6b9c4ea8513",
        "protectedObjects": [{
            "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
            "ID": "cda926be-58c7-4d3f-acb2-e9cdb57d8aaf"
        }],
        "ID": "51ae2287-405e-42a6-807f-ca5e6f2a41d0"
    }, {
        "roleID": "e3cf7416-9cf3-42ff-84e6-e9d4283dfd18",
        "protectedObjects": [{
            "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
            "ID": "cda926be-58c7-4d3f-acb2-e9cdb57d8aaf"
        }],
        "ID": "a147bf72-1a51-49f5-baf9-ac3f2b6faf50"
    }],
    "userName": "user1",
    "otherAttributes": {
        "realmid": "",
        "mobile": "",
        "name": "",
        "department": "",
        "email": ""
    },
    "enabled": true
}]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">LIst all roles</h4><p>The <em>/osmc/admin/roles</em> lists all rows in the server.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09895de5-df3d-4961-b0a2-072397b91b10"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1da72351-7f11-4333-b81f-cd943b9fbaad"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[[{
    "permissions": [{
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_create.resource",
        "operationName": "create.resource",
        "ID": "930c939c-6ec4-4c90-9458-92eefc73b11b",
        "operationDisplayName": "Create Project",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
    }, {
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_categorize.resources",
        "operationName": "categorize.resources",
        "ID": "9a223c45-71eb-4e45-b374-a8dd319afcea",
        "operationDisplayName": "Categorize Projects",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
    }, {
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_list.all.resources",
        "operationName": "list.all.resources",
        "ID": "d91a7ba9-a017-44ac-9ff8-4b35635cb7b9",
        "operationDisplayName": "List All Projects",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
    }],
    "name": "Project Creator",
    "description": "Global or category-specific role. Users who are assigned to this role can add projects to the server including the ability to categorize them: create new categories or manage existing ones.",
    "ID": "15c045d8-44e1-4e14-8175-b209b6ae70a4"
}, {
    "permissions": [{
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_edit.resource",
        "operationName": "edit.resource",
        "ID": "0b972f77-368c-4511-9285-0069a1a8bf07",
        "operationDisplayName": "Edit Projects",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
    }, {
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_read.resource",
        "operationName": "read.resource",
        "ID": "9649cb30-6933-49f1-b309-7aade63340cc",
        "operationDisplayName": "Read Projects",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
    }, {
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.resource",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.resource_edit.resource.properties",
        "operationName": "edit.resource.properties",
        "ID": "a93ff74f-baae-4aea-8f79-1a9d423f35fa",
        "operationDisplayName": "Edit Project Properties",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Project"
    }],
    "name": "Project Contributor",
    "description": "Project-specific role. Users who are assigned to this role can modify content of selected project.",
    "ID": "417494bc-d0e8-449a-a8ac-5476dc2e6537"
}, {
    "permissions": [{
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.server",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.server_list.all.users",
        "operationName": "list.all.users",
        "ID": "34e47503-ad58-401b-a3d9-fdb0e00ea651",
        "operationDisplayName": "List All Users",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Server"
    }, {
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.server",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.server_remove.user",
        "operationName": "remove.user",
        "ID": "3f7a74c4-9a95-40a6-837a-2aaf7f5f91ef",
        "operationDisplayName": "Remove User",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Server"
    }, {
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.server",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.server_create.user",
        "operationName": "create.user",
        "ID": "d616eb9e-d1d4-4f2d-ad24-3cfb6e57d08e",
        "operationDisplayName": "Create User",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Server"
    }, {
        "operationAssignableAccessScope": {},
        "protectedType": "com.nomagic.esi.server",
        "name": "com.nomagic.esi.server_edit.user.properties",
        "operationName": "edit.user.properties",
        "ID": "d81818d4-0d98-4464-b05c-e54e4af82877",
        "operationDisplayName": "Edit User Properties",
        "protectedTypeDisplayName": "Server"
    }],
    "name": "User Manager",
    "description": "Global role. Users who are assigned to this role can create and manage users in a server.",
    "ID": "1b3a3af6-887f-4891-a3df-b0e7b9141ff2"
}]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">List users assigned to a particular role</h4><p>The <em>_/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users</em> lists users assigned to a particular role.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3c3da89-5c27-4b3b-a0d2-a9837b55ddbc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1f120ab-496b-4ac4-8175-c2bb63c72bd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP response body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[["admin","observer1"]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Unassign a role from a particular user</h4><p>Sending HTTP DELETE to <em>/admin/roles/{roleId}/users/{username}</em> unassigns a particular role from a specified user.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>HTTP Response</strong></p><p>204 with empty body.</p><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Unassign a project role from a particular user</h4><p>Sending DELETE to <em>_/osmc/workspaces/\{workspaceId\}/resources/\{resourceId\}/roles/\{roleId\}/users/\{username\}_</em> unassigns a project role from a particular user.</p><p><strong>HTTP Status</strong></p><p>204 with empty body.</p><p><br /></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Assign a role to a particular user</h4><p>Sending POST to <em>/osmc/admin/roles/{roleId}/users</em> assigns a role to a user.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b73926ca-e6c3-4f0a-a22b-9675af71c27d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request header</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Content-Type: text/plain]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43c88f46-412f-4ba3-b8ad-e4849cd86cf9"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">HTTP request body</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[user1,user2]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong>HTTP Status</strong></p><p>201 with empty body</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992432 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00040: Comments pane

- page_id: `137992432`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992432/Comments+pane
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:24:41.207+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['comments-pane', 'filtering-comments', 'sorting-comments', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The comments pane of a published model portal.

The comments pane can be shown or hidden.

To show/hide the comments pane

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show-hide_comments_pane.png'] in the title bar of a published model portal.

##### Navigating between comments and commented items

Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.

##### Searching for comments

The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.

To search for comments

1. Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.
2. Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:
  - Under the comment search box, select the Unresolved check box, to search only for unresolved comments.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **Unread** check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.
  - Under the comment search box, select the **High priority** check box, to search for comments with high priority.

When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c822421d-7fbb-4b98-9594-2d5882617869"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The comments pane is located on the right side of a published model portal. It displays all comments of all the views of a published model and their replies. Comments on the comments pane can be textual or graphical, resolved or unresolved, low or high priority. See the figure below to learn how different kinds of comments are displayed on the comments pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="comments_pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The comments pane of a published model portal.</h6><p>The comments pane can be shown or hidden.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show/hide the comments pane</p><hr style="" /><ul><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show-hide_comments_pane.png" /></ac:image> in the title bar of a published model portal.</li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Navigating between comments and commented items</h3><p>Clicking a comment or its reply in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the commented area. If the comment is graphical, clicking it in the comments pane opens the commented item in the content pane and selects the comment. Navigation between comments and commented items work both ways. If you select a commented item or a graphical comment in the content pane, the related comment is selected in the comments pane. If you select an item in the content pane that has several comments, the list of comments opens and you can select the comment you want find. The figures below display how graphical comments and items with textual comments are displayed in the content pane.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commented_items_in_content_pane.png" /></ac:image> </p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Commented items with textual comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="graphical_comments_in_content_pane.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Graphical comments displayed on the content pane.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Searching for comments</h3><p>The comments pane allows you to search for comments by the comment name, text, author and even creation date. You can also select to see only the unresolved comments.</p><p><br /></p><p>To search for comments</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Type a search phrase in the comment search box on the top of the comments pane.</li><li>Optionally, do one or both of the following to filter comments:<br /><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Unresolved</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a"> check box, to search only for unresolved comments.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>Unread</strong> check box, to search only for the comments that have not been read.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d59b19e-0160-4ea4-9d35-9f7abc83ab1a">Under the comment search box, select the <strong>High priority</strong> check box, to search for comments with high priority.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></li></ol><p><br />When you start typing the search phrase in the comments search box, the comment pane shows only the comments that match the search phrase text. Also, selecting one or several of the available filters helps to narrow down search results.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992587 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00041: Committing changes

- page_id: `137992587`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992587/Committing+changes
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:50:00.701+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing models in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Every time you modify a model in Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, the changes are saved locally, so you have to commit them to Magic Collaboration Studio when you are done editing. If there are changes in a Cameo Collaborator document that are not yet committed, the yellow dot over the cloud icon instantly notifies you about it, as shown in the image below.

To commit changes to Magic Collaboration Studio

1. In the app bar, click the cloud icon with the yellow dot notifying that there are uncommitted changes (see the image below).
2. In the **Uncommitted changes** dialog, enter the commit message in the **Commit message** field. This step is optional.
3. In the same dialog, Click the **Commit** button to commit model changes to Magic Collaboration Studio. Discarding model changesIf you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the **Discard** button in the **Uncommitted changes** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Committing model changes to Magic Collaboration Studio.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This step is optional.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the same dialog, Click the <strong>Commit</strong> button to commit model changes to Magic Collaboration Studio.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d191a22c-cd4f-426f-ba5e-fded8b0c3c7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Discarding model changes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to discard uncommitted model changes, click the <strong>Discard</strong> button in the <strong>Uncommitted changes</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="committing_changes.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Committing model changes to Magic Collaboration Studio.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991474 space=MCS2024x version=5 -->
## PAGE 00042: Common Access Card

- page_id: `137991474`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991474/Common+Access+Card
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:39:59.814+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Authentication server
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### **Basic Configuration**

In order for Common Access Card (CAC) authentication to work, SSL must be enabled in Web Application Platform.

Next, you need to enable certificate authentication:

```text

```

The next step is to configure which part of the subject DN (distinguished name) will be used as the username (**authentication.certificate.username.template**), and which part will be displayed in the login screen (**authentication.certificate.displayname.template**).

Both of these values default to using (CN)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Configure username

```text

```

The template can contain ASCII characters as well as placeholders in parenthesis that are replaced with relative distinguished name (RDN) values from the DN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Distinguished Name) or SAN (if the username is constructed from the Subject Alternative Name) .

For example, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB:

Template: *(CN)*, username: *JohnDoe*

Template: *(O)-(CN)*, username: *MyCompany*-*JohnDoe*

Template: *CERT_(CN)*, username: *CERT_JohnDoe*

##### Configure the value displayed in the login button

To configure the value displayed in the login button, we must edit the **authentication.certificate.displayname.template** property:

```text

```

For example, as shown in the picture above, when the subject DN or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is *CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB*, and the display template is *(CN) CERTIFICATE*, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.

For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Authentication by certificate' space=''].

##### TrustStore Configuration

CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.

Certificate verification should be enabled in the *WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml* file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on [this page](https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig). E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:

- certificateVerification with the value optional or required (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed) .
- truststoreFile - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.
- truststorePassword - truststore password.
- truststoreType - e.g. "JKS" or "PKCS12".

##### Certificate Revocation List

The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url** or **authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file**, and point it to the location of the revocation list.

```text

```

the <strong>authentication.certificate.displayname.template</strong> property:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a94afc71-b451-4bb8-bec3-e194fdeb8601"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.displayname.template=(CN)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, as shown in the picture above, when the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="74ff4462-1120-4b86-9374-542687e42278">subject DN</ac:inline-comment-marker> or SAN (of type 4) on the certificate is <em>CN=JohnDoe,O=MyCompany,C=GB</em>, and the display template is <em>(CN) CERTIFICATE</em>, the button will display „JOHNDOE CERTIFICATE“.</p><p>For a list of all the advanced properties available for configuration, please refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Authentication by certificate" /></ac:link>.  </p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">TrustStore Configuration</h3><p>CAC integration requires that a truststore containing the Certificate Authority (CA) certificates that issue the user's certificates would exist.</p><p>Certificate verification should be enabled in the <em>WebAppPlatform/conf/server.xml</em> file. See the Tomcat configuration information for it on <a href="https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-9.0-doc/config/http.html#SSL_Support_-_SSLHostConfig" style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-blue-bolder,#0c66e4);">this page</a>. E.g., if the Tomcat SSL implementation is JSSE-based, you will need to add the following attributes to the SSLHostConfig property under Connector:</p><ul><li><strong>certificateVerification</strong> with the value <em>optional</em> or <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8f2e55d2-d5a5-496a-9fb8-f96539dbcb98">required</ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8f2e55d2-d5a5-496a-9fb8-f96539dbcb98"> (if ONLY the certificate authentication is allowed)</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li><strong>truststoreFile</strong> - path to the truststore file containing the CA certificates.</li><li><strong>truststorePassword</strong> - truststore password.</li><li><strong>truststoreType</strong> - e.g. &quot;JKS&quot; or &quot;PKCS12&quot;.</li></ul><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Certificate Revocation List</h3><p>The authentication server supports 2 methods of handling certificate revocation lists - via a URL, or via a local list stored in the file system. To enable this feature, uncomment either <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url</strong> or <strong>authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file</strong>, and point it to the location of the revoc<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1354e677-93f8-4d14-a6a2-e1f397799f4f">ation list.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51f3c881-22bb-4a81-85d3-737159d3974d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[authentication.certificate.revocation.list.url=http://someserver.somedomain.com/revocation.lst
````html
authentication.certificate.revocation.list.file=/opt/local/revovcation.lst]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````
