<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991591 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00044: Configuring data logging

- page_id: `137991591`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991591/Configuring+data+logging
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:50:52.370+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you want to log information about user actions in Web Application Platform, Magic Collaboration Studio responses, and other technical data like Web Application Platform errors, you have to configure logging as described below.

To configure logging

1. In the Java application server (on which Web Application Platform runs) home directory, go to the *shared* > *conf* folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to <tomcat_home>\shared\conf .
2. Using a plain text editor create the logback.xml file in that directory.
3. Copy and paste the following text to the *logback.xml* file: truewebapp
 

 %d{HH:mm:ss.SSS} [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 true
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/web-app.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/web-app.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 true
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/twc_rest.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/twc_rest.%d{yyyy-MM-dd}.%i.log
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/admin-console.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/admin-console.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 true
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/collaborator.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/collaborator.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/document-exporter.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/document-exporter.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 true
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/resources.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/resources.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/resource-usage-map.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/resource-usage-map.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/reports.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/reports.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 true
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/simulation.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/simulation.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n

 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/authentication.log
 ${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/authentication.%i.log.zip
 1
 10
 100MB
 %date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n]]> Described appendersThe *logback.xml* file describes three appenders between the <appender> tags. The STDOUT appender is used when logging data to the console. The WEBAPPPLATORM_FILE and TWC_RESPONSE_FILE appenders are used when logging data to the *webappplatform.log* and *twc_response.log* files.
4. Specify the following properties for the WEBAPPPLATORM_FILE and TWC_RESPONSE_FILE appenders:
  - Full paths to the log files (between the <file> tags). To specify a path, replace the <tomcat_home> placeholder in the path with the home directory of the Java application server that Web Application Platform runs on.
  - File name patterns of the log files (between the <fileNamePattern> tags). You can leave the default file name pattern values.
  - Maximum log file sizes (between the <maxFileSize> tags. You can leave the default maximum file size values.
  - Maximum history in days (between the <maxHistory> tags). You can leave the default maximum history values.
  - Total size capacity (between the <totalSizeCap> tags). You can leave the default total size capacity values.
5. Specify one of the following logging levels for the TWC_RESPONSE_LOGGER and "com.nomagic.webappplatform" loggers or leave the default value "debug": Specifying logging levelsThe*logback.xml*file has three loggers. TheTWC_RESPONSE_LOGGER logs data to the *twc_response.log file*, the "com.nomagic.webappplatform" logger logs data the*webappplatform.log* file, and the "com.nomagic.webappplatform.collaborator.licensing.LicensingService" logger logs data to the console.You can change any of the levels listed above for each logger. By default, all logging levels are "debug".
  - all - designates events of all the levels including custom levels.
  - debug - designates fine-grained informational events that are most useful to debug an application.
  - error - designates error events that might still allow the application to continue running.
  - fatal - designates very severe error events that will presumably lead the application to abort.
  - info - designates informational messages that highlight the progress of the application at coarse-grained level.
  - trace - designates finer-grained informational events than "debug".
  - warn - designates potentially harmful situations.
  - off - turns logging of.
6. Save and close the logback.xml file.
7. Restart the Java application server.

##### How to turn off logging

You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.

To turn off logging

1. Locate the logger the logging of which you want to turn off.
2. Change the logging level of that logger to "off".
3. Restart the Java application server.

#### NOTE: Turning logging back on

Turning logging back on

To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from "off" to the logging level you want to use. Available logging levels are described in step 5 of the above section.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you want to log information about user actions in Web Application Platform, Magic Collaboration Studio responses, and other technical data like Web Application Platform errors, you have to configure logging as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To configure logging</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Java application server (on which Web Application Platform runs) home directory, go to the <em>shared</em> &gt; <em>conf</em> folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to </span><em>&lt;tomcat_home&gt;\shared\conf</em>.</li><li>Using a plain text editor create the <em>logback.xml</em> file in that directory.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy and paste the following text to the <em>logback.xml</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cade4c65-0afd-4a09-88ba-999971549f8b"><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<configuration debug="false">

	<contextName>webapp</contextName>
	<property name="WEBAPP_LOG_BASE" value="${catalina.base}" />

	<appender name="CONSOLE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.ConsoleAppender">
    	<encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
        	<Pattern>%d{HH:mm:ss.SSS} [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</Pattern>
    	</encoder>
	</appender>

	<appender name="WEB-APP-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
        <append>true</append>
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/web-app.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
  			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/web-app.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
  			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
  			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
  			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
		<encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
          <pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
  	</appender>

	<appender name="TWC_RESPONSE_FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
		<append>true</append>
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/twc_rest.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/twc_rest.%d{yyyy-MM-dd}.%i.log</fileNamePattern>
			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
		<encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
			<pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
	</appender>

	<appender name="ADMIN-CONSOLE-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/admin-console.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
  			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/admin-console.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
  			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
  			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
  			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
        <encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
            <pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
  	</appender>

	<appender name="COLLABORATOR-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
        <append>true</append>
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/collaborator.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
  			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/collaborator.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
  			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
  			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
  			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
        <encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
            <pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
  	</appender>

	<appender name="DOCUMENT-EXPORTER-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/document-exporter.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/document-exporter.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
		<encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
			<pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
	</appender>

	<appender name="RESOURCES-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
        <append>true</append>
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/resources.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
  			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/resources.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
  			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
  			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
  			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
        <encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
            <pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
  	</appender>

	<appender name="RESOURCE-USAGE-MAP-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/resource-usage-map.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/resource-usage-map.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
		<encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
			<pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
	</appender>

	<appender name="REPORTS-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/reports.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/reports.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
		<encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
			<pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
	</appender>

	<appender name="SIMULATION-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
		<append>true</append>
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/simulation.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/simulation.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
		<encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
			<pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
	</appender>

	<appender name="AUTHENTICATION-FILE" class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.RollingFileAppender">
		<file>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/authentication.log</file>
		<rollingPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.FixedWindowRollingPolicy">
			<fileNamePattern>${WEBAPP_LOG_BASE}/logs/webappplatform/authentication.%i.log.zip</fileNamePattern>
			<minIndex>1</minIndex>
			<maxIndex>10</maxIndex>
		</rollingPolicy>
		<triggeringPolicy class="ch.qos.logback.core.rolling.SizeBasedTriggeringPolicy">
			<maxFileSize>100MB</maxFileSize>
		</triggeringPolicy>
		<encoder class="ch.qos.logback.classic.encoder.PatternLayoutEncoder">
			<pattern>%date [%thread] %-5level %logger - %msg%n</pattern>
		</encoder>
	</appender>

	<root level="WARN">
		<appender-ref ref="CONSOLE"/>
	</root>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.webappplatform" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="WEB-APP-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.authentication" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="WEB-APP-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.webappplatform.adminconsole" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="ADMIN-CONSOLE-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.webappplatform.resourcemanager" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="RESOURCES-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.webappplatform.collaborator" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="COLLABORATOR-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.webappplatform.export" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="DOCUMENT-EXPORTER-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.webappplatform.resourceusagemap" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="RESOURCE-USAGE-MAP-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.webappplatform.reports" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="REPORTS-FILE"/>
	</logger>

    <logger name="com.nomagic.webappplatform.simulation" level="INFO" additivity="false">
        <appender-ref ref="SIMULATION-FILE"/>
    </logger>

	<logger name="com.nomagic.authentication.server" level="INFO" additivity="false">
		<appender-ref ref="AUTHENTICATION-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="org.springframework" level="OFF">
		<appender-ref ref="WEB-APP-FILE"/>
	</logger>

	<logger name="TWC_REST_LOGGER" level="OFF">
		<appender-ref ref="TWC_RESPONSE_FILE" />
	</logger>

</configuration>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e84133eb-238d-4186-b54a-660a5df5b35b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Described appenders</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <em>logback.xml</em> file describes three appenders between the &lt;appender&gt; tags. The STDOUT appender is used when logging data to the console. The WEBAPPPLATORM_FILE and TWC_RESPONSE_FILE appenders are used when logging data to the <em>webappplatform.log</em> and <em>twc_response.log</em> files.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Specify the following properties for the WEBAPPPLATORM_FILE and TWC_RESPONSE_FILE appenders:<br /><ul><li>Full paths to the log files (between the &lt;file&gt; tags). To specify a path, replace the &lt;tomcat_home&gt; placeholder in the path with the home directory of the Java application server that Web Application Platform runs on.</li><li>File name patterns of the log files (between the &lt;fileNamePattern&gt; tags). You can leave the default file name pattern values.</li><li>Maximum log file sizes (between the &lt;maxFileSize&gt; tags. You can leave the default maximum file size values.</li><li>Maximum history in days (between the &lt;maxHistory&gt; tags). You can leave the default maximum history values.</li><li>Total size capacity (between the &lt;totalSizeCap&gt; tags). You can leave the default total size capacity values.</li></ul></li><li>Specify one of the following logging levels for the TWC_RESPONSE_LOGGER and &quot;com.nomagic.webappplatform&quot; loggers or leave the default value &quot;debug&quot;:<br /><ul><li><p>all - designates events of all the levels including custom levels.</p></li><li><p>debug - designates fine-grained informational events that are most useful to debug an application.</p></li><li><p>error - designates error events that might still allow the application to continue running.</p></li><li><p>fatal - designates very severe error events that will presumably lead the application to abort.</p></li><li><p>info - designates informational messages that highlight the progress of the application at coarse-grained level.</p></li><li><p>trace - designates finer-grained informational events than &quot;debug&quot;.</p></li><li><p> warn - designates potentially harmful situations.</p></li><li><p>off - turns logging of.<br /><br /></p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e68c30b-47fa-426b-a2f2-482f5b291155"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Specifying logging levels</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The </span><em>logback.xml</em><span> file has three loggers. The </span>TWC_RESPONSE_LOGGER logs data to the <em>twc_response.log file</em>, the &quot;com.nomagic.webappplatform&quot; logger logs data <span>the </span><em>webappplatform.log</em> file, and the &quot;com.nomagic.webappplatform.collaborator.licensing.LicensingService&quot; logger logs data to the console.</p><p>You can change any of the levels listed above for each logger. By default, all logging levels are &quot;debug&quot;.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Save and close the <em>logback.xml</em> file.</li><li>Restart the Java application server.</li></ol><h3><br />How to turn off logging</h3><p>You can disable data logging to a specific file by changing the logging level of the related logger.</p><p><br /></p><p>To turn off logging</p><hr /><ol><li>Locate the logger the logging of which you want to turn off.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Change the logging level of that logger to &quot;off&quot;.</p></li><li>Restart the Java application server.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5eea956c-4dba-4676-ba50-11ebe8d5b40e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Turning logging back on</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To turn on the data logging that has been disabled, change the logging level of the related logger from &quot;off&quot; to the logging level you want to use. Available logging levels are described in step 5 of the above section.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991582 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00045: Configuring email notifications for services

- page_id: `137991582`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991582/Configuring+email+notifications+for+services
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:45:11.639+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform services can generate email notifications and send them to a remote email system. To enable email notifications, configure Web Application Platform services, as described below.

To enable email notifications

1. Go to the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory.
2. Open for editing the *webappplatform.properties* file. In the *webappplatform.properties* file:The values of some properties are placeholders, e.g., **mail.from.default=info@company.com**. Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.
3. Specify the properties under the *Platform mailing engine properties used to send notifications via e-mail* property group.
4. If the email server uses SSL or TLS, specify the properties under the Mailing engine TLS secure properties property group. Otherwise, go to the next step.
5. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform services can generate email notifications and send them to a remote email system. To enable email notifications, configure Web Application Platform services, as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable email notifications</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf</em> directory.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open for editing the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e24de815-3242-4ef4-8043-eedd63e07e43"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The values of some properties are placeholders, e.g., <strong>mail.from.default=info@company.com</strong>. Make sure to replace the placeholders with actual values.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Read the comments with property descriptions to ensure accuracy when specifying property values.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the properties under the <em>Platform mailing engine properties used to send notifications via e-mail</em> property group.</p></li><li>If the email server uses SSL or TLS, specify the properties under the <em>Mailing engine TLS secure properties</em> property group. Otherwise, go to the next step.</li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991450 space=MCS2024x version=5 -->
## PAGE 00046: Configuring Java heap space

- page_id: `137991450`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991450/Configuring+Java+heap+space
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:46:38.186+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Configuration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

42

This page provides instructions for changing the Java heap space (Xmx) for both Cassandra and Magic Collaboration Studio on Linux and Windows, respectively.

##### Configuring Java heap space for Cassandra

###### On Linux

To configure Java heap space for Cassandra on Linux, you must set the variables **MAX_HEAP_SIZE** and**HEAP_NEWSIZE**which are commented by default in the file **cassandra-env.sh**. The location of the file **cassandra-env.sh**depends on the type of Cassandra installation.

- Package installation: /etc/cassandra/conf/cassandra-env.sh
- Tarball installation: <Cassandra_install_folder>/conf/cassandra-env.sh

Uncomment **MAX_HEAP_SIZE**, **HEAP_NEWSIZE** variables and adjust **MAX_HEAP_SIZE** manually. Otherwise, Cassandra will calculate the heap size based on the system's memory using the following formula:

```text

```

The formula details are outlined below:

- For the system memory 0-2 GB: use half of it.
- For the system memory 2-4 GB: use 1 GB.
- For the system memory 4-32 GB: use a quarter of it.
- For the system memory more than 32 GB: use 8 GB.

###### On Windows

We recommend setting the **min (-Xms)** and **max (-Xmx)** heap to the same value in the Cassandra document to avoid pausing the GC during resizing.

To change the value using the Registry Editor

1. Run regedit from the command line. 
2. Browse for the key *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\cassandra\Parameters\Java*. 
3. Right-click **Options** and select **Modify...**. You can then change the min and max heap size from **-Xms** and **-Xmx**.

##### Configuring Java heap space for Magic Collaboration Studio

###### On Linux

- If you start Magic Collaboration Studio from the executable file twcloud , you can configure the -Xmx parameter from the file jvm.options (located in the same folder).

###### On Windows

- If you start Magic Collaboration Studio from the executable file twcloud.exe , you can configure the -Xmx parameter from the file jvm.options (located in the same folder).
- If you start Magic Collaboration Studio from a Windows service, you must use the Registry Editor to update the value.

To change the value using the Registry Editor

1. Run regedit from the command line. 
2. Browse for the key *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\Magic Collaboration Studio\Parameters\Java*. 
3. Right-click **JvmMx** and select **Modify...**. 
4. Select **Base to Decimal** and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).

##### Configuring Java heap space for Web Application Platform

Heap space for Web Application Platform is handled by Tomcat.

###### On Linux

- Heap settings are located in /opt/local/TeamworkCloud/WebAppPlatform/bin/setenv.sh JVM_OPTS="-server -XX:+UseParallelGC -Xms4096M -Xmx8192M"

###### On Windows

- The heap setting for Web Application Platform is located in the registry

To change the value using the Registry Editor

1. Run regedit from the command line. 
2. Browse for the key *HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java*. 
3. Right-click **JvmMx** and select **Modify...**. 
4. Select **Base to Decimal** and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d808253-a6a1-4daf-84e0-be9e0e0dfab9"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>This page provides instructions for changing the Java heap space (Xmx) for both Cassandra and Magic Collaboration Studio on Linux and Windows, respectively.</p><h3>Configuring Java heap space for Cassandra</h3><h4>On Linux</h4><p>To configure Java heap space for Cassandra on Linux, you must set the variables <strong>MAX_HEAP_SIZE</strong> and<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <strong>HEAP_NEWSIZE </strong></span>which are commented by default in the file <strong>cassandra-env.sh</strong>. The location of the file <strong>cassandra-env.sh </strong>depends on the type of Cassandra installation.</p><ul><li>Package installation: <em>/etc/cassandra/conf/cassandra-env.sh</em></li><li>Tarball installation: <em>&lt;Cassandra_install_folder&gt;/conf/cassandra-env.sh</em></li></ul><p>Uncomment <strong>MAX_HEAP_SIZE</strong>, <strong>HEAP_NEWSIZE</strong> variables and adjust <strong>MAX_HEAP_SIZE</strong> manually. Otherwise, Cassandra will calculate the heap size based on the system's memory using the following formula:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6a03598-1137-4ed2-ad6c-5a467ba0734b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Calculating Java heap size</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[max(min (1/2 ram), 1024MB), min(1/4 ram, 8GB))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The formula details are outlined below:</p><ul><li>For the system memory 0-2 GB: use half of it.</li><li>For the system memory 2-4 GB: use 1 GB.</li><li>For the system memory 4-32 GB: use a quarter of it.</li><li>For the system memory more than 32 GB: use 8 GB.</li></ul><h4>On Windows</h4><p>We recommend setting the <strong>min (-Xms)</strong> and <strong>max (-Xmx)</strong> heap to the same value in the Cassandra document to avoid pausing the GC during resizing.</p><p><br />To change the value using the Registry Editor</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Run regedit from the command line.<br />2. Browse for the key <em>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\cassandra\Parameters\Java</em>.<br />3. Right-click <strong>Options</strong> and select <strong>Modify...</strong>. You can then change the min and max heap size from <strong>-Xms</strong> and <strong>-Xmx</strong>. </p><h3>Configuring Java heap space for Magic Collaboration Studio</h3><h4>On Linux</h4><ul><li>If you start Magic Collaboration Studio from the executable file <strong>twcloud</strong>, you can configure the <strong>-Xmx</strong> parameter from the file <strong>jvm.options </strong>(located in the same folder).</li></ul><h4>On Windows</h4><ul><li>If you start Magic Collaboration Studio from the executable file <strong>twcloud.exe</strong>, you can configure the <strong>-Xmx</strong> parameter from the file <strong>jvm.options </strong>(located in the same folder).</li><li>If you start Magic Collaboration Studio from a Windows service, you must use the Registry Editor to update the value.</li></ul><p><br />To change the value using the Registry Editor</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Run regedit from the command line.<br />2. Browse for the key <em>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\Magic Collaboration Studio\Parameters\Java</em>.<br />3. Right-click <strong>JvmMx</strong> and select <strong>Modify...</strong>.<br />4. Select <strong>Base to Decimal</strong> and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).</p><h3>Configuring Java heap space for Web Application Platform</h3><p>Heap space for Web Application Platform is handled by Tomcat.</p><h4>On Linux</h4><ul><li>Heap settings are located in <em>/opt/local/TeamworkCloud/WebAppPlatform/bin/setenv.sh</em><br />JVM_OPTS=&quot;-server  -XX:+UseParallelGC  -Xms4096M -Xmx8192M&quot;</li></ul><h4>On Windows</h4><ul><li>The heap setting for Web Application Platform is located in the registry</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the value using the Registry Editor</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Run regedit from the command line.<br />2. Browse for the key <em>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java</em>.<br />3. Right-click <strong>JvmMx</strong> and select <strong>Modify...</strong>.<br />4. Select <strong>Base to Decimal</strong> and change the value. The value is in megabytes (mb).</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991590 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00047: Configuring memory usage

- page_id: `137991590`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991590/Configuring+memory+usage
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-02T16:27:37.997+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

For Web Application Platform to run smoothly, you should configure the memory usage for the Java application server the Web Application Platform runs on (e.g., Apache Tomcat). The sections below explain how to do that on different operating systems.

##### Configuring memory usage on Windows

If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Windows, configure memory usage by following one of the given workflows.

To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started by running an executable

1. In the Java application server home directory (on which Web Application Platform runs), go to the *bin* folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to <tomcat_home>\bin .
2. Using a plain text editor, create the setenv.bat file in that directory if it does not already exist.
3. Copy and paste the following line to the *setenv.bat* file: Memory usage valuesThe memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. Save and close the file.
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] .

To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started as a service

1. Stop the Web Application Platform if it is running.
2. Open the command-line interface and go to the Web Application Platform installation directory.
3. In the command-line interface, run the following command: .exe //US//WebApp --JvmMs=32000 --JvmMx=32000 ++JvmOptions='-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote=true']]> ImportantMake sure you enter the actual version number, instead of the placeholder, in the Apache Tomcat executable name.The memory usage values (JvmMs=32000 and JvmMx=32000) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''].

You can also change memory usage values in the registry. To do that, open the registry and go to *...Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java*. Then right-click the *Options* file and select **Modify**. In the open dialog, change memory usage values to -Xms32000m and -Xmx32000m.

##### Configuring memory usage on Linux

If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Linux, configure memory usage by following the steps outlined below.

To configure memory usage on Linux

1. In the Java application server (on which Web Application Platform runs)home directory, go to the*bin*folder. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to <tomcat_home>/bin .
2. Using a plain text editor, create the setenv.sh file in that directory if it does not already exist.
3. Copy and paste the following lines to the *setenv.sh* file: Memory usage valuesThe memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.
4. Save and close the file.
5. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86e20975-9a28-4aee-820b-16212d350eb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>For Web Application Platform to run smoothly, you should configure the memory usage for the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server</span> the Web Application Platform runs on (e.g., <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Apache Tomcat</span>). The sections below explain how to do that on different operating systems.</p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Configuring memory usage on Windows</h3><p>If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Windows, configure memory usage by following one of the given workflows.</p><p><br /></p><p>To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started by running an executable</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server <span>home directory</span> (on which Web Application Platform runs), go to the <em>bin</em> folder. For example, if you use <span>Apache Tomcat, go to </span></span><em>&lt;tomcat_home&gt;\bin</em>.</li><li>Using a plain text editor, create the <em>setenv.bat</em> file in that directory if it does not already exist.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy and paste the following line to the <em>setenv.bat</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfe54299-58bd-43d1-873d-f2abeba1da4e"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[set JAVA_OPTS=-Dfile.encoding=UTF-8 -Xms32G -Xmx32G]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="628e3463-1c92-43ed-9b2e-58cfd7f7b909"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Memory usage values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Save and close the file.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To configure memory usage when Web Application Platform is started as a service</span></p><hr style="" /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Stop the Web Application Platform if it is running.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Open the command-line interface and go to the Web Application Platform installation directory.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the command-line interface, run the following command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35224c3c-500a-4a71-961d-face327d14db"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[.\bin\tomcat<version>.exe //US//WebApp --JvmMs=32000 --JvmMx=32000 ++JvmOptions='-Dcom.sun.management.jmxremote=true']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="99097b00-b699-4350-ab39-3e067b9b8468"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Make sure you enter the actual version number, instead of the placeholder, in the Apache Tomcat executable name.</li><li>The memory usage values (JvmMs=32000 and JvmMx=32000) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. You do not have to change them.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Restart Web Application Platform]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p>You can also change memory usage values in the registry. To do that, open the registry and go to <em>...Apache Software Foundation\Procrun 2.0\WebApp\Parameters\Java</em>. Then right-click the <em>Options</em> file and select <strong>Modify</strong>. In the open dialog, change memory usage values to -Xms32000m and -Xmx32000m.<br /><br /></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Configuring memory usage on Linux</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If your instance of the Web Application Platform is running on Linux, configure memory usage by following the steps outlined below.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To configure memory usage on Linux</span></p><hr style="" /><ol><li>In the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Java application server <span>(on which Web Application Platform runs) </span>home directory, <span>go to the </span><em>bin</em><span> folder</span>. For example, if you use Apache Tomcat, go to </span><em>&lt;tomcat_home&gt;/bin</em>.</li><li>Using a plain text editor, create the <em>setenv.sh</em> file in that directory if it does not already exist.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Copy and paste the following lines to the <em>setenv.sh</em> file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a56ce7ef-6c14-4709-aa21-88aa98e3e9fa"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Xms32G"
export CATALINA_OPTS="$CATALINA_OPTS -Xmx32G"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b0ccbed-452d-4f51-ac7b-804ac3680242"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Memory usage values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The memory usage values (Xms32G and Xmx32G) are recommended values for Web Application Platform. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991580 space=MCS2024x version=6 -->
## PAGE 00048: Configuring or changing Administrator user password

- page_id: `137991580`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991580/Configuring+or+changing+Administrator+user+password
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:44:43.680+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After installing the Web Application Platform, you may need to configure the Administrator user password.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

It is recommended to set up and use the default Administrator user. However, you can also [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating users and user groups' space=''] in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Users application' space=''] of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Admin web applications' space=''].

To set up or change the Administrator user password

1. Open the Users application and select the Administrator user. If you previously used Teamwork Cloud with a custom Administrator password and have only recently installed Web Application Platform, skip this step and proceed to step 5
2. In the **User** pane, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select **Change password**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **Change password** pane, enter the current Administrator password, then enter and confirm the new password in the appropriate boxes.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
5. Use the console of the machine where the server is deployed to open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/ *webappplatform.properties* file.
6. Change the twc.admin.password property value to the same password you set in the Users application.
7. Restart Web Application Platform (webapp service).

However, you can also <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating users and user groups" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a new user]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991589 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00049: Configuring Web Application Platform to work with Magic Collaboration Studio via TLS

- page_id: `137991589`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991589/Configuring+Web+Application+Platform+to+work+with+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+via+TLS
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-30T10:32:34.154+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Web Application Platform Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If a secureconnection between the Magic Collaboration Studio server and the client is enabled using the TLS protocol, you must configure Web Application Platform as described below.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

Before configuring Web Application Platform, make sure that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling secure connection between client and server' space=''].

To configure Web Application Platform to work with Magic Collaboration Studio via TLS

1. Go to the <wap_installation_directory>\apache-tomcat\shared\conf directory and find the webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the *webappplatform.properties* file, uncomment the following properties and specify their values as shown below. Replace the placeholders between (<...>) with the actual values.The **twc.client.keystore.password** property is only required if a keystore is password-protected. twc.client.keystore.path=twc.client.keystore.password=]]>
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If a secure</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> connection between the Magic Collaboration Studio server and the client is enabled using the TLS protocol, you must configure Web Application Platform as described below.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b2f9679-a9d7-4821-850a-d34d420e710e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before configuring Web Application Platform, make sure that <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enabling secure connection between client and server" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[a secure connection between Magic Collaboration Studio and the client is enabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To configure Web Application Platform to work with Magic Collaboration Studio via TLS</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>&lt;wap_installation_directory&gt;\apache-tomcat\shared\conf</em> directory and find the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>webappplatform.properties</em> file, uncomment the following properties and specify their values as shown below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5491380-e569-4a08-afbf-ab41fff48a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Replace the placeholders between (&lt;...&gt;) with the actual values.</li><li>The <strong>twc.client.keystore.password</strong> property is only required if a keystore is password-protected.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="073d37d7-5ff7-45e8-8539-b8c1101f26c0"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[twc.client.tls=true
twc.client.keystore.type=<keystore_type>
twc.client.keystore.path=<full_path_to_keystore_file>
twc.client.keystore.password=<keystore_password>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Restart Web Application Platform.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992421 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00050: Content pane

- page_id: `137992421`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992421/Content+pane
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:23:57.973+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: ['navigate-to-magicdraw', 'open-in-magicdraw', 'open-in-model-editor', 'zoom-in', 'zoom-out', 'hyperlinks', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

The content pane displays the standard properties, relations, and/or visual representation of the item selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space='']. If you select a diagram that has graphical comments, those comments are displayed on the content pane as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The content pane of a published model portal.

##### Changing image size

On the content pane, images are displayed in their actual size. If an image is larger than the content pane, you can scroll to the desired part by clicking the image and dragging it. You can also zoom images in and out, as described below.

To zoom an image in or out

- If you want to zoom an image in, click [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_in.png'] on the top right corner of the image.
- If you want to zoom an image out, click [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_out.png'] on the top right corner of the image.

The number between the zoom in and zoom out buttons shows the percentage of the actual image size.

##### Navigating from items with hyperlinks

Some items displayed on the content pane have hyperlinks that help you navigate in a published document. If you hover the mouse pointer over an item with a hyperlink, it changes to [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Clicking that item navigates to its representation in the document. If an Item is represented in several views of a document, clicking the item opens the menu, allowing you to choose the representation to which you want to navigate. For example, if you click a Block symbol in a diagram, you can navigate to the characteristics table of that Block or its representation in different diagrams, as displayed below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### If an item with a hyperlink is displayed in several views of a document, clicking it opens a menu with navigation options.

##### Opening items in a modeling tool

If you have a modeling tool installed on your computer, you can open an item in a modleing tool (e.g., MagicDraw), right from a published document.

To open an item in a modeling tool

1. On the content pane, find the Open in Model Editor property of the item you want to open.
2. Click the item name displayed as the property value.

Clicking an item name (as displayed below) opens the related model in a modeling tool, such as MagicDraw, and selects the item in the model browser. If you click a hyperlink to a diagram, the diagram is opened on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image shows the hyperlink to the Climate Control Block in a modeling tool.

You can also enable hyperlinks for paragraphs in a published document.

To enable paragraph hyperlinks to a modeling tool

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] in the app bar of a published model.
2. From the menu, select Show paragraph links to model editor .

When you enable paragraph hyperlinks to a modeling tool, the hyperlink icon is displayed next to each paragraph of a document, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A paragraph with a hyperlink icon that navigates to the paragraph related element in a modeling tool.

Clicking a hyperlink icon opens the related model in a modeling tool and selects the paragraph related model element in the model browser. If the related element is a diagram, the diagram is opened on the diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

If the related element is a diagram, the diagram is opened on the diagram pane.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992600 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00051: Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents

- page_id: `137992600`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992600/Contextualizing+Cameo+Collaborator+documents
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:50:38.939+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a number of functionalities allowing you to contextualize Cameo Collaborator documents. You can create new sections anywhere in a document, add paragraphs to these sections, and fill them with content, like explanatory texts and images. This allows you to enrich the published data so it can be understood by stakeholders or customers unfamiliar with modeling languages. In addition, you can manage new sections and paragraphs with just a few clicks.

To learn more, refer to the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud offers a number of functionalities allowing you to contextualize Cameo Collaborator documents. You can create new sections anywhere in a document, add paragraphs to these sections, and fill them with content, like explanatory texts and images. This allows you to enrich the published data so it can be understood by stakeholders or customers unfamiliar with modeling languages. In addition, you can manage new sections and paragraphs with just a few clicks.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To learn more, refer to the following topics:</p><p style="text-align: left;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4c9cf6b9-ef76-4640-8052-31459362d5b3" /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991852 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00052: Converting users to internal or external

- page_id: `137991852`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991852/Converting+users+to+internal+or+external
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-01-05T13:16:39.821+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two types of users in Magic Collaboration Studio: internal and external. Internal users are created inside the system, whereas external users are imported from LDAP servers. An authorized user can convert an external user to an internal one and vice versa. When you convert a user from internal to external, you can select the LDAP domain this user will be mapped to.

To convert a user to internal

1. In the User application, do one of the following:
  - Click the external user you want to convert. In the open User pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Convert to Internal. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the name of the external user you want to convert and select Convert to Internal .
2. When the Convert to Internal pane opens, enter and confirm a new password for the user. You can also modify the full username if needed.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_roles.png'] to convert the user.

To convert a user to external

1. In the User application, do one of the following:
  - Click the internal user you want to convert. In the open User pane, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Convert to External.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the name of the internal user you want to convert and select Convert to External .
2. When a question dialog (displayed below) opens, select the LDAP domain you want to map the user to and click Convert . [ATTACHMENT filename='converting_users_to_external.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4c6db82d-1601-4ee8-8fae-1bc6be16587e">There are two types</ac:inline-comment-marker> of users in Magic Collaboration Studio: internal and external. Internal users are created inside the system, whereas external users are imported from LDAP servers. An authorized user can convert an external user to an internal one and vice versa. When you convert a user from internal to external, you can select the LDAP domain this user will be mapped to.</p><p><br /></p><p>To convert a user to internal</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>User </strong>application, do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the external user you want to convert. In the open <strong>User </strong>pane, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Convert to Internal.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_users_to_internal.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></strong></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the name of the external user you want to convert and select <strong>Convert to Internal</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>When the <strong>Convert to Internal</strong> pane opens, enter and confirm a new password for the user. You can also modify the full username if needed.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_roles.png" /></ac:image> to convert the user. </li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To convert a user to external</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>User </strong>application, do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the internal user you want to convert. In the open <strong>User </strong>pane, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Convert to External.</strong></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the name of the internal user you want to convert and select <strong>Convert to External</strong>. </li></ul></li><li>When a question dialog (displayed below) opens, select the LDAP domain you want to map the user to and click <strong>Convert</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_users_to_external.png" /></ac:image></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991808 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00053: Copying roles and assignments

- page_id: `137991808`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991808/Copying+roles+and+assignments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:50:58.992+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Managing users > Managing user roles and assignments
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you want to assign the same roles or assignments that belong to a user or a user group to another user or user group, you can quickly do so by copying them using the **User**pane. This section shows you how to copy user roles and assignments using the **Role Assignments** pane on the **User**pane.

To copy roles and/or assignment(s) from one user to another

1. On the User application, click the target user to open the User pane.
2. In the Roles card, click Change .
3. Select the Copy assignments tab.
4. In the search box, start typing the name of the user whose assignments you want to copy.
5. Select a username from the search result list. All the roles and assignments associated with the selected user will be automatically copied. [ATTACHMENT filename='copying_user_assignments.png']
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you want to assign the same roles or assignments that belong to a user or a user group to another user or user group, you can quickly do so by copying them using the <strong>User </strong>pane. This section shows you how to copy user roles and assignments using the <strong>Role Assignments</strong> pane on the <strong>User </strong>pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To copy roles and/or assignment(s) from one user to another</p><hr /><ol><li>On the <strong>User </strong>application, click the target user to open the <strong>User </strong>pane. </li><li>In the <strong>Roles</strong> card, click <strong>Change</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Copy assignments</strong> tab.<strong><br /></strong></li><li>In the search box, start typing the name of the user whose assignments you want to copy.</li><li>Select a username from the search result list. All the roles and assignments associated with the selected user will be automatically copied.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="copying_user_assignments.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Copying roles and assignments" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_roles.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Copying roles and assignments" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> to save the changes. </span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992331 space=MCS2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00054: Creating a custom template

- page_id: `137992331`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992331/Creating+a+custom+template
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2024-03-13T14:05:54.099+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with custom document templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Custom templates allow you to customize the appearance and content of a Cameo Collaborator document. You can specify which model elements are published, what element information is displayed, and the format in which it is displayed.Acustom template can be stored in one of two ways depending on how you intend to use it:

- In a specific model - use this method if you intend to use the template to publish only one specific model.
- Stored as a separate .mdzip file - use this method if you intend to apply the template to multiple models.

You can create a custom document template as described below.

To create a custom document template

1. Open your modeling tool, and create a new model. Creating a template in the model to be publishedIf you want tostore your document template in the model you intend to publish, skip the first step and create a dedicated Package for the template inside the model. Such a template can only be used to publish the model it is stored in.
2. [Use the *Document View Collaborator Profile*](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024x/Using+other+projects+in+a+project) in the model. Select it from the list in the *<install.root>\profiles* predefined location.
3. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborator View Diagram' space='']:
  1. Right-click the route Package of the model (or the Package dedicated for storing the template) and in the menu select Create Diagram .
  2. In the open dialog, click Collaborator View Diagram and enter the diagram name.
4. In the Collaborator View Diagram, create the Document element which is the root element of the template. The name of the Document is also the name of your custom template.
5. In the same diagram, create one or multiple View elements to represent different types of content.
6. Create Directed Composition relationships from the Document to each of the Views.
7. In the Collaborator View diagram, create the Viewpoint element for each of the Views created in step 5.
8. Create Conform relationships from each of the Views to its Viewpoint.
9. Create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram' space=''] for each Viewpoint element created in the previous step:
  1. In the Collaborator View Diagram (created in step 3), right-click the Viewpoint shape and in the menu select Create Diagram .
  2. In the open dialog, click Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram and enter the diagram name.
10. In each Collaborator Viewpoint Method Diagram, use different types of operators (Collect, Filter, and Sort) to define how model information should be collected and displayed in each view of the document.
11. Save the model. If you have created the template as a separate file, it is recommended to save it in the modelLibraries folder of the installation directory of your modeling tool or another location dedicated for storing custom templates.

#### TIP: Customizing the copy of a predefined template

Customizing the copy of a predefined template

Although you can create a completely new custom template, it is highly recommended that you modify the copy of a predefined template instead. It is a quicker method since you can reuse the elements that are already created, and easily remove the elements that you don't need.

However,**DO NOT** change a predefined template. If you want to customize a predefined template, first create a copy of this template with a different name, and then modify the newly created template file.

To create a copy of a predefined template for customization

1. Open the desired predefined template from the modelLibriaries folder located in the installation directory of your modeling tool.
2. Clear the **Standard/System Profile** checkbox in the **Project Properties** dialog since it is not recommended to change system files:
  1. In the main menu, go to File > Project Properties .
  2. In the bottom left corner of the Project Properties dialog, click the More button.
  3. In the Shared Packages tab of the dialog, clear the Standard/System Profile check box.
  4. Click OK to close the dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_properties dialog.png']
3. In the main menu, select **File** >**Save Project As**, enter a new name for your template, and click the **Save** button.

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates
- [ATTACHMENT filename='Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip']
