<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992191 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: Creating a webhook

- page_id: `137992191`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992191/Creating+a+webhook
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:51:13.973+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Working with webhooks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create two types of webhooks – resource-scoped or model element-scoped. Resource-scoped webhooks listen for resource-commit events. Webhooks that are element-scoped listen for model element commit events.

Tagged commit events, i.e., events when a resource is committed with specific commit tags, are only available for resource-scoped webhooks.

##### Creating resource-scoped webhooks

To create a resource-scoped webhook

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Webhooks** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a webhook.
3. In the first step of the wizard, type in the **Webhook title**and **Webhook** **URL**.
4. Select **Resource**as the **Webhook scope**. With this webhook scope, you can also select **Commit**or **Tagged commit**event. For the option of **Tagged commit**, enter the commit tags to listen for: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. For the webhook to work, keep the option **Enable** turned on.
6. You can also choose to **Protect webhook URL with basic authentication.**If you choose this option, enter the username and password: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **Next**.
8. In the second step of the wizard, select the **Resource scope**of the webhook. You can select either the **Resource**or **Category**option and search for a resource or a category in the search field: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Once you select the resources or categories, click **Create**to create your webhook .

##### Creating model element-scoped webhooks

To create a model element-scoped webhook

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Webhooks** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a webhook.
3. In the first step of the wizard, type in the **Webhook title**and **Webhook** **URL**.
4. Select **Model element**as the **Webhook scope.** Note that **Tagged commit**is not available for this option: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. 721pxFor the webhook to work, keep the option **Enable** turned on.
6. You can also choose to **Protect webhook URL with basic authentication.**If you choose this option, enter the username and password: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click **Next**.
8. In the second step of the wizard, select the **Resource scope**of the webhook. You can select either the **Resource**or **Category**option and search for a resource or a category in the search field: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. Click **Next**.
10. In the next step select the **Element scope.**You can also check the **Select recursively**box if you want to include the element and its child elements into the webhook scope. If **Select recursively**is left unchecked, only the selected element will be included in the webhook scope: 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
11. After making your selections, click **Create**to finish creating your new webhook.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create two types of webhooks – resource-scoped or model element-scoped. Resource-scoped webhooks listen for resource-commit events. Webhooks that are element-scoped listen for model element commit events.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9485d426-80a4-47ba-929b-f7272e1bb022"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Tagged commit events, i.e., events when a resource is committed with specific commit tags, are only available for resource-scoped webhooks.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating resource-scoped webhooks</h3><p>To create a resource-scoped webhook</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the <strong>Settings </strong>application and select <strong>Webhooks</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the wizard for creating a webhook.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the first step of the wizard, type in the <strong>Webhook title </strong>and <strong>Webhook</strong> <strong>URL</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Resource </strong>as the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Webhook scope</strong>. With this webhook scope, you can also select </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Commit </strong>or <strong>Tagged commit </strong>event.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the option of <strong>Tagged commit</strong>, enter the commit tags to listen for:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_webhook_creation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">For the webhook to work, keep the option <strong>Enable</strong> turned on.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also choose to <strong>Protect webhook URL with basic authentication. </strong>If you choose this option, enter the username and password:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="578"><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_url_username_and_password.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the second step of the wizard, select the <strong>Resource scope </strong>of the webhook. You can select either the <strong>Resource </strong>or <strong>Category </strong>option and search for a resource or a category in the search field:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="239" ac:width="766"><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_resource_select.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Once you select the resources or categories, click <strong>Create </strong>to create your webhook</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.</span></li></ol><h3>Creating model element-scoped webhooks</h3><p>To create a model element-scoped webhook</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the <strong>Settings </strong>application and select <strong>Webhooks</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the wizard for creating a webhook.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the first step of the wizard, type in the <strong>Webhook title </strong>and <strong>Webhook</strong> <strong>URL</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select <strong>Model element </strong>as the </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Webhook scope.<br /></strong></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Note that <strong>Tagged commit </strong>is not available for this option:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="578"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_element_scoped_webhook.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">721pxFor the webhook to work, keep the option <strong>Enable</strong> turned on.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also choose to <strong>Protect webhook URL with basic authentication. </strong>If you choose this option, enter the username and password:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="578"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_element_webhook_authentication.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the second step of the wizard, select the <strong>Resource scope </strong>of the webhook. You can select either the <strong>Resource </strong>or <strong>Category </strong>option and search for a resource or a category in the search field:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_scope.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the next step select the <strong>Element scope.<br /></strong>You can also check the <strong>S<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">elect recursively </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">box if you want to include the element and its child elements into the webhook scope. If <strong>Select recursively </strong>is left un</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">checked</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">, only the selected element will be included in the webhook scope:<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="578"><ri:attachment ri:filename="webhook_element_scope.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating a webhook" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After making your selections, click <strong>Create </strong>to finish creating your new webhook.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992483 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00056: Creating and editing graphical comments

- page_id: `137992483`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992483/Creating+and+editing+graphical+comments
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-06T14:53:09.227+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: ['creating-comments', 'adding-comments', 'creating-graphical-comments', 'adding-graphical-comments', 'editing-comments', 'editing-graphical-comments', 'creating-a-graphical-comment', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

##### Creating graphical comments

You can create graphical comments for images in a published document, such as diagrams, maps, and matrices. Later, graphical comments can be as many times as necessary. Graphical comments allow you to draw on images using various shapes, as well as add text.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create graphical comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model.

To create a graphical comment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. On the content pane, select the image you want to comment on. The **Create graphical comment** screen opens.
4. on the top of the screen to draw as many different shapes as you want. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing textual informationTo edit the textual information of a graphical comment, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the shape palette. When the comment form opens, change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click **Done** to save the changes.
5. When you finish, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to cancel comment creation.

The graphical comment is created and displayed both on the content pane and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space='']. You can easily [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space=''] between graphical comments displayed on the content pane and comments displayed on the comments pane by clicking them.

##### Editing graphical comments

You can edit the drawn shapes and/or the textual information of a graphical comment.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create graphical comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model.

To edit the textual information of a graphical comment

1. In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit**. The **Edit comment** pane opens.
4. In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.
5. If needed, click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
6. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save your changes, or click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_comment.png'] to cancel comment editing.

To edit the drawing of a graphical comment

1. In the comments pane, find the comment you want to edit.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit drawing**. The **Edit graphical comment** screen opens.
4. on the top of the screen to draw new shapes or select and edit existing shapes, as shown below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. When you finish, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the screen to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to cancel comment editing.

##### Using the shape palette

When , use the shape palette on the top of the **Create graphical comment** screen to draw various shapes and even write text on a diagram. The following table describes all the icons found in the shape palette:

| Shape palette icon | Icon name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Color | Click the color icon to select one of the six available colors for the shapes you will draw. You can draw shapes using multiple colors in one graphical comment. You can also select a shape and change its color at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Text shape | Select the text shape icon, click any area of a diagram, and type the desired text. You can select and resize or move the text shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Line shape | Select the line shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a line. You can select and move the line shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Arrow shape | Select the arrow shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an arrow. You can select and move the arrow shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Freeform shape | Select the free form shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw the desired shape. You can select and resize or move the free form shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Rectangle shape | Select the rectangle shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw a rectangle. You can select and resize or move the rectangle shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Ellipse shape | Select the ellipse shape icon, then click and hold the mouse button to draw an ellipse. You can select and resize or move the ellipse shape at any time during the comment creating/editing process. |
|  | Select | Click this icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to move, resize or rotate it. |
|  | Delete | Click the delete icon and select any shape of a graphical comment to delete it. |
|  | Comment form | Click this icon to open the comment form, where you can change the comment title, text, and/or priority. Click Done to save the changes. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992473 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00057: Creating and editing textual comments

- page_id: `137992473`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992473/Creating+and+editing+textual+comments
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-02-24T13:23:53.725+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: ['create-a-textual-comment', 'creating-textual-comments', 'creating-comments', 'editing-comments', 'edit-comment', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

##### Creating textual comments

You can create textual comments for any element, including standard properties and relations. Textual comments can be as many times as necessary.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can create textual comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model

To create a textual comment

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to comment on. Commentable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer, as displayed below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. When the **New comment** pane opens on the right side of the document portal, enter the comment title and comment text in the appropriate boxes.
5. If needed, click the **High priority** switch on the bottom of the new comment pane to set the comment priority to high.
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner of the new comment pane to save the comment, or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to cancel comment creation.

The textual comment is created and displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space='']. The commented item in the content pane is highlighted in yellow. You can easily [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space=''], as shown below. If an item has more than one comment, clicking it opens the list of comments, allowing you to choose the comment you want to navigate to.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Navigating from comments to commented items and vice versa.

##### Editing textual comments

You can edit any textual comment displayed on the comments pane of a published model portal, as described below.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

You can edit textual comments if:

- Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.
- You have permission to create comments.
- You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing from graphical user interface' space=''] when publishing a model

To edit a textual comment

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of a comment and select **Edit**. Displaying comment related actionsIf you do not see the icons of comment-related actions, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of a comment.
2. When the Edit comment pane (displayed below) opens on the right side of the document portal, do one or more of the following: [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_coment_pane.png']
  1. Edit the comment title.
  2. Edit the comment text.
  3. Click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.

After completing the above steps the comment is updated and displayed on the comments pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Textual comments can be <ac:link ac:anchor="Editing textual comments"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[edited]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> as many times as necessary.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3e502d0-7654-4a01-8392-b68075c8e9b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create textual comments if:</p><ul><li>Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.</li><li>You have permission to create comments.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="commenting"><ri:page ri:content-title="Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Commenting is not <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_comment.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>On the content pane, select the item you want to comment on. Commentable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer, as displayed below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="commentable_item.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li class="confluence-link"><p>When the <strong>New comment</strong> pane opens on the right side of the document portal, enter the comment title and comment text in the appropriate boxes.</p></li><li class="confluence-link"><p>If needed, click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the new comment pane to set the comment priority to high.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the new comment pane to save the comment, or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png" /></ac:image> to cancel comment creation.</p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The textual comment is created and displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[comments pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The commented item in the content pane is highlighted in yellow. You can easily <ac:link ac:anchor="Navigating between comments and commented items"><ri:page ri:content-title="Comments pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[navigate between comments and commented items]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, as shown below. If an item has more than one comment, clicking it opens the list of comments, allowing you to choose the comment you want to navigate to.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigating_between_comments_and_items.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Navigating from comments to commented items and vice versa.</h6><h3><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Editing textual comments<br /></span></h3><p>You can edit any textual comment displayed on the comments pane of a published model portal, as described below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97a27db8-87a9-4a4a-b951-2bc811966dde"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can edit textual comments if:</p><ul><li>Cameo Collaborator is installed with a commercial license.</li><li>You have permission to create comments.</li><li>You are the author of a comment. Only users with the Administer Resources permission (or the Index Manager, Resources Manager, or Resource Synchronization Manager role) can edit all existing comments.</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="commenting"><ri:page ri:content-title="Publishing from graphical user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Commenting is not disabled]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when publishing a model</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br />To edit a textual comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of a comment and select <strong>Edit</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4e50668-f830-4140-9cf5-e4cc1eceaefc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Displaying comment related actions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not see the icons of comment-related actions, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom right corner of a comment.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>When the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane (displayed below) opens on the right side of the document portal, do one or more of the following:<ol><li>Edit the comment title.</li><li>Edit the comment text.</li><li>Click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to change the comment priority.</li></ol><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_coment_pane.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps the comment is updated and displayed on the comments pane.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992601 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00058: Creating and managing document sections

- page_id: `137992601`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992601/Creating+and+managing+document+sections
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:51:13.794+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

You can create sections anywhere in a Cameo Collaborator document and reorder them in the scope of the current document view. New sections can later be filled with any type of content, like paragraphs and images.

##### Creating document sections

Follow the steps below to create a new section in a Cameo Collaborator document.

To create a document section

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the Create section chip at the top of a section.
4. In the Create section pane on the right side of the screen, enter the section name in the appropriate box.
5. In the Create section pane, click the Place section drop-down box and select one of the following options:
  - Below - to create a section below the current section.
  - Above - to create a section above the current section.
  - Inner section - to c reate a subsection within the current section, positioned one level deeper in the navigation tree hierarchy.
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

The newly created section is empty and only has a name. Now you can fill it with content, like [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating paragraphs' space=''] and images.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can change a section's name the same way you edit other items in a Cameo Collaborator document. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating document sections.

##### Reordering document sections

You can reorder the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

Note that the position of a document section can only be changed within the scope of the current document view.

To change the position of a document section

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Manage sections .
2. In the **Manage sections** dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section whose position you what to change. The sections you can reorder are highlighted in the **Manage sections** dialog.
3. Drag the section to a new location, as shown in the figure below. If you want to move a section one level deeper in the document hierarchy, i.e., make it an inner section of another document section, drag and drop the section on top of another section.
4. Once you are happy with the new position of the document section, click the Save button on the bottom right corner of the window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Reordering document sections.

##### Deleting document sections

You can delete the sections created in a Cameo Collaborator document as described below.

To delete a document section

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] and select Manage sections .
2. In the **Manage sections** dialog, expand the document navigation tree and locate the section you want to delete. The sections you can delete are highlighted in the **Manage sections** dialog.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the section to delete it.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991594 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00059: Creating comment backup

- page_id: `137991594`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991594/Creating+comment+backup
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:50:52.845+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud Administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to create a comment backup for a specific document. You can save the backup as a plain text file for future use, e.g, system recovery.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

In order to create a comment backup and import comments from the backup file, the comments must be stored in a document (not in the project).

To create the comment backup for a Cameo Collaborator document

1. Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP GET request with the following URL and headers:
  - URL: https://<host>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/<uuid>/branch/<uuid>/comments/export. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS2024x'] you need to add to the URL. Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (<>) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.
  - Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS2024x'] .
2. Save the retrieved data as a plain text file.

The text file with retrieved data is your comment back up. You can use that backup to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document published from the same project.

To import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document from the backup file

1. Use an HTTP client to send an HTTP POST request with the following URL, headers, and body:
  - URL: https://<host>:<port>/collaborator/api/collaborator/document/<uuid>/branch/<uuid>/comments/import. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS2024x'] you need to add to the URL. Make sure that you replace the placeholders in the URL (between angle brackets (<>) with the actual data, e.g., Cameo Collaborator for TWC host, port, document, and branch IDs, etc.
  - Headers: Cookie, Connection, User-Agent. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating comment backup' space='MCS2024x'] .
  - Body: the data you retrieved from an HTTP GET request when exporting comments from the document. When you specify the body of the HTTP request, select the raw data type and JSON data format.
2. Reload the document to which you have imported comments.

After completing the above steps and reloading the document, the imported comments should be visible in the comments pane.

##### How to find document and branch IDs

When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to form a URL with document and branch IDs. The following steps explain how to find these IDs.

To find document and branch IDs

1. In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.
2. Select the "comments" HTTP request and locate Request URL in the request headers.
3. Find the target document and target branch IDs in the Request URL.

##### How to find HTTP request headers

When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to know the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent headers. The following steps explain how to find them.

To find Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers

1. In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.
2. Select the "comments" HTTP request.
3. Find the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The following steps explain how to find these IDs.</p><p><br /></p><p>To find document and branch IDs</p><hr /><ol><li>In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.</li><li>Select the &quot;comments&quot; HTTP request and locate Request URL in the request headers.</li><li>Find the target document and target branch IDs in the Request URL.</li></ol><h3><br />How to find HTTP request headers</h3><p>When creating an HTTP request to import comments to a Cameo Collaborator document, you need to know the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent headers. The following steps explain how to find them.</p><p><br /></p><p>To find Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers</p><hr /><ol><li>In a web browser, open a network monitor in developer tools and go to the document from which you want to export comments.</li><li>Select the &quot;comments&quot; HTTP request.</li><li>Find the Cookie, Connection, and User-Agent request headers.</li></ol><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992581 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00060: Creating element values

- page_id: `137992581`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992581/Creating+element+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:51:40.758+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing models in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you can create values for stereotype tags and textual properties right in a Cameo Collaborator document. This will simplify your workflow because you will no longer need a modeling tool or any workarounds to add new values to your model.

To create a value for a stereotype tag

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024x'].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to create a value for. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. On the bottom of the **Edit** pane, click the **Add new value** button.
4. When a new value card is added, enter the desired value.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a value for a stereotype tag in a Cameo Collaborator document.

To create a value for a textual property

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024x'].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to create a value for. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. When the **Edit** pane opens, enter the desired value in the value card.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating a value for a textual property in a Cameo Collaborator document.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992177 space=MCS2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00061: Creating indexing configurations

- page_id: `137992177`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992177/Creating+indexing+configurations
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-03-16T12:55:01.640+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To improve the efficiency of resource usage, you can index resources and monitor their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository. This allows you to see if modifying or deleting certain model elements impacts other models that use these elements.

This chapter explains how to index resources for their element usages within the selected scope of the Teamwork Cloud repository so that later you could query these usages through the modeling tool.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- To enable this functionality, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] . Elasticsearch allows you to index model element usage data and serve it efficiently when queried from a modeling tool.
- Only users with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User roles and permissions' space=''] can create indexing configurations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating indexing configurations.

To create indexing configurations

1. In the right-side menu of the Settings application, select **Indexing management**.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the screen as shown in the figure above.
3. In the open wizard, select the resource you want to create an indexing configuration for and click **Next**. NoteYou can index just one resource at a time. Note that it is possible to search for element usages of indexed resources only.
4. Select the scope to search for the element usages of the indexed resource, selected in step 3. Click **Next**. Note on ScopeThe scope defines the resources or categories in which the element usages will be searched for. You can select one or several resources or categories as the indexing scope. If you want to search for the indexed resource element usages in the whole repository, select the **Select all** check box at the top of the resource tree.Your chosen scope determines the speed of indexing and the required disk space. See more detailed information in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] section of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Global element usage indexing and search' space=''] page.
5. In the last step of the wizard, select one of the following indexing modes:
  - Automatic (default) - the resource is automatically indexed every time new project versions are committed in the indexing scope.
  - Manual - the resource is indexed only when indexing is initiated by an authorized user.
  - By specific commit tag - the resource is indexed only when new project versions are committed with a specified commit tag in the indexing scope. If you select this indexing mode, the Commit tag box appears where you need to specify the desired commit tag. [ATTACHMENT filename='by_specific_commit_tag.png']
6. Click the Create button.

The created indexing configuration automatically appears in the Indexing configurations list on the content pane. To refresh the indexing status, reload the web page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This allows you to see if modifying or deleting certain model elements impacts other models that use these elements.</span></p><p>This chapter explains how to <span See more detailed information in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Systemrequirements"><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[System requirements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> section of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Global element usage indexing and search" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the last step of the wizard, select one of the following indexing modes:<ul><li>Automatic (default) - the resource is automatically indexed every time new project versions are committed in the indexing scope.</li><li>Manual - the resource is indexed only when indexing is initiated by an authorized user.</li><li>By specific commit tag - the resource is indexed only when new project versions are committed with a specified commit tag in the indexing scope. If you select this indexing mode, the <strong>Commit tag</strong> box appears where you need to specify the desired commit tag.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="by_specific_commit_tag.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />The created indexing configuration automatically appears in the Indexing configurations list on the content pane. To refresh the indexing status, reload the web page.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992609 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00062: Creating paragraphs

- page_id: `137992609`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992609/Creating+paragraphs
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:51:54.557+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Contextualizing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

If a Cameo Collaborator document is published for editing, you can add new paragraphs to it with text, images, and hyperlinks to model elements or other document sections.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To create paragraphs in a Cameo Collaborator document:

- The document must be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing a model for editing' space=''] .
- You need the Read Resources permission or Resource Reviewer role for the document.
- You need the Read Resources and Edit Resources permission or Resource Contributor role for the project with read-write permissions for the project branch.

To create a paragraph

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the **Create paragraph** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to insert a paragraph. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the **Create paragraph** screen, enter the paragraph text. Text formattingUse the text formatting toolbar on the top of the **Create paragraph** screen to change the text font, size, color, alignment, etc. You can also use the toolbar to add hyperlinks, tables, and images to the paragraph. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_paragraph_content.png']
5. On the top right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] to save the paragraph.

##### Editing paragraphs

You can edit the content of a paragraph created in a Cameo Collaborator document the same way you edit textual element values. For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space=''].

##### Changing paragraph location

You can change the location of a paragraph created in Cameo Collaborator by cutting and pasting it in a new location.

To change a paragraph location

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space=''].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''].
3. Click the Cut icon under the paragraph whose location you want to change. [ATTACHMENT filename='cutting_pragraph.png']
4. Click the **Paste** icon in the location of an open document section where you want to move the paragraph. TipIf you want to move the paragraph from one document section to another, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space=''] to open a different section of the same document view and then paste the paragraph. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992165 space=MCS2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00063: Creating remote resource configurations

- page_id: `137992165`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992165/Creating+remote+resource+configurations
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2024-09-19T10:43:22.884+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

In Magic Collaboration Studio, you can create remote resource configurations allowing youto synchronize resources from one Magic Collaboration Studio server to another based on the selected synchronization trigger.This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. To learn how you can benefit from this functionality, see .

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Be aware that **cross-cluster resource synchronization** is only supported between deployments running the **same major version**. While the **minor version** does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.

- To create remote resource configurations, you must have the Remote Synchronization Manager role. The role scope needs to include the target Magic Collaboration Studio server categories where you want the synchronized resources to be saved.
- Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.
- Remote resource configurations are created and managed on the target Magic Collaboration Studio server.

To create a remote resource configuration

1. Go to the **Settings**application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. In the bottom right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the wizard for creating a remote resource configuration.
3. In the first step of the wizard, log in to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server:
  1. In the **Source Magic Collaboration Studio address** box, enter the address of the source server from which you want to synchronize resources or categories.
  2. In the **Username** and **Password** boxes, enter the credentials of a source server user. Required source server user permissionsFor successful synchronization, the user whose credentials are used to log in to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server needs to have the Read Resources permission in the source server.
4. Click **Next**.
5. Select the source server resources and/or categories you want to synchronize with the target Teamwork Could server (the current server) and click **Next**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Select the category in the target Magic Collaboration Studio server where you want the synchronized resources to be stored and click **Next**.
7. Select one of the following synchronization triggers: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Manual** - the resource or category is synchronized only when initiated by a user.
  - **On every new commit** - the resource or category is synchronized whenever a new resource version becomes available in the source server.
  - On specific commit tag - the resource or category is synchronized when a resource is committed to the source server with a specific commit tag.
8. Click **Create**.

A separate remote resource configuration is created for every resource and/or category selected in step 5. All existing remote resource configurations and their details including synchronization status are displayed in the remote repository management screen as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The remote repository management screen where you can create and manage remote resource configurations.

##### Synchronizing resources manually

After creating a remote resource configuration, the related resource or category is synchronized depending on the selected synchronization mode. However, you can also synchronize resources manually as described below.

Manual

To synchronize resources manually

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. Select the remote resource configurations of the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
3. In the top right corner of the screen, click [ATTACHMENT filename='synchronize.png'] .

##### Deleting remote resource configurations

If remote resource configurations are no longer needed, you can delete them as described below.

To delete a remote resource configuration

1. Go to the Settings application and select **Remote repository management** on the left side of the screen.
2. Select the remote resource configurations you want to delete.
3. In the top right corner of the screen, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
4. Optionally, run [Data Manager](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024x/Data+manager) and use one of the commands for deleting resources to completely remove the remote resource configurations from Magic Collaboration Studio. NoteStep 4 is mandatory if you want to create another remote resource configuration for the same resource. In such a case, it is not enough to delete the configuration you want to replace by using just the Magic Collaboration Studio UI. You need to remove it via Data Manager as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94d0dcd4-7774-4e7e-9cfb-51040c899fa8"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In Magic Collaboration Studio, you can create remote resource configurations allowing you </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">to synchronize resources from one Magic Collaboration Studio server to another based on the selected synchronization trigger. </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. To learn how you can benefit from this functionality, see <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross-cluster resource synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cde10e88-3be8-4dba-b27e-f63e0cadfdf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be aware that <strong>cross-cluster resource synchronization</strong> is only supported between deployments running the <strong>same major version</strong>. While the <strong>minor version</strong> does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b3f2fe0-c487-4409-9d94-1010b4ad64e3"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To create remote resource configurations, you must have the Remote Synchronization Manager role. The role scope needs to include the target Magic Collaboration Studio server categories where you want the synchronized resources to be saved.</li><li>Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.</li><li>Remote resource configurations are created and managed on the target Magic Collaboration Studio server.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a remote resource configuration</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the <strong>Settings </strong>application and select <strong>Remote repository management</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the bottom right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_index.png" /></ac:image> to open the wizard for creating a remote resource configuration.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the first step of the wizard, log in to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server:</span><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Source Magic Collaboration Studio address</strong> box, enter the address of the source server from which you want to synchronize resources or categories.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the <strong>Username</strong> and <strong>Password</strong> boxes, enter the credentials of a source server user.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1575fcd-054f-4e67-82f7-ea7d6bf9a1f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Required source server user permissions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For successful synchronization, the user whose credentials are used to log in to the source Magic Collaboration Studio server needs to have the Read Resources permission in the source server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the source server resources and/or categories you want to synchronize with the target Teamwork Could server (the current server) and click <strong>Next</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_resources.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the category in the target Magic Collaboration Studio server where you want the synchronized resources to be stored and click <strong>Next</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select one of the following synchronization triggers:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Manual</strong> - the resource or category is synchronized only when initiated by a user.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>On every new commit</strong> - the resource or category is synchronized whenever a new resource version becomes available in the source server.</span></li><li><strong>On specific commit tag</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> - the resource or category is synchronized when a resource is committed to the source server with a specific commit tag.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ul><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronization_mode.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click <strong>Create</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br />A separate remote resource configuration is created for every resource and/or category selected in step 5. All existing remote resource configurations and their details including synchronization status are displayed in the remote repository management screen as shown below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remote_repository_management.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The remote repository management screen where you can create and manage remote resource configurations.</span></h6><h3><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Synchronizing resources manually</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After creating a remote resource configuration, the related resource or category is synchronized depending on the selected synchronization mode. However, you can also synchronize resources manually as described below.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1b8b1e8-a5ac-433a-a480-c50585fe30ca"><ac:rich-text-body>If you select the <strong>Manual</strong> synchronization mode for a remote resource configuration, you need to synchronize resources by yourself as described in this section.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>To synchronize resources manually</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Remote repository management</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the remote resource configurations of the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.</span></li><li>In the top right corner of the screen, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synchronize.png" /></ac:image>.</li></ol><h3>Deleting remote resource configurations</h3><p>If remote resource configurations are no longer needed, you can delete them as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a remote resource configuration</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Go to the Settings application and select <strong>Remote repository management</strong> on the left side of the screen.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the remote resource configurations you want to delete.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the top right corner of the screen, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png" /></ac:image>.<br /></span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Optionally, run <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024x/Data+manager">Data Manager</a> and use one of the commands for deleting resources to completely remove the remote resource configurations from Magic Collaboration Studio.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="750e2775-4d27-4f60-aeab-ae691375e2c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Step 4 is mandatory if you want to create another remote resource configuration for the same resource. In such a case, it is not enough to delete the configuration you want to replace by using just the Magic Collaboration Studio UI. You need to remove it via Data Manager as well.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992291 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00064: Creating resource usage maps

- page_id: `137992291`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992291/Creating+resource+usage+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:51:21.111+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Resource Usage Map
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Usage Map application allows you to create usage maps for one, several or all the projects in the Magic Collaboration Studio server. In addition, you can have have multiple usage maps open simultaneously to compare the usages of different projects as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a resource usage map.

To create a resource usage map

1. In the Resource Usage Map application, click the Select icon. If there are other usage maps open already, the **Select** icon is located at the top left corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map application structure' space='MCS2024x'].
2. In the open dialog, select the resources whose usages you want to see in the resource usage map by doing one of the following:
  - To select one or several resources, start typing the resource name in the search box and select the desired resources from the search results.
  - To select all resources in one or several categories, select to search for categories as shown in the image below, start typing the category name in the search box, and select the desired categories form the search results.
  - To select all Teamwork Cloud projects, select the **Select all** check-box on the top of the dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the **Select** button.

After completing the above steps, the usage map of the selected resources is displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map application structure' space='MCS2024x'] of the web application portal. You can use the filters on the left side of the portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. Tonavigate through all existing usage maps, select a respective tab on the top of the content pane. You can alsoselect a resource in the map to view its details.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Resource Usage Map application allows you to create usage maps for one, several or all the projects in the Magic Collaboration Studio server. In addition, you can have have multiple usage maps open simultaneously to compare the usages of different projects as shown below.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_usage_map.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating resource usage maps" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">An example of a resource usage map.</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a resource usage map</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Resource Usage Map application, click the </span><strong style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> icon. If there are other usage maps open already, the <strong>Select</strong> icon is located at the top left corner of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Usage Map application structure" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the open dialog, select the resources whose usages you want to see in the resource usage map by doing one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To select one or several resources, start typing the resource name in the search box and select the desired resources from the search results.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To select all resources in one or several categories, select to search for categories as shown in the image below, start typing the category name in the search box, and select the desired categories form the search results.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To select all Teamwork Cloud projects, select the <strong>Select all</strong> check-box on the top of the dialog.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_resources_or_categories.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Creating resource usage maps" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click the <strong>Select</strong> button.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br />After completing the above steps, the usage map of the selected resources is displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Usage Map application structure" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the web application portal. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can use the filters on the left side of the portal to choose what types of usages should be displayed in the map. To </span>navigate through all existing usage maps, select a respective tab on the top of the content pane. You can also<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> select a resource in the map to view its details.</span></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991962 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00065: Creating roles

- page_id: `137991962`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991962/Creating+roles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-02T16:40:28.019+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Roles application > Managing roles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Role**application allows you to create a role and select the permissions you want the role to have. For example, if you have users who will be responsible for viewing resources and making changes, you can assign the Author role to them. This role is a custom role therefore, you can edit or delete it if need be.

To create a user role

1. On the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_role.png'] to open the Create role pane.
2. Type the name of a user role and d esc r i pt i on (opt i o n a l ) .
3. Se l ect at l east o ne pe rmi ssion t o assign t o th e ro l e .
4. Click the Save button.

You can assign the newly created role to one or more users/groups by clicking the **Change** button in the **Role assignments**card. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space='']** opens and you will be able to add users/groups.

To set a custom scope to the newly created role, you must add resource(s).
