<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991692 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00066: Creating users and user groups

- page_id: `137991692`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991692/Creating+users+and+user+groups
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:03:06.662+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a new user account

1. From the left side users/user groups filter select Users.
2. In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button [ATTACHMENT filename='Action_button.png'] and select a Create User button.
3. In the **Create User** pane, fill in the required fields. A text box with a red asterisk (*) means that the information is mandatory. Thus, the **Create** button will be enabled only if you fill in the **Username**, **Password,** and **Confirm password** text boxes.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner to save the user account.

#### TIP: Enable or disable user access

Enable or disable user access

When creating a new user or editing user information, you may select to either enable or disable their access. If you want the user to access the Magic Collaboration Studio system and carry out the tasks assigned, select **Enable** (selected by default). If you clear the **Enable**, the user's status will be disabled and thus he or she cannot log into Teamwork Cloud Admin and Magic Collaboration Studio in the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the user account is saved, the **User details**pane will be opened, here you can:

- Edit user details,
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space=''] ,
- Assign a user to a group.

To create new user groups

1. From the left users/user groups filter select **User groups**.
2. In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button [ATTACHMENT filename='Action_button.png'] and Select a Create user group button, and in Create user group pane fill in the required fields.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the top right corner to save the user group.

After the user group is saved, the **Group details**pane will be opened, here you can:

- Edit group details,
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing custom roles' space=''] ,
- Assign users to a group.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a new user account</p><hr /><ol><li>From the left side users/user groups filter select <strong>Users.</strong></li><li>In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="28"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Action_button.png" /></ac:image> and select a <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create User</strong> button.</li><li><p>In the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create User</strong> pane, fill in the required fields.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="19399d63-c91a-4667-be18-5b28313b1f0a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A text box with a red asterisk (<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);">*</span>) means that the information is mandatory. Thus, the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create</strong> button will be enabled only if you fill in the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Username</strong>, <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Password,</strong> and <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Confirm password</strong> text boxes. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_resource_details.png" /></ac:image></span> on the top right corner to save the user account. </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea1e4e0e-f781-4f83-8df0-7db7e11c838d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Enable or disable user access</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When creating a new user or editing user information, you may select to either enable or disable their access. If you want the user to access the Magic Collaboration Studio system and carry out the tasks assigned, select <strong>Enable</strong> (selected by default). If you clear the <strong>Enable</strong>, the user's status will be disabled and thus he or she cannot log into Teamwork Cloud Admin and Magic Collaboration Studio in the modeling tool. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add_user.png" /></ac:image></p><p>After the user account is saved, the <strong>User details </strong>pane will be opened, here you can: </p><ul><li>Edit user details,</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing custom roles" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Assign roles]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</li><li>Assign a user to a group.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To create new user groups</p><hr /><ol><li><p>From the left users/user groups filter select <strong>User groups</strong>.</p></li><li>In the lower right side of the Users application, click the action button <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="28"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Action_button.png" /></ac:image> and Select a <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create user group</strong> button, and in <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create user group</strong> pane fill in the required fields.</li><li><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save_resource_details.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner to save the user group. </p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>After the user group is saved, the <strong>Group details </strong>pane will be opened, here you can: </p><ul><li>Edit group details,</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing custom roles" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Assign roles]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,</li><li>Assign users to a group.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991572 space=MCS2024x version=8 -->
## PAGE 00067: Cross-cluster resource synchronization

- page_id: `137991572`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991572/Cross-cluster+resource+synchronization
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:44:01.088+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio administration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### WARNING: Important

Important

Be aware that **cross-cluster resource synchronization** is only supported between deployments running the **same major version**. While the **minor version** does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.

**On this page**

4

Teamwork Cloud offers a one-way resource synchronization functionality allowing you to move resources from one Teamwork Cloud server to another and periodically update them. This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. Therefore you can use resource synchronization as an alternative to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration between disconnected teams' space='MDTWRT'] and then updating server projects with local changes. In addition, you can perform a one-time synchronization operation to offload resources to another server for archiving.

- Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.
- The synchronization feature checks project uniqueness by Project ID, Resource ID, and Project Name.

##### Using resource synchronization for collaborating

You can periodically synchronize Teamwork Cloud resources from the source server to the target server so that other users can use synchronized projects in other projects stored on the target server.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting up a periodic synchronization operation to share resources that can be reused on the target server.

To use resource sync for collaboration between disconnected Teamwork Cloud sites

1. On the target Teamwork Cloud server, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating remote resource configurations' space='TWCloudTWRT'] to synchronize selected resources from the source to the target server:
  1. Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
  2. Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.
  3. Select one of the following synchronization triggers: on every commit, on commit with a specific tag, or manual synchronization
2. Use the synchronized resources in your projects stored on the target server.
3. Update synchronized resources when new project versions become available.

- The source server determines all of the resource's metadata. Therefore, if you change the name of a synchronized project on the source server, the name also changes on the target server on subsequent synchronization iteration. If you change the name of a synchronized project on the target server, the name changes back to the one on the source server during synchronization.
- If you move a synchronized project to a different category on the source server, the project continues synchronizing as before. If you do so on the target server, on subsequent synchronization iteration, the synchronized project returns to its original category specified in the synchronization configuration. However, if a whole category is being synchronized and you remove a resource from that category, this resource is not synchronized anymore. Only the remaining resources in the category are synchronized further.

###### Multi-hop synchronization

In the case of external collaboration between separate entities, direct network connectivity is usually not permitted between the two parties due to IT-level security constraints. As a remedy to this issue, the cross-cluster sync feature can be set up to support the so-called multi-hop deployment topology. That is, a separate Teamwork Cloud instance can be spun up in a neutral environment outside of the involved organizations' network, so the need to connect the two together is eliminated. Instead, resources can be shared and synced between the source and target in multiple hops through the common/neutral Teamwork Cloud repository in the middle.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing resources in multiple hops via a common or neutral environment.

##### Using resource synchronization for resource offloading

If there are dormant recourses in your Teamwork Cloud server, you can move them to another server for archiving to free up space on the main server (e.g., production server).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Setting up a one-time synchronization operation to move resources to another server.

To use resource sync for moving resources to another Teamwork Cloud server

1. On the target Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., archive server), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating remote resource configurations' space='TWCloudTWRT'] for a single time synchronization operation:
  1. Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., production server) and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.
  2. Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.
  3. Select one the **On every new commit** synchronization trigger.
2. Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Manager' space='TWCloudTWRT'] to remove the synced resources from the source server to free up server space.
3. Remove the remote resource configuration created in step 1.

###### Removing the read-only flag from offloaded resources

If you have used resource synchronization to offload resources to a remote server, these resources are read-only and you can no longer edit them. However, there is a way to remove the read-only flag from synchronized resources and make them active again.

Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.

The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.

To remove the read-only flag from offloaded resources

- Run [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Manager' space='TWCloudTWRT'] and execute the following command. Before running Data Manager, stop Magic Collaboration Studioservices to prevent users from accessing the database. bashDJangotrue,--categoryID=,>]]> Exampledatamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48

##### Configuring TLS encrypted connection for cross-cluster synchronization

Both source and target server must have the same values set in Webapp:

```text

```

It is not necessary to set the same port for TLS in Webapp. However, keep in mind that during login, you need to set a port when adding a resource. Otherwise, it will use the**target**server's TLS port to log in to the**source**server, where it has a different TLS port.

To enable encrypted synchronization in Teamwork Cloud, append the following parameter to the end of *application.conf*:

```text

```

Both Teamwork Cloud servers must have a certificate signed by a trusted Certificate Authority (CA). You can use self-signed certificates, but you may need to add the certificate to your JVM keystore and OS path as well*.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cde10e88-3be8-4dba-b27e-f63e0cadfdf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be aware that <strong>cross-cluster resource synchronization</strong> is only supported between deployments running the <strong>same major version</strong>. While the <strong>minor version</strong> does not need to match, both deployments must be on the same major version to ensure compatibility and smooth operation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f828e5c5-0195-4a40-b347-6a933a07f2f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Teamwork Cloud offers a one-way resource synchronization functionality allowing you to move resources from one Teamwork Cloud server to another and periodically update them. This is a convenient way to collaborate with disconnected teams or departments and share not only resources but their entire history as well. Therefore you can use resource synchronization as an alternative to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MDTWRT" ri:content-title="Collaboration between disconnected teams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exchanging .mdzip files]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and then updating server projects with local changes. In addition, you can perform a one-time synchronization operation to offload resources to another server for archiving.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b3f2fe0-c487-4409-9d94-1010b4ad64e3"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Cross-cluster resource synchronization works only in one direction - from the source to the target server. Synchronized resources on the target server are read-only and cannot be edited to ensure version consistency.</li><li>The synchronization feature checks project uniqueness by Project ID, Resource ID, and Project Name.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Using resource synchronization for collaborating</span></h3><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can periodically synchronize Teamwork Cloud resources from the source server to the target server so that other users can use synchronized projects in other projects stored on the target server.</span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:height="179" ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_sync_for_collaboration.png" /></ac:image></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Setting up a periodic synchronization operation to share resources that can be reused on the target server.</span></span></h6><p><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To use resource sync for collaboration between disconnected Teamwork Cloud sites</span></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">On the target Teamwork Cloud server, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloudTWRT" ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a remote resource configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to synchronize selected resources from the source to the target server:</span></span><ol><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.</span></span></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.</span></span></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select one of the following synchronization triggers: on every commit, on commit with a specific tag, or manual synchronization</span></span></li></ol></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use the synchronized resources in your projects stored on the target server.</span></span></li><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Update synchronized resources when new project versions become available.</span></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="56febc20-94db-4a00-981e-3a7d34d8b48e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The source server determines all of the resource's metadata. Therefore, if you change the name of a synchronized project on the source server, the name also changes on the target server on subsequent synchronization iteration. If you change the name of a synchronized project on the target server, the name changes back to the one on the source server during synchronization.</li><li>If you move a synchronized project to a different category on the source server, the project continues synchronizing as before. If you do so on the target server, on subsequent synchronization iteration, the synchronized project returns to its original category specified in the synchronization configuration.<br />However, if a whole category is being synchronized and you remove a resource from that category, this resource is not synchronized anymore. Only the remaining resources in the category are synchronized further.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Multi-hop synchronization</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the case of external collaboration between separate entities, direct network connectivity is usually not permitted between the two parties due to IT-level security constraints. As a remedy to this issue, the cross-cluster sync feature can be set up to support the so-called multi-hop deployment topology. That is, a separate Teamwork Cloud instance can be spun up in a neutral environment outside of the involved organizations' network, so the need to connect the two together is eliminated. Instead, resources can be shared and synced between the source and target in multiple hops through the common/neutral Teamwork Cloud repository in the middle.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:width="900"><ri:attachment ri:filename="twc_sync.png" /></ac:image></span></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing resources in multiple hops via a common or neutral environment.</h6><h3><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Using resource synchronization for resource offloading</span></span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If there are dormant recourses in your Teamwork Cloud server, you can move them to another server for archiving to free up space on the main server (e.g., production server).</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:height="175" ac:width="600"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_sync_for_project_archiving.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Setting up a one-time synchronization operation to move resources to another server.</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To use resource sync for moving resources to another Teamwork Cloud server</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">On the target Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., archive server), <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloudTWRT" ri:content-title="Creating remote resource configurations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a remote resource configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for a single time synchronization operation:</span><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Connect to the source Teamwork Cloud server (e.g., production server) and select the resources and/or categories you want to synchronize.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the category on the target Teamwork Cloud server where you want the synced resources to be stored.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select one the <strong>On every new commit</strong> synchronization trigger.</span></li></ol></li><li>Use <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloudTWRT" ri:content-title="Data Manager" /></ac:link> to remove the synced resources from the source server to free up server space.</li><li>Remove the remote resource configuration created in step 1.</li></ol><h4><br />Removing the read-only flag from offloaded resources</h4><p>If you have used resource synchronization to offload resources to a remote server, these resources are read-only and you can no longer edit them. However, there is a way to remove the read-only flag from synchronized resources and make them active again.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55e23054-2732-4c6e-85ef-3afcafee884e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.</p><p>The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To remove the read-only flag from offloaded resources</p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Run <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloudTWRT" ri:content-title="Data Manager" /></ac:link> and execute the following command.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dca1efc7-9114-43c6-80eb-c91fbd70f659"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Before running Data Manager, stop <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Magic Collaboration Studio </span>services to prevent users from accessing the database.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8cd2891-5915-4917-8e00-0f89f2567773"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-rrof, --remove-read-only-flag <-resourceID=<value1>,<value2> --categoryID=<value1>,<value2>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="867413c3-3f26-42e0-89de-ba871cd19a90"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>datamanager -rrof --resourceID=a4c498c9-f125-453c-b9cb-3d5a6e675714 --categoryID=a1d569ea-fe6a-402f-8a25-aef6b932de48</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Configuring TLS encrypted connection for cross-cluster synchronization</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Both source and target server must have the same values set in Webapp:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a05279d8-79e4-498b-b007-6019f2a91324"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[twc.client.keystore.type
twc.client.keystore.path
twc.client.keystore.password
twc.client.tls=true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">It is not necessary to set the same port for TLS in Webapp. However, keep in mind that during login, you need to set a port when adding a resource. Otherwise, it will use the </span><strong>target</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> server's TLS port to log in to the </span><strong>source</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> server, where it has a different TLS port.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To enable encrypted synchronization in Teamwork Cloud, append the following parameter to the end of <em>application.conf</em>:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efade764-2ec2-4e74-a43a-0389c346f98d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[esi.server.sync.tls-enabled = true]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Both Teamwork Cloud servers must have a certificate signed by a trusted Certificate Authority (CA). You can <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">use self-signed certificates, but you may need to add the certificate to your JVM keystore and OS path as well</span><em>.</em></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992351 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00068: Customizing the appearance of a project portal

- page_id: `137992351`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992351/Customizing+the+appearance+of+a+project+portal
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:08:59.437+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with custom document templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

You can customize the appearance of a published project by customizing the template you plan to use for publishing. The best practice is to save an existing predefined template with a different name, and then make changes to the newly created template file. After creating a new template file, you need to use it in the project you intend to publish. There is the option to change the default color of the title bar, document header and text, to add or remove the search button, and other style options. The procedure bellow describes how to modify the *Entire Model* template (stored as *Cameo Collaborator Library.mdzip)*, but you can also modify other predefined templates the same way.

To customize the appearance a project portal

1. Go to the modelLibraries folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and open the Cameo Collaborator template you want to use for publishing as a separate project.
2. Save the project in the same directory using a different name (e.g., Custom template.mdzip ).
3. When you receive the warning not to edit standard/system profiles and libraries, click Continue .
4. Open a View Diagram of the template and double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype to open its properties Specification window.
5. Specify the values of the following properties:
  - 
    - Primary Color Palette - defines the color of the app bar in the project portal.
    - Secondary Color Palette - defines the color of the main portal icons, e.g., icons for creating and saving a comment.
6. Click Close and save the project.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The sample of a project portal showing the colors that can be customized.

##### Setting the default scope for publishing

In the custom template, you can set the default scope for publishing. When the default scope is set, you don't need to specify it while publishing the project. In this case, the default scope is published even if you specify another scope in the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog.

To set the default scope for publishing

1. If you haven't done so already, use your custom template in the project you intend to publish.
2. Open the View Diagram of the custom template.
3. In the Containment tree, select the package you want to set as the default scope for publishing (e.g. Categorization ) and drag it onto the diagram pane.
4. Draw the «Expose» dependency from the element with the «Document» stereotype to the package. [ATTACHMENT filename='setting_scope.png'] The figure displays the View Diagram of the Entire Model template with a default scope that is specified through the «Expose» dependency.
5. If you want to add more packages to the default scope, repeat steps 3 and 4.
6. Save the project.
7. Select the custom template in the Cameo Collaborator Publisher dialog when you publish the project to Cameo Collaborator.

##### Setting the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document

The home page of a published project is the diagram that opens by default after the project portal is loaded. It can be set directly in the model that you intend to publish.

To more easily set the home page of your Cameo Collaborator document, see *Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip*file provided with Cameo Collaborator Publisher. The *2. Diagrams in a Package Template*sample of this fileshows you how to create a document displaying all diagrams from a specific package with one diagram set as the home page.

To set the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. If you use a default template to publish Cameo Collaborator documents, proceed to step 2. If you use a custom template do the following:
  1. Open the custom template you intend to use for publishing.
  2. Open the Specification window of the View element which will be used to display the diagram you want to set as the home page.
  3. Click the specification cell of the Active Element property and in the drop-down list select the FindLandingPage value.
  4. Set the **Active Landing Page** property to *true*. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Using multiple ViewsIf your custom template has multiple Views, set the **Active Landing Page** for one View only.
  5. Close the Specification window and save the template.
2. Open the model you intend to publish.
3. Make sure that the Document View Collaborator Profile is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='MD2024x'] . You can find it in the <intall.root>\profiles directory.
4. In the model browser, right-click the diagram you want to display as the home page of the project portal and select Home Page . [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_home_page.png']

After completing the above steps, the «HomePage» stereotype is applied to the selected diagram. When you publish your model, a diagram with this stereotype is automatically displayed as the home page of a project portal.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong> On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ddb8d7c4-53ff-422e-abe4-8fe6f8f5ded4"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>You can customize the appearance of a published project by customizing the template you plan to use for publishing. The best practice is to save an existing predefined template with a different name, and then make changes to the newly created template file.</span> After creating a new template file, you need to use it in the project you intend to publish. There is the option to change the default color of the title bar, document header and text, to add or remove the search button, and other style options. The procedure bellow describes how to modify the <em>Entire Model</em> template (stored as <em>Cameo Collaborator Library.mdzip)</em>, but you can also modify other predefined templates the same way.</p><p><br /></p><p>To customize the appearance a project portal</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <em>modelLibraries</em> folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and open the Cameo Collaborator template you want to use for publishing as a separate project.</li><li>Save the project in the same directory using a different name (e.g., <em>Custom template.mdzip</em>).</li><li>When you receive the warning not to edit standard/system profiles and libraries, click <strong>Continue</strong>.</li><li>Open a View Diagram of the template and double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype to open its properties Specification window.</li><li>Specify the values of the following properties:<br /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Primary Color Palette</strong> - defines the color of the app bar in the project portal.</li><li><strong>Secondary Color Palette</strong> - defines the color of the main portal icons, e.g., icons for creating and saving a comment.</li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> and save the project.</li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="customizing_project_portal.png" /></ac:image> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">The sample of a project portal showing the colors that can be customized.</span></h6><h3>Setting the default scope for publishing</h3><p>In the custom template, you can set the default scope for publishing. When the default scope is set, you don't need to specify it while publishing the project. In this case, the default scope is published even if you specify another scope in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog. </p><p><br /></p><p>To set the default scope for publishing</p><hr /><ol><li>If you haven't done so already, <a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD185/Using+other+projects+in+a+project">use your custom template in the project</a> you intend to publish.</li><li>Open the View Diagram of the custom template.</li><li>In the Containment tree, select the package you want to set as the default scope for publishing (e.g. <em>Categorization</em>) and drag it onto the diagram pane.</li><li>Draw the «Expose» dependency from the element with the «Document» stereotype to the package.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="setting_scope.png" /></ac:image><br />The figure displays the View Diagram of the <em>Entire Model</em> template with a default scope that is specified through the «Expose» dependency.<br /><br /></li><li>If you want to add more packages to the default scope, repeat steps 3 and 4.</li><li>Save the project.</li><li>Select the custom template in the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog when you publish the project to Cameo Collaborator.</li></ol><h3><br />Setting the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document</h3><p>The home page of a published project is the diagram that opens by default after the project portal is loaded. It can be set directly in the model that you intend to publish.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c37cdaf3-a80d-4ce3-bc38-1126c5430c38"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To more easily set the home page of your Cameo Collaborator document, see <em>Cameo Collaborator Template Samples.mdzip </em>file provided with Cameo Collaborator Publisher. The <em>2. Diagrams in a Package Template </em>sample of this file </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">shows you how to create a document displaying all diagrams from a specific package with one diagram set as the home page.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To set the home page of a Cameo Collaborator document</p><hr /><ol><li>If you use a default template to publish Cameo Collaborator documents, proceed to step 2. If you use a custom template do the following:<ol><li>Open the custom template you intend to use for publishing.</li><li>Open the Specification window of the View element which will be used to display the diagram you want to set as the home page.</li><li>Click the specification cell of the <strong>Active Element</strong> property and in the drop-down list select the <strong>FindLandingPage</strong> value.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set the <strong>Active Landing Page</strong> property to <em>true</em>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification_window_of_a_view.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3f92a329-8262-4937-9183-39f87d27ac55"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Using multiple Views</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If your custom template has multiple Views, set the <strong>Active Landing Page</strong> for one View only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Close the Specification window and save the template.</li></ol></li><li>Open the model you intend to publish.</li><li>Make sure that the Document View Collaborator Profile is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[used in your model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can find it in the <em>&lt;intall.root&gt;\profiles</em> directory.</li><li>In the model browser, right-click the diagram you want to display as the home page of the project portal and select <strong>Home Page</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_home_page.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p>After completing the above steps, the «HomePage» stereotype is applied to the selected diagram. When you publish your model, a diagram with this stereotype is automatically displayed as the home page of a project portal.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991570 space=MCS2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00069: Data Manager

- page_id: `137991570`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991570/Data+Manager
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-11T14:43:39.715+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Administration Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio administration
- labels: ['reset-admin-password']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

Data Manager is a Magic Collaboration Studio Java utility for interfacing directly with the Cassandra database. The Data Manager package can be found in the *Utilities*directory of your server installation, and as a portable zip package in the 3DS installer distribution.

Data Manager is used to make permanent and irrecoverable modifications to the Cassandra database. A full database backup should always be performed before making any modifications to the database.

Always use the Data Manager package included with your version of Magic Collaboration Studio distribution.

Running Data Manager

To run Data Manager

1. Stop Magic Collaboration Studio services to prevent users from accessing the database.
2. Ensure Cassandra is up and running: use the nodetool status command to verify the database status is Up and Normal .
3. Run a Data Manager command. If Data Manager is executed for the first time on the system, you will be prompted to provide the path to Java. Use the same version of Java as Magic Collaboration Studio. Execute with the *-help*option to ensure Data Manager is working: bashDJangotrue
4. After finishing all Data Manager steps, start Magic Collaboration Studio services to resume user access.

##### Data Manager configuration

Data Manager has its own configuration files, located in the Data Manager installation path, under *configuration*.

###### Database access

For best performance, run Data Manager on the Cassandra server. By default, Data Manager will access the database via Localhost. If you need to run Data Manager remotely from the Cassandra server, modify the**contact-points**field for **datastax-java-driver******in application.conf.

###### Cassandra Authentication

Data Manager will connect to Cassandra on localhost using anonymous authentication. If your Cassandra node is configured to bind to a specific IP address (by default, port 9042 binds to all interfaces), or if you have configured Cassandra to use an authenticator other than *AllowAllAuthenticator*, you will need to modify the**auth-provider******fields in**application.conf accordingly.

###### Logs

Data Manager will append to a detailed log file on each execution. The datamanager.log file is stored in the logs directory of the Data Manager installation path. You can change the location of the log file and the level of logging in logback.xml.

##### Data Manager commands

The following table outlines the commands you can use in Data Manager to manage your data.

| Server tool command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| -help | Displays Help information. |
| -dk, --drop-keyspace <keyspace=value> --silent-drop | Drops the 19.0 keyspace to free up the storage space after the database migration from 19.0 to 2021x.Add --silent-drop to run the command silently, i.e., without requesting user permission.You need to clear the removed keyspace snapshots using the nodetool utility command:]]> |
| -dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> | Deletes the given resource(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple resource IDs using space as a separator.If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server. |
| -drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>> | Deletes the given branch(es) and all of its related information. Specify multiple branch ID using a comma as a separator.includeChildBranch has two values: true and false. When the includeChildBranch value is set to false, and the branch has child branches, this branch will not be deleted.When the includeChildBranch value is set to true, the branch including its child branches will be deleted.This command does not work for the branches of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its branches. |
| -drd,--delete-marked-deleted-resources | Deletes all resources that were marked as deleted.If you delete a synchronized resource from the target server, the resource will be removed together with its remote resource configuration allowing you to create new configurations with the same resource from the source server. |
| -drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>> | Deletes the given revision(s) and all of its related information. Specify multiple revisions using a comma as a separator.includeChildRevision has two values: true and false. When the includeChildRevision value is set to false, and the revision is not the latest, it will not be deleted.When the includeChildRevision value is set to true, the upward revisions and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.This command does not work for the revisions of a synchronized resource. You can delete only the whole synchronized resource, but not its revisions. |
| -du,--delete-user <username> | Deletes the given username(s) and all of its related information.To specify multiple usernames, use comma as a separator.If the username consists of two words (for example, dan smith) add double quotation marks (" ") before and after the username:text |
| -rrof, --remove-read-only-flag <-resourceID=<value1>,<value2> --categoryID=<value1>,<value2>> | Removes the read-only flag from a synchronized resource or category in the target server. Specify multiple resources or categories using a comma as a separator, for example:Do not remove the read-only flag from periodically synchronized resources or categories. This will cause version inconsistencies after subsequent synchronizations.The read-only flag removal is recommended only if resources or categories were moved to another server as a result of a one-time synchronization operation and you want to make them active again. |
| -lb,--list-branch <resourceID=value> | Lists all branches of the given resource. |
| -lc,--list-cluster-ids | Lists previous cluster IDs and the current one. |
| -li,--list-index | Lists all available data that have indexes. |
| -lr,--list-resources | Lists all available resources. |
| -lrd,--list-marked-deleted-resources | Lists all resources that are marked as deleted. |
| -lrv,--list-revision <resourceID=value branchID=value> | Lists all revisions in the given branch. |
| -lu,--list-users <--no-info> | Lists all usernames and their information, sorted by name.You can add --no-info to show only the usernames. |
| -ram,--reset-administrator | Resets admin password, status, and permissions.If there is no Administrator account, it will be created.If the Administrator account exists in the database:If the account is for an external user, it will be reset to an internal user with a new password. This user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.If the account is an internal user, only a new password will be generated, and the user will be shown in the Teamwork Cloud Admin console.The default roles of the Teamwork Cloud Admin will be restored to make sure that this account can fully operate as the default administrator. |
| -rc,--recreate-cluster-id | Recreates a cluster ID. |
| -ri,--rebuild-index <data=value1,value2> | Rebuilds all indexes for the given data and uses the data=* option to rebuild all indexes of all available data. |
| -urd,--unmark-deleted-resources <resource ID> | Unmarks the given resource(s) previously marked as deleted. Specify multiple resource ID using space as a separator. |
| -vb,--validate-blob <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value --readAllWithCQL --createCompareOfThriftAndCQL> | Validates blob information. |
| -ve,--validate-eobject <resourceID=value1,value2 saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates the eObject information of a specific resource ID and uses the resourceID=* option to validate all resources. |
| -version | Displays the version of Data Manager. |
| -vi,--verify-index <data=value1,value2> | Verifies all indexes for the given data and uses the data=* option to verify all indexes of all available data. |
| -vr,--validate-rbac <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates a user, user group, role, and role assignment information. |
| -vrc,--validate-resource-commit <savePath=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates a metamodel, branch, and committed information. |
| -col,--cleanup-object-locks | Removes released object locks (that are no longer used) from the database.How to safely execute this command:Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal nodetool repair which you can find in the Cassandra/bin directory. Once nodetool repair is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal nodetool compact. After running the command in nodetool compact terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed. |
| -rcc,--rebuild-change-count <--resourceID=<resourceID1>,<resourceID2>> | Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.Example:datamanager -rccdatamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c |
| -crb,--clean-resource-blobs <--resourceID=<resourceID1>,<resourceID2>> | Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional resourceID argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned. |
| -ll,--list-ldap | Lists all LDAP realm information. |
| -rlu, --remap-ldap-users | Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.Exampledatamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b |
| -vlu,--validate-ldap-users <saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value> | Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration. |
| -udb,--unmark-deleted-branches <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2>> | Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted. |

The **delete** command to delete resources and users works as follows.

###### Deleting selected resource

```bash
...]]>
```

These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:

- Tags information of the resource.
- Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories).
- Branch information.
- Commit information.
- Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed).
- Locking information.

###### Deleting selected user

```bash
, ...]]>
```

This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:

- Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).
- Session (the existing user session will be removed).
- Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).
- User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).

#### NOTE: Notes on deleting usernames

Notes on deleting usernames

- For usernames consisting of two words (such as *dan smith*), add double quotation marks (" ") before and after the username, as shown: text
- In the modeling tool's history panels, the word deleted will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.

###### Deleting selected branch

```bash
, includeChildBranch=>]]>
```

The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:

- Tags information of each revision in the branch
- Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch
- Commit information of each revision in the branch
- Locking information of each object locked in the branch
- Branch information of the branch
- If the branch has child branches, depending on the includeChildBranch value, the branch will be deleted or not:
  - When the includeChildBranch value is set to False and the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.
  - When the includeChildBranch value is set to True, then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.

###### Deleting revisions

```bash
includeChildRevision=>]]>
```

The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:

- Tags information of the revision
- Object information created/modified at the revision
- Commit information of the revision
- Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to
- Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch
- If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the includeChildRevision value, the revision will be deleted or not:
  - When the includeChildRevision value is set to False, and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.
  - When the includeChildRevision value is set to T rue, then revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.

style="text-align: left;"><li>Before performing object lock cleanup make sure that all Cassandra nodes are running and accessible.</li><li>After Data Manager finishes cleaning locks, run the command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool repair</strong> which you can find in the <em>Cassandra/bin</em> directory. Once <strong>nodetool repair </strong>is done, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li><li>DataManager will ask to run command in a separate terminal <strong>nodetool compact</strong>. After running the command in <strong>nodetool compact </strong>terminal, return to Data Manager and confirm operation to proceed.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><p>-rcc,--rebuild-change-count &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Rebuilds the commit change count of all or the selected resources.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e9397cb-2a34-4dc4-9979-ab9aa5e39b0d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The process may take a while depending on the commit and change count.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40487135-04e4-41b7-8e01-40229da20b94"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can specify multiple revisions using commas as separators.</p><p>Example:</p><ul><li>datamanager -rcc</li><li>datamanager -rcc resourceID=956ec67b-e79f-4b51-9197-2f041265b637</li><li><span>datamanager -rcc resourceID=0c81b8e6-71ef-4dc6-b2f3-201626da1feb,5126b26c-275e-472d-adb5-8abd0cb9492c</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-crb,--clean-resource-blobs &lt;--resourceID=&lt;resourceID1&gt;,&lt;resourceID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Cleans resource metadata and obsolete local cache blobs. If the optional <strong>resourceID</strong> argument is not set, all the listed resource blobs (for archiving resources) will be cleaned.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-ll,--list-ldap</p></td><td><p>Lists all LDAP realm information.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-rlu, --remap-ldap-users</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Remaps LDAP user realms to another existing LDAP realm.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d17ead5-c338-4444-8707-2824840f8d12"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>datamanager -rlu source-realm=b8ea2561-b952-4588-8fff-269f49f0764f target-realm=4d1bdd4b-8ea7-4b4c-bfdc-7ee48ab9be3b</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-vlu,--validate-ldap-users &lt;saveDir=value onlyInconsistent=value&gt;</p></td><td><p>Validates users which have an LDAP realm ID pointing to a non-existing LDAP configuration.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-udb,--unmark-deleted-branches &lt;resourceID=value branchID=&lt;ID1&gt;,&lt;ID2&gt;&gt;</p></td><td><p>Unmarks the given branches that were previously marked as deleted.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />The <strong>delete</strong> command to delete resources and users works as follows.</p><h4>Deleting selected resource </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abe1732a-965d-4141-8318-eb14b852a461"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-dr,--delete-resource <resource ID> <resource ID> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>These commands delete a selected resource and all of the following resource-related information:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of the resource. </li><li>Category information (the resource will be removed from all categories). </li><li>Branch information. </li><li>Commit information. </li><li>Role assignment information (role assignment information related to the resource will be removed). </li><li>Locking information.</li></ul><h4>Deleting selected user </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d89fa469-6680-4099-8b73-5b9199426522"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-du,--delete-user <username>,<username> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>This command deletes a selected user and all of the following user-related information:</span></p><ul><li>Role assignment information (all role assignment information belonging to the user will be removed).</li><li>Session (the existing user session will be removed).</li><li>Lock information (model elements in projects locked by the user will be unlocked).</li><li>User group information (the user will be removed from all user groups).<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fdb4f757-4a39-4a2e-b0bc-bd228aefce15"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Notes on deleting usernames</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">For usernames consisting of two words (such as <em>dan smith</em>), add double quotation marks (&quot; &quot;) before and after the username, as shown:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac17643d-503e-4234-bc3c-db295661ad2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[   --delete-user "dan smith" 
````html
   -du "dan smith"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the modeling tool's history panels, the word <em>deleted</em> will appear in brackets next to the deleted users.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Deleting selected branch </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82a7bd5f-fc36-4868-bcb6-59a08022d27f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drb,--delete-branch <resourceID=value branchID=<ID1>,<ID2> includeChildBranch=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The branch-related information which will be removed together with the branch includes:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of each revision in the branch</li><li>Object information created/modified at each revision in the branch</li><li>Commit information of each revision in the branch</li><li>Locking information of each object locked in the branch</li><li>Branch information of the branch</li><li>If the branch has child branches, depending on the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong><strong> </strong>value, the branch will be deleted or not: <em> </em><br /><ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch</strong> value is set to <em>False </em>and<em> </em>the branch has child branches, then this branch will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildBranch </strong>value is set to <em>True, </em>then even if the branch has child branches it will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul><h4>Deleting revisions </h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7deaa30e-49cc-4711-9f3b-1d2227ceb92f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-drr,--delete-revision <resourceID=value revision=<revision1,revision2> includeChildRevision=<true/false>>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>The revision-related information which will be removed together with the revision includes:</span></p><ul><li>Tags information of the revision</li><li>Object information created/modified at the revision</li><li>Commit information of the revision</li><li>Locking information of objects whose id's do not exist anymore in the branch that the revision belongs to</li><li>Branch information of the branch if the revision was the first revision of the branch</li><li>If a selected revision is not the latest, depending on the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong><strong> </strong>value, the revision will be deleted or not: <ul><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision</strong> value is set to <em>False, </em>and the revision is not the latest, then it will not be deleted.</li><li>When the <strong>includeChildRevision </strong>value is set to <em>T</em><em>rue,</em> then<em> </em>revisions upward and branches within it from the selected version will be deleted.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137994327 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00070: Data markings support

- page_id: `137994327`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137994327/Data+markings+support
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:55:08.957+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Data Markings and Classification Plugin' space='DMP2024x'] to ensure consistency between documents and related Magic Collaboration Studio projects.The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classificationor sensitivity. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.

For information on how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Magic Collaboration Studio, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin' space=''].

The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.

The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.

Data markings are supported in tables as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Data markings displayed in a table banner.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">Cameo Collaborator documents support data markings created using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DMP2024x" ri:content-title="Data Markings and Classification Plugin" /></ac:link> to ensure consistency between documents and related Magic Collaboration Studio projects. </span>The Data Markings and Classification Plugin allows you to mark your model elements to indicate different levels of classification<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">or sensitivity</span>. All the data markings created in the modeling tool, including diagram and table banners, are also visible in a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81888686-7ea9-4d43-8e16-47dacf2e4a5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information on <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">how to use data markings to classify resources, categories, users, and user groups in Magic Collaboration Studio, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing data markings in Teamwork Cloud Admin" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The figure below demonstrates how data markings are displayed in parentheses next to element names in the navigation and content panes of a Cameo Collaborator document.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_element_names.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings next to element names in a Cameo Collaborator document.</h6><p><br /></p><p>The following figure shows data markings displayed on element shapes and in a diagram banner.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_diagrams.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings on diagram shapes and in a diagram banner.</h6><p>Data markings are supported in tables as well.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_markings_in_tables.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Data markings displayed in a table banner.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992558 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00071: Deleting a comment

- page_id: `137992558`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992558/Deleting+a+comment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-07T14:41:38.576+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.

To delete a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening the comments panel' space=''].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the bottom of the comment and select Delete .

#### TIP: Useful to know

Useful to know

- Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.
- You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can delete the comments that are no longer needed, but keep in mind that this action is irreversible.</p><p><br /></p><p>To delete a comment</p><hr /><ol><li><p>In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to delete.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening the comments panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Cameo Collaborator comments panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>In the comments panel, find the comment you want to delete.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="541e0626-073b-4cd7-8106-6c818482f294"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Useful to know</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Deleting a comment also deletes all of its replies.</li><li>You can delete individual comment replies the same way you delete comments.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991336 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00073: Deploying Cassandra Multi-Data Center

- page_id: `137991336`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991336/Deploying+Cassandra+Multi-Data+Center
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:42:13.723+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio Installation > Advanced Magic Collaboration Studio installation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**4**

Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh2 and newer versions support Apache Cassandra multiple data centers (Multi-DC) capability. Multi-DC Cassandra ensures continuous availability of data to Magic Collaboration Studio users. There are two main scenarios for using multiple data centers: live backup/disaster recovery and geographical location. The deployment configuration of a live backup data center is presented here as an example.

Please note that Multi-DC deployment requires in-depth knowledge of Cassandra architecture and administration. Incorrect deployment or operation can result in total data loss. Technical support is not available, as this is considered a custom deployment strategy.

##### **Scenarios for deploying a Multi-DC Cassandra cluster**

###### **Live backup/disaster recovery**

Multiple data centers can be deployed at different physical locations as a disaster recovery solution. In this situation, one Cassandra data center would be the primary access point, while an off-site secondary data center would continuously receive the latest database updates. If the primary data center goes down, data can be restored from the secondary data center by bringing new Cassandra nodes online at the primary data center. Magic Collaboration Studio can also be reconfigured to connect to the secondary data center as a temporary solution.

In this scenario, there is no degradation in performance. Read and write operations are performed on the primary data center first, then eventually synced to the second data center as a background operation.

###### **Geographical location**

For organizations with users spread across multiple geographical locations, it may be desirable to optimize data access by location. Each region can have a dedicated data center. All of the data centers would form one Cassandra cluster, and all of them would have access to the latest data.

In this scenario, performance would mainly be affected by the database write operations. Write operations would be applied to all data centers. Read operations would remain local and thus not affect performance.

##### **Deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for Magic Collaboration Studio**

This is the recommended configuration procedure for deploying Multi-DC Cassandra for Magic Collaboration Studio.

To configure Multi-DC Cassandra cluster

1. 
2. Start Cassandra cluster and verify all data centers are online.
3. 
4. 
5. For an existing database , .
6. Start Magic Collaboration Studio services and verify operation.

**Configuring Cassandra cluster with multiple data centers**

Our example deployment is a single Cassandra cluster with two data centers located in separate geographical locations. Each Cassandra data center has three nodes. When performing the initial installation and configuration of Cassandra nodes, make sure the version of Cassandra is consistent across all nodes. Also, make sure to synchronize clocks across all nodes.

Start by updating the *cassandra.yaml* configuration file.

Set a unique cluster name for all nodes. This deployment is a single cluster spanning multiple data centers. The cluster name should be the same across all nodes.

```text

```

Set the seed provider for all data centers. Assign at least two seed nodes for each data center. Specify the address for all seeds. Alternatively, use a seed provider service to keep track of seed node addresses.

```text

```

Then, change the endpoint snitch implementation on each node. The default snitch is *SimpleSnitch*, which does not support Multi-DC. Cassandra uses snitch to gather network topology information. In this case, we are using *GossipingPropertyFileSnitch* for Multi-DC setup. Cassandra will obtain data center information from the *cassandra-rackdc.properties* file when *GossipingPropertyFileSnitch* is set.

```text

```

Refer to Cassandra documentation if you are updating snitch for an exiting database: [https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/operations/opsSwitchSnitch.html)

Edit the *cassandra-rackdc.properties* file for each node. All nodes in a data center should have the same “dc” name.

For the primary data center:

```text

```

For the backup data center:

```text

```

Start each Cassandra node. Port 7000 is the default inter-node communication (encrypted and unencrypted) port for a Cassandra cluster. Make sure port 7000 is open for communication between data centers. Please note that the initial connection between data centers can take some time. Run *nodetool status* to confirm that all data centers have connected to the cluster.

For an existing Cassandra cluster, run *nodetool repair* and *nodetool cleanup* after the above configurations have been made.

###### **Configuring Magic Collaboration Studio for Multi-DC**

Once all Cassandra nodes are online and data center connections are confirmed, configure the Magic Collaboration Studio *application.conf* file for Multi-DC.

In the *esi.persistence.cassandra.keyspace* section, we have added the following parameters for our example. With 3 nodes in each data center, the replication factor is set to 3. The consistency level is set to “FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL" for a disaster recovery scenario. Please refer to the table below for consistency setting options. If deploying an entirely new database, the “twc” keyspace will be created with the replication strategy and factors specified here. For an existing database, you will have to manually edit the keyspace replication parameters (see Cassandra Keyspace Configuration below).

```text

```

- When *is-multi-dc* is set to *true*, the *replication-factor* parameter is ignored.
- The *dc-replication-factors* configuration will only be applied when creating a new “twc” keyspace. If there is an existing “twc” keyspace in Cassandra, Magic Collaboration Studio will use the existing replication factor set in the database. Use *ALTER KEYSPACE* to change the replication factor.

| Keyspace Consistency Setting | Write Consistency Level | Read Consistency Level |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_GLOBAL | ALL | ONE |
| BEST_READ_PERFORMANCE_LOCAL | ALL | LOCAL_ONE |
| BEST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL | EACH_QUORUM | EACH_QUORUM |
| FAST_RELIABILITY_GLOBAL | EACH_QUORUM | LOCAL_QUORUM |
| BEST_RELIABILITY_LOCAL | LOCAL_QUORUM | LOCAL_QUORUM |

In the *esi.persistence.datastax-java-driver.basic* section, set the data center name and contact points to local values. The local data center will be used as the primary database for client requests.

```text

```

Use the seed nodes from the local data center specified in *cassandra.yaml*

```text

```

###### **Configuring authentication in Web Application Platform for Multi-DC**

The Authentication web app will need to be configured to use the local data center (*datacenter1* in this example). Add the *cassandra.localDatacenter* parameter in *authserver.properties*. Update *cassandra.contactPoints* to local Cassandra node addresses, if needed.

```text

```

###### **Configuring Cassandra keyspace**

If you are deploying Multi-DC with an existing Cassandra database, you will need to manually reconfigure the replication parameters for the “twc” keyspace.

Use *cqlsh*, the Cassandra command-line interface, to modify “twc” keyspace replication parameters.

```bash

```

Run *nodetool repair* after keyspace modification to update all nodes.

To verify replication in *cqlsh*:

```bash

```

These steps can also be used to create the “twc” keyspace. Note that manual keyspace creation is not needed, as Magic Collaboration Studio will create the keyspace during initial startup.

```bash

```

##### **Multi-DC Cassandra Best Practices**

- The Cassandra version must be the same across all nodes and data centers
- At least two seed nodes should be assigned for each data center
- Clocks should be synchronized for all node

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a2bcab6-eec9-4706-8f59-df3a3322213f"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Magic Collaboration Studio 2022x Refresh2 and newer versions support Apache Cassandra multiple data centers (Multi-DC) capability. Multi-DC Cassandra ensures continuous availability of data to Magic Collaboration Studio users. There are two main scenarios for using multiple data centers: live backup/disaster recovery and geographical location. The deployment configuration of a live backup data center is presented here as an example.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Please note that Multi-DC deployment requires in-depth knowledge of Cassandra architecture and administration. Incorrect deployment or operation can result in total data loss. Technical support is not available, as this is considered a custom deployment strategy.</span></p><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Scenarios for deploying a Multi-DC Cassandra cluster</strong></span></h3><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Live backup/disaster recovery</strong></span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Multiple data centers can be deployed at different physical locations as a disaster recovery solution. In this situation, one Cassandra data center would be the primary access point, while an off-site secondary data center would continuously receive the latest database updates. If the primary data center goes down, data can be restored from the secondary data center by bringing new Cassandra nodes online at the primary data center. Magic Collaboration Studio can also be reconfigured to connect to the secondary data center as a temporary solution.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">In this scenario, there is no degradation in performance. Read and write operations are performed on the primary data center first, then eventually synced to the second data center as a background operation.</span></p><h4 style="line-height: 1.42857;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Geographical location</strong></span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">For organizations with users spread across multiple geographical locations, it may be desirable to optimize data access by location. Each region can have a dedicated data center.
````
