<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992321 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00080: Document templates

- page_id: `137992321`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992321/Document+templates
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:06:10.824+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['using-predefined-templates']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:

- **Predefined templates**, which are stored the *modelLibraries* folder in the installation directory of your modeling tool and appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e. g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.
- **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with custom document templates' space='']**, which are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user defined location. These templates appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog, if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. [Learn how to create a custom Cameo Collaborator template.](https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/webapp/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&viewType=document§ionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&guest=true)

The following table briefly describes all predefined templates and lists No Magic's modeling tools where each of them is available.

| Template name | Short description | Available in... |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BPMN | Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:ProcessesData ObjectsOrganization StructuresInformation Systems | MagicDraw + Cameo Business Modeler PluginCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Entire Model | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model. | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Diagrams | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model. | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Requirements Document | Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal. | MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler PluginMagicDraw + SysML PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Requirements | Apply this template to publish a project as the Requirements portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureTest CasesTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw + Cameo Requirements Modeler PluginMagicDraw + SysML PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| Software Engineering | Apply this template to publish a project as the Software Engineering portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureImplementationTest CasesArchitecture TraceabilityTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw (with or without plugins)Cameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| SysML | Apply this template to publish a project as the SysML portal, which provides the following views of the model:RequirementsStructureInterfacesConstraintsBehaviorDiagrams | MagicDraw + SysML PluginMagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Systems ModelerCameo Enterprise Architecture |
| UAF | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Security ViewResources View - ResourcesSystems View - ResourcesPhysical Resources View - ResourcesResources View - Resource ExchangesSystems View - Resource ExchangesPhysical Resource View - Resource ExchangesResources View - FunctionsSystems View - FunctionsPhysical Resources View - FunctionsService Oriented View - Service InterfacesServices View - Service InterfacesService Oriented View - Service SpecificationsServices View - Service SpecificationsService Oriented View - Service FunctionsServices View - Service FunctionsProgramme View - ProjectsProject View - ProjectsAcquisition View - Projects50%Personnel View - Organisational RelationshipsOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsLogical View - Organisational RelationshipsLogical View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Operational ExchangesLogical View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational PerformersLogical View - Operational PerformersStrategic View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - CapabilitiesCapability View - CapabilitiesStrategic View - CapabilitiesDiagramsData and InformationDictionaryTechnical Standards | MagicDraw + UAF Plugin |
| UPDM | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Capability View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - CapabilitiesOperational View - NodesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsSystems View - ResourcesSystems View - FunctionsSystems View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service Accesses50%Services View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service InterfacesServices View - InterfacesServices View - Service FunctionsProject View - ProjectsStandardsDictionaryDataDiagrams | MagicDraw + UPDM 2 PluginCameo Enterprise Architecture |

**Additional resources**

- Webinar *Creating Templates in Cameo Collaborator*
- User Guide for Creating Cameo Collaborator Templates

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992445 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00081: Document view modes

- page_id: `137992445`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992445/Document+view+modes
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:25:11.340+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Structure of the document portal
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can view a Cameo Collaborator document in several different view modes:

- Model view : In this view mode, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space=''] of a document portal shows only the content of the item selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Navigation pane' space=''] . In addition, the Model view shows model information and is the default mode of a Cameo Collaborator document.
- Document view : This mode hides model details and allows you to browse a Cameo Collaborator document continuously. You can scroll through the entire content of the selected model view, or select an item in the navigation pane to load the related content.
- Wiki view : Similar to the Document view, The Wiki view hides model details while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view.

To change the view mode of a Cameo Collaborator document

1. In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click [ATTACHMENT filename='model_view_list.png'] and select the model view whose view mode you want to change.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] .
3. In the open menu, click Active view and select the desired view mode. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_document_view_mode.png']

After changing the document view mode, the selected model view is reloaded and displayed in a new mode.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can view a Cameo Collaborator document in several different view modes:</p><ul><li><strong>Model view</strong>: In this view mode, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Content pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[content pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of a document portal shows only the content of the item selected in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Navigation pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[navigation pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. In addition, the Model view shows model information and is the default mode of a Cameo Collaborator document.</li><li><strong>Document view</strong>: This mode hides model details and allows you to browse a Cameo Collaborator document continuously. You can scroll through the entire content of the selected model view, or select an item in the navigation pane to load the related content.</li><li><strong>Wiki view</strong>: <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">Similar to the Document view, The Wiki view</span> hides model details while displaying the content of just one section, like the Model view.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the view mode of a Cameo Collaborator document</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>In the app bar of a Cameo Collaborator document, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="11"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_view_list.png" /></ac:image>  and select the model view whose view mode you want to change.</li><li>On the right side of the app bar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the open menu, click <strong>Active view</strong> and select the desired view mode.<br /><br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_document_view_mode.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br />After changing the document view mode, the selected model view is reloaded and displayed in a new mode.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=301368812 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00082: Downloading permissions report

- page_id: `301368812`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/301368812/Downloading+permissions+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-23T14:15:24.962+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application
- labels: ['twc', 'mcs']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can download a user's permission report and view the permissions they were assigned in Excel format.

To download the permissions report, you must have the following permissions: **List All Resources**,******Manage Security Roles**, and**Manage User Permissions**.

To download the permissions report

Do one of the following:

- In the Users application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] next to the user and select Generate permissions report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- In the My account application, c lick [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom left corner to download the report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can download a user's permission report and view the permissions they were assigned in Excel format.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89f16d31-5ebf-41e2-8c09-1227705c5513"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To download the permissions report, you must have the following permissions: <strong><span>List All Resources</span></strong><span>,</span><strong><span> </span></strong><span><strong>Manage Security Roles</strong>, and </span><span><strong>Manage User Permissions</strong>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To download the permissions report</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2a8b23f9-20f1-4185-a36e-e59c72590ead">In the <strong>Users </strong>application, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> next to the user and select <strong>Generate permissions report.<br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generate_permissions_report.jpg" /></ac:image></strong></li><li data-uuid="7e5f1551-b783-43e2-a7cc-e092087b6cbb">In the <strong>My account </strong>application, c<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">lick <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Download icon.png" /></ac:image></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">at the bottom left corner to download the report.<br /></span></li></ul><p><br /><ac:image ac:width="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="my_account_app_download.jpg" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992553 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00083: Editing a comment

- page_id: `137992553`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992553/Editing+a+comment
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-07T14:41:28.169+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with comments in a modeling tool
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

You can edit comments in the Cameo Collaborator comments panel as described below.

To edit a comment

1. In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to edit.
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening the comments panel' space=''].
3. In the comments panel, find the comment you want to edit.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the right side of the comment to show available actions.
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom of the comment and select **Edit**. The **Edit comment** pane opens.
6. In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.
7. If needed, click the High priority switch on the bottom of the Edit comment pane to change the comment priority.
8. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save.png'] on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.

##### Discarding comments

If you do not want to save comment changes after starting to edit the comment, you can cancel the editing process.

To discard a comment

- After starting to edit a comment, click [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel_comment.png'] on the top left corner of the Edit comment pane to cancel the changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12b48982-619b-45ce-bf2f-e4af52d77a89"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can edit comments in the Cameo Collaborator comments panel as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit a comment</p><hr style="" /><ol><li><p>In a modeling tool, open the model related to the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document with comments you want to edit.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Opening the comments panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Cameo Collaborator comments panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>In the comments panel, find the comment you want to edit.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png" /></ac:image> on the right side of the comment to show available actions.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the bottom of the comment and select <strong>Edit</strong>. The <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane opens.</p></li><li>In the open pane, edit the comment title and/or comment text.</li><li>If needed, click the <strong>High priority</strong> switch on the bottom of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to change the comment priority.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="save.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the pane to save the changes.</li></ol><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Discarding comments</h3><p>If you do not want to save comment changes after starting to edit the comment, you can cancel the editing process.</p><p><br /></p><p>To discard a comment</p><hr style="" /><ul><li>After starting to edit a comment, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cancel_comment.png" /></ac:image> on the top left corner of the <strong>Edit comment</strong> pane to cancel the changes.</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992139 space=MCS2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: Editing an LDAP server configuration properties

- page_id: `137992139`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992139/Editing+an+LDAP+server+configuration+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:51:10.669+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing LDAP servers
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the configuration properties of an LDAP server connected to the Magic Collaboration Studio system. The system will update the configuration properties with the new values automatically.

To edit the configuration properties of an LDAP server do one of the following

- On the **LDAP management** page, select card with LDAP server which you want to edit****and click **Edit**button.
  - Change wanted configuration parameters.
  - Click Save to save changes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the configuration properties of an LDAP server connected to the Magic Collaboration Studio system. The system will update the configuration properties with the new values automatically.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit the configuration properties of an LDAP server do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li><p>On the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">LDAP management</strong> page, select card with LDAP server which you want to edit<strong> </strong>and click <strong>Edit </strong>button. </p><ul><li>Change wanted configuration parameters.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong> to save changes. </li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991979 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00085: Editing custom roles

- page_id: `137991979`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991979/Editing+custom+roles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-02T16:41:51.442+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Roles application > Managing roles
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

3

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating roles' space=''], you can modify it by changing its name, description, and permissions as described in the following sections.

#### NOTE: Editing roles

Editing roles

- You can edit only custom (user-created) roles.
- You must have the Security Manager role to edit custom roles.

##### Editing role details

In the Roles application, you can edit the name and description of a custom role as described below.

To edit role details

1. Do one of the following actions:
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Edit button in the role details card. [ATTACHMENT filename='editing_role_details.png']
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='actions.png'] next to a role name and select Edit role details .
2. When the Edit role details pane opens, change role name and or description and click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_role.png'] .

##### Changing role permissions

You can use the Roles application to add or remove [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='']of a custom role. Note that a role must have at least one permission.

To add or remove permissions of a role

1. Do one of the following actions:
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, select a role and when the Role pane opens, click the Change button in the role details card.
  - In the content pane of the Roles application, click [ATTACHMENT filename='actions.png'] next to a role name and select Change permissions .
2. When the Change permissions pane opens, select the permissions you want to assign to the role (as shown below) and/or clear the check-boxes next to the permissions you want to remove. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_permissions_pane.png']
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_role.png'] to save the changes.

## PAGE 00086: Editing element values

- page_id: `137992572`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992572/Editing+element+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T10:51:40.506+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Editing models in Cameo Collaborator
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Deleting property values

297032173

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Deleting property values

297032185

297032171

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Deleting property values

297032174

If you need to provide feedback on a model or address reviewer's comments, edit certain element values right in the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

- In order to edit element values in a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Publishing a model for editing' space='MCS2024x'] .
- Before starting editing element values, read about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model editing behavior and constraints' space='MCS2024x'] to understand how this feature works.

To edit element values in a Cameo Collaborator document

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space='MCS2024x'].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024x'].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
4. In the **Edit** pane (displayed below), change the item value and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example shows how you can edit requirement text.

Once you finish the steps described above, the modified item value is saved both in the Cameo Collaborator document and and Magic Collaboration Studio project. See the figures below for more examples of editing different types of values.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing a Boolean type of value.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Editing an Enumeration type of value.

#### TIP: Deleting property values

Deleting property values

To delete the value of an editable property:

1. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024x'].
2. On the content pane, select the item you want to delete. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
3. When the **Edit** pane opens, click next to the value you want to delete.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

##### Reordering the values of multi-value properties

You can use the model editing feature to reorder the values of multi-value properties as described below.

To reorder the values of multi-value properties

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening a resource' space='MCS2024x'].
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the bottom right corner of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Content pane' space='MCS2024x'].
3. On the content pane, select the item you want to edit. Editable items are highlighted in yellow when hovering over them with a mouse pointer.
4. When the **Edit** pane opens, drag a value to the desired position as displayed below. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the changes.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992569 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00087: Editing models in Cameo Collaborator

- page_id: `137992569`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992569/Editing+models+in+Cameo+Collaborator
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-08T15:16:48.073+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

You can edit certain model elements right in a Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document. This is very beneficial for the whole product team, because engineers and even stakeholders can make changes to a model without having to use a modeling tool. In addition, the model editing capability speeds up the collaboration on a model. It allows you to instantly correct property values in a Cameo Collaborator document and skip the commenting process.

The sections below explain several main workflows where the model editing feature can be used.

#### NOTE: Documents published from synchronized projects

Documents published from synchronized projects

If a Cameo Collaborator document is published from a synchronized project on the target Magic Collaboration Studio server, the commenting and model editing capabilities in the document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space=''].

##### Model editing to provide feedback

If you need to review a Cameo Collaborator document and provide feedback on the model, follow the workflow below.

To provide feedback on a model

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document you want to review.
2. Review the document, including its structure, element characteristics, and diagrams.
3. Do one or several of the following actions:
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with comments in Cameo Collaborator' space=''] for items that need to be addressed.
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space=''] if you know what they should be.
4. Inform the author that you have finished reviewing the document so that he/she could react to your feedback.

##### Model editing to react to feedback

When a reviewer provides feedback on a Cameo Collaborator document, address the comments right in the document without having to use a modeling tool.

To react to feedback

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud document that has comments you need to address.
2. In the app bar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Comments pane' space=''] and read the comments.
3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing element values' space=''] in the document to address the reported issues.
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Replying to comments' space=''] to inform the reviewer that required changes have been made or ask for additional information.
5. Inform the reviewer that you have finished updating the model and ask for another round of review.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991783 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00088: Enabling and disabling users

- page_id: `137991783`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991783/Enabling+and+disabling+users
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:04:13.603+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Users application > Managing users > Managing users details
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to disable login for user. To enable or disable user login you must have **User manager** role.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Easily distinguish enabled user from disabled.

To enable/disable user login, do one of the following

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the user and from menu select to Disable user/Enable user .
- Go to User pane and select to edit user details,
  - Change sliders position next to Enabled (slide left to disable, slide right to enable user`s login).
- Go to User pane and click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on Account card, Actions menu will open.
  - From menu select to Disable user/Enable user.

If you disable a user's access while he or she is online or logged in, Teamwork Cloud Admin will not log them out immediately. Changes to access permission will take effect immediately once the user logs out.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to disable login for user. To enable or disable user login you must have <strong>User manager</strong> role. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabled_disabled_users.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Easily distinguish enabled user from disabled.</h6><p>To enable/disable user login, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> </span> next to the user and from menu select to <strong>Disable user/Enable user</strong>.</li><li>Go to <strong>User</strong> pane and select to edit user details,<ul><li> Change sliders position next to <strong>Enabled </strong>(slide left to disable, slide right to enable user`s login). </li></ul></li><li>Go to <strong>User</strong> pane and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on <strong>Account </strong>card, <strong>Actions</strong> menu will open. <br /><ul><li>From menu select to <strong>Disable user/Enable user. </strong></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f37d2c2-3674-43dd-9dd0-560cee5f9f94"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you disable a user's access while he or she is online or logged in, Teamwork Cloud Admin will not log them out immediately. Changes to access permission will take effect immediately once the user logs out.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992041 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00089: Enabling external user creation

- page_id: `137992041`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992041/Enabling+external+user+creation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-03-13T14:23:37.212+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing server settings
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This option allows you to manage external user creation process. With changing switch positions user creation can be disabled or enabled:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If you enable the Create external user on successful authentication option, an external user will be created upon successful authentication. This means that external users will be able to log in to Teamwork Cloud Admin even if no user existed before. An external user will be created upon such login. If you disable this option, no external users will be created if the user tries to log in.

After an external user is created, it takes some time for it to show up inthe **User** application list. This delay is caused by the properties caching.

- Once you enable external user creation, you can also enable the Default user permissions template . With this option enabled, external users created upon successful authentication are added to the Default Template user group and given its permissions and assignments.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This option allows you to manage external user creation process. With changing switch positions user creation can be disabled or enabled: </p><p><br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="User_management.PNG" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="32399256-5ed8-48e8-9d61-59b8a10ca7ab">If you enable the<strong> Create external user on successful authentication </strong>option, an external user will be created upon successful authentication. This means that external users will be able to log in to Teamwork Cloud Admin even if no user existed before. An external user will be created upon such login.<br />If you disable this option, no external users will be created if the user tries to log in.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78a82261-7405-43f9-9dfb-b128f1cf1369"><ac:rich-text-body><p>After an external user is created, <span>it takes some time for it to show up in </span>the <strong>User</strong> application list. This delay is caused by the <span>properties</span> caching.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="f3f8e625-afc5-4c8b-8091-77dedbf85109">Once you enable external user creation, you can also enable the <strong>Default user permissions template</strong>. With this option enabled, external users created upon successful authentication are added to the Default Template user group and given its permissions and assignments.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992026 space=MCS2024x version=7 -->
## PAGE 00090: Enabling secure connection between client and server

- page_id: `137992026`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992026/Enabling+secure+connection+between+client+and+server
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-05-15T15:17:30.454+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Admin web applications > Settings application > Managing server settings
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**5**

By default, communication between the Teamwork Cloud server and client is unencrypted. This page provides information on how to encrypt communication using TLS over TCP.

##### Enabling TLS encryption on server side

To enable a secure connection to Teamwork Cloud, you must enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's **Server Settings** page. This page also allows you to disable the option if you do not need to use a secure connection (see the following figure). You can always enable it whenever necessary. The most straightforward way to set up encryption is to use the keystore generated for Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. Locate and download the server Java KeyStore (default: *<install_root>/****TeamworkCloud/configuration/keystore.p12*). Make a note of the password used for the keystore. If there is a corresponding public certificate (e.g. *teamworkcloud.crt*) on the server, download it as well. If there is no public certificate, use the following command to extract the public certificate from the Java keystore:

```bash
-keystore  -file ]]>
```

If you need to generate a new keystore, see [Regenerating self-signed keystore](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/mcs2024x/Managing+SSL+certificate#ManagingSSLcertificate-Regeneratingself-signedkeystore).

The unencrypted port (default: 3579) remains active even after encryption is enabled. To prevent unencrypted connections, block the port at the firewall level.

To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud Admin

1. Go to the Settings app.
2. Move the slider to the right to enable the TLS protocol.
3. Enter a custom port for encrypted communication, or keep default value of 10002.
4. Upload the Java keystore file, and type the password.
5. Click Save .

##### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Enabling TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud.

##### Setting up client-side TLS

To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol on the client-side, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Server Certificates' space='MD2024x'] tool and provide the public certificate.

The default port for a secure connection is 10002. If you are using another port for a secure connection instead of the default, append the port number to the server name in the **Login** dialog:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The unecrypted port (3579) remains active even after TLS is enabled. Teamwork Cloud will continue to accept communication through both encrypted and unencrypted ports. Use firewall to disable port 3579 and block unencrypted communication.

#### EXPAND: Generating a new key pair manually

Generating a new key pair manually

To enable a secure connection between the client and the server, you need to generate two keystore files, each in their own keystores:

- keystore.jks (contains key and certificate) – server-side – upload it using Teamwork Cloud Admin console.
- cert.jks (contains public certificate) – client-side – add it using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Server Certificates' space='MD2024x'] option group in Environment Options.

1. Go to your Java directory and open the folder named bin .
2. Enter keytool -genkey -alias MyDomain -keyalg RSA -keystore keystore.jks -keysize 2048 and press Enter . You can give any name to*MyDomain* and **keystore.jks**.
3. Enter a password for your keystore and answer the questions asked. A certificate with a key is generated and placed into the keystore.
4. To extract a certificate without a key, type keytool -export -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore keystore.jks and press Enter .
5. To place a certificate into another keystore, e.g. cert.jks , type keytool -import -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore cert.jks and press Enter . You can give any name to **cert.jks**. However, by default, the tool searches for this exact name. Two keystore files are generated: one for the server ( keystore.jks ) and one for the client ( cert.jks ). You can now proceed to enable a secure connection between the client (modeling tool) and the server (Teamwork Cloud).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e012f520-d448-43ff-940f-2e6a38a53a05"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">5</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>By default, communication between the Teamwork Cloud server and client is unencrypted. This page provides information on how to encrypt communication using TLS over TCP.</p><h3>Enabling TLS encryption on server side</h3><p>To enable a secure connection to Teamwork Cloud, you must enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) on the Teamwork Cloud Admin's <strong>Server Settings</strong> page. This page also allows you to disable the option if you do not need to use a secure connection (see the following figure). You can always enable it whenever necessary. The most straightforward way to set up encryption is to use the keystore generated for Teamwork Cloud and/or Web Application Platform. <span>Locate and download the server Java KeyStore (default: <em>&lt;install_root&gt;/</em></span><em> </em><span><em>TeamworkCloud/configuration/keystore.p12</em>). Make a note of the password used for the keystore. If there is a corresponding public certificate (e.g. <em>teamworkcloud.crt</em>) on the server, download it as well. If there is no public certificate, use the following command to extract the public certificate from the Java keystore:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ec4149b3-edfc-4638-9615-c21e2bb76fa4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[keytool -export -alias <teamworkcloud> -keystore <keystore.p12> -file <teamworkcloud.crt>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81d2bf01-d7ee-414c-b837-b008ca6ae249"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to generate a new keystore, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/mcs2024x/Managing+SSL+certificate#ManagingSSLcertificate-Regeneratingself-signedkeystore">Regenerating self-signed keystore</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16cd75e5-08bb-4b6c-895d-c0558e82a18d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The unencrypted port (default: 3579) remains active even after encryption is enabled. To prevent unencrypted connections, block the port at the firewall level.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span> </span>To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud Admin</p><hr /><ol><li>Go to the <strong>Settings</strong> app.</li><li>Move the slider to the right to enable the TLS protocol. </li><li>Enter a custom port for encrypted communication, or keep default value of 10002.</li><li>Upload the <strong>Java <span>keystore </span></strong>file, and type the password.</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><h3 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP_Secure_Connection.jpg" /></ac:image></h3><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Enabling TLS protocol in Teamwork Cloud</span><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">.</span></h6><h3>Setting up client-side TLS</h3><p>To enable a secure connection using the TLS protocol on the client-side, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Server Certificates" /></ac:link> tool and provide the public certificate.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc7b6091-11cb-4b03-926c-04d2152afeeb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default port for a secure connection is 10002. If you are using another port for a secure connection instead of the default, append the port number to the server name in the <strong>Login</strong> dialog:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="login_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed6bf311-e684-4c82-a57f-7b7151f64604"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The unecrypted port (3579) remains active even after TLS is enabled. Teamwork Cloud will continue to accept communication through both encrypted and unencrypted ports. Use firewall to disable port 3579 and block unencrypted communication.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f799e476-60ca-42f0-92a9-e767a686d4cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Generating a new key pair manually</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><span>To enable a secure connection between the client and the server, you need to generate two keystore files, each in their own keystores:</span><ul><li><strong><span>keystore</span>.jks </strong>(contains key and certificate) – server-side – upload it using Teamwork Cloud Admin console.</li><li><strong>cert.jks</strong> (contains public certificate) – client-side – add it using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Server Certificates" /></ac:link> option group in Environment Options.</li></ul><br />To generate a key pair<hr /><ol><li>Go to your <strong>Java </strong>directory and open the folder named <strong>bin</strong>.</li><li>Enter<em> keytool -genkey -alias MyDomain -keyalg RSA -keystore <span>keystore</span>.jks -keysize 2048 </em>and press <strong>Enter</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cd4558d-c979-473d-bf41-edd5de405c45"><ac:rich-text-body>You can give any name to<em> MyDomain</em> and <strong>keystore.jks</strong>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Enter a password for your keystore and answer the questions asked. A certificate with a key is generated and placed into the keystore. </li><li>To extract a certificate without a key, type <em>keytool -export -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore <span>keystore</span>.jks</em> and press <strong>Enter</strong>.</li><li>To place a certificate into another keystore, e.g. <strong>cert.jks</strong>, type <em>keytool -import -alias MyDomain -file certificate.cer -keystore cert.jks</em> and press <strong>Enter</strong>. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4db5da94-0333-4f2f-b369-8d4ec95b4c0d"><ac:rich-text-body>You can give any name to <strong>cert.jks</strong>. However, by default, the tool searches for this exact name.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>Two keystore files are generated: one for the server (<strong>keystore.jks</strong>) and one for the client (<strong>cert.jks</strong>). You can now proceed to enable a secure connection between the client (modeling tool) and the server (Teamwork Cloud). </li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992363 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00091: Enabling the Document View

- page_id: `137992363`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992363/Enabling+the+Document+View
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:09:43.179+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with custom document templates
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Document Viewis the mode of a published project that allows you to view it as a continuous document. Essentially, by using the Document View, you can scroll through the entire content of a project view instead of jumping from one navigation tree element to another as in the Model View. The Document View can be enabled for individual views of a project. After selecting a view, you are presented with the document containing information from that view. The sections of the document are numbered hierarchically both in the Navigation pane and the content pane.

The Document View can be enabled by customizing the template that will be used for publishing or from the title bar of a published project.

To enable the Document View in the template used for publishing

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «view» stereotype. In the View Diagram of the template, each element with the «view» stereotype represents a separate navigation tree of a published project. If your template has more than one such element, you can enable the Document View for each of them separately.
3. In the open Specification window, set the **Document View** property as *true.* [ATTACHMENT filename='view_properties_specification_window.png'] The figure shows the Specification window of the element with the «view» stereotype with the Document View enabled.
4. Click the **Close** button.
5. Save the template file.

The Document View is enabled for the selected view of a published project.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

The **Document View** property can also be enabled in the Specification window of the element with the «Document» stereotype. Doing this overrides the property values set for individual elements with the «view» stereotype.

To enable the Document View from the title bar of a published project

1. In the app bar of a published model, click [ATTACHMENT filename='model_view_list.png'] and select the model view for which you want to enable the Document View mode.
2. On the right side of the app bar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Document View .

The Document View is enabled for the selected navigation tree of a published project.

When the mode is set from the title bar, it's only valid until the refresh of a web portal.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Document View<span class="apple-converted-space"> </span>is the mode of a published project that allows you to view it as a continuous document. Essentially, by using the Document View, you can scroll through the entire content of a project view instead of jumping from one navigation tree element to another as in the Model View. The Document View can be enabled for individual views of a project. After selecting a view, you are presented with the document containing information from that view. The sections of the document are numbered hierarchically both in the Navigation pane and the content pane.</p><p>The Document View can be enabled by <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="80f36ffe-9a5d-4b86-b967-16639c0ea136">customizing the template</ac:inline-comment-marker> that will be used for publishing or from the title bar of a published project.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Document View in the template used for publishing</p><hr style="" /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b956ced9-c860-4226-b0c3-8a64af1514e2">Open the View Diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker> of the template that you intend to use for publishing.</li><li><p>Double-click the element with the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e8db20d-74b9-4595-a134-9b0e28657d36">«view» stereotype</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26f4b52b-1f5e-45e4-b3a0-c01e43945ffb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the View Diagram of the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ee1e1072-7e6d-44f4-a718-d4a3021b7a5f">template,</ac:inline-comment-marker> each element with the «view» stereotype represents a separate navigation tree of a published project. If your template has more than one such element, you can enable the Document View for each of them separately.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>In the open Specification <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b1187258-8a81-40be-a3c8-f4ec80913960">window,</ac:inline-comment-marker> set the <strong>Document View</strong> property as <em>true.<br /> </em></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="view_properties_specification_window.png" /></ac:image><br />The figure shows the Specification window of the element with the «view» stereotype with the Document View enabled.<br /><br /></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</p></li><li>Save the template file.</li></ol><p>The Document View is enabled for the selected view of a published project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1858f022-fb12-4b14-a1da-6c364d39ca0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Document View</strong> property can also be enabled in the Specification window of the element with the «Document» stereotype. Doing this overrides the property values set for individual elements with the «view» stereotype.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Document View from the title bar of a published project</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>In the app bar of a published <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e0885b7d-63de-4cfc-9843-3ece468c268b">model,</ac:inline-comment-marker> click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_view_list.png" /></ac:image> and select the model view for which you want to enable the Document View mode.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="993662e5-3cbd-410d-a40f-69db4b41c344">On the right side of the app </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="48bc66a3-c9b8-4a0f-869d-243e838788eb"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="993662e5-3cbd-410d-a40f-69db4b41c344">bar,</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="993662e5-3cbd-410d-a40f-69db4b41c344"> click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="993662e5-3cbd-410d-a40f-69db4b41c344"> and select </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="993662e5-3cbd-410d-a40f-69db4b41c344">Document View</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="993662e5-3cbd-410d-a40f-69db4b41c344">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ol><p><br />The Document View is enabled for the selected navigation tree of a published project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9403120-b3ac-4031-8451-d2d1e4e2b109"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When the mode is set from the title bar, it's only valid until the refresh of a web portal.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992371 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00092: Enabling the Single Tree View

- page_id: `137992371`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992371/Enabling+the+Single+Tree+View
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:10:14.461+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Working with custom document templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views.

To enable the Single Tree View

1. Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.
2. Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.
3. In the open Specification window, set the Single Tree View property as true .
4. Click the Close button.
5. Save the template file.

When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Single Tree View is the mode of a published model that allows you to render different model views as the nodes of a single navigation tree. This mode enables you to navigate a document without having to switch between different views. </p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the Single Tree View</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>Open the View Diagram of the template that you intend to use for publishing.</li><li>Double-click the element with the «Document» stereotype.</li><li>In the open Specification window, set the <strong>Single Tree View</strong> property as <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Close</strong> button.</li><li>Save the template file.</li></ol><p><br />When you publish a model using this template, all the views of a published model are rendered in a single navigation tree. To access the content of the desired view, simply expand the node of that view in the Navigation pane.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991584 space=MCS2024x version=7 -->
## PAGE 00093: Encrypting property values

- page_id: `137991584`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991584/Encrypting+property+values
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:47:19.212+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Configuration > Hardening Magic Collaboration Studio
- labels: ['encrypt-properties', 'property-encryption']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

##### Generating keys for encryption and decryption

By default, properties are stored as plain text. If required, any of these properties can be encrypted. For property encryption and decryption, you need to generate a pair of keys by using our property encryption tool. The tool is provided as the *encryptor.jar* file and the steps below explain how to use it.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

The encryption tool uses Java 11. Therefore, the system **PATH** variable needs to point to the location of the *bin*folder of the Java 11 home directory, for example, *C:\Java\Java11\bin* or */etc/alternatives/jre_11*.

#### NOTE: Note on special characters

Note on special characters

Encrypted properties (like passwords) can include special characters, such as exclamation marks, ampersands, or quotes. These characters must be escaped because the shell that runs the command interprets them within the property and does not invoke the encryptor.The characters that need to be escaped and the escape method depend on the command line shell used to run the command, socheck your shell documentation on how to escape the property value if it contains special characters.

The following examples use the property value **!&**:

- Invoking the command in the Linux bash shell: texttrue

- Invoking the command in the Windows Command Shell: texttrue

To encrypt properties

1. Click the link to download the [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.jar'] file.
2. Open the command-line interface and navigate to the directory where the encryptor.jar file is located.
3. Run the java -jar encryptor.jar command. You should see the information on how to use the tool as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor_open.png']
4. Run the **java -jar encryptor.jar generate-keys <path_to_folder_for_keys>** command to generate a pair of keys for property encryption and decryption. Make sure to replace the**<path_to_folder_for_keys>**placeholder with the actual path to the directory where you want to store the keys.You should see a confirmation that the keys have been successfully generated. Keys for encryption and decryptionAfter executing the command, the following keys are created in the specified directory:**propertiesEncryptionKey.pub** - a public key used to encrypt property values.**propertiesEncryptionKey** - a private key used to decrypt property values.
5. To encrypt a property, run the java -jar encryptor.jar encrypt <unencrypted_property_value> <path_to_the public_key> command. Make sure to replace the <unencrypted_property_value> and <path_to_the public_key> placeholders with the actual property value and the path to the propertiesEncryptionKey.pub file. The tool will output the encrypted property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='encryptor.png']

##### Configuring Teamwork Cloud encrypted property values

To configure Teamwork Cloud encrypted property values

1. Go to the <install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio/TeamworkCloud/configuration/ directory and edit the application.conf file.
2. In the *application.conf*file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Cassandra password, should look similar to this one:**password=ENC("lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==")**.
3. In the same *application.conf* file, add the **esi.config.decrypt_key_file******property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **esi.config.decrypt_key_file=*<install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio/**TeamworkCloud/keys/**propertiesEncryptionKey***. Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in the location which Teamwork Cloud can access.
4. Restart Teamwork Cloud service.

##### Configuring Web Application Platform encrypted property values

To configure Web Application Platform encrypted values

1. Go to the <install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf directory and open the webappplatform.properties file.
2. In the *webappplatform.properties* file, replace the actual property value with the encrypted property value in the following format: **ENC(encrypted_property_value)**. Example of an encrypted propertyFor example, an encrypted Teamwork Cloud administrator password, should look similar to this one:**twc.admin.password=ENC(lcvPbmmI32vn1jD2EYrQfMLu7ydX+/DW8wljMsk/ 
+UcjrPWXELau1YC1FFTa3UBMptu3sFK6wjOuLipveVBJYzo0k+yfgt1qnD1ud/3E7LsRGwMe4srlAIF7Kfq36pPywTu58NBYp6M8yktIDTtKtQSvuZF8Qcb38Vw56OkGCd9Io+vdM5aTN 
TNi2ls7jnN09BuqD6lZvwJ/atcrd0grfEhxwM3PSw0zzUS+EenjWedTxogxpfXLtUbTEuzwYSMGqyi5goX9Wb3vnkMPs7xlL/ZhqYYIQCXwQaREbzLIH3oJEHJxRvUpFIhWJoc/7LJfn/09ukx0wjgF296kEgI+rA==)**.
3. Go to the <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices/WebAppPlatform/conf directory and open the catalina.properties file.
4. In the *catalina.properties* file, add the **properties.encryption.private.key.location** property and specify the path to the private key (the*propertiesEncryptionKey* file) as its value, e.g., **properties.encryption.private.key.location=*<install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio/WebApp**Platform/conf/keys/propertiesEncryptionKey****.* Private key locationThe private key (the *propertiesEncryptionKey* file) should be in the location which Web Application Platform can access.
5. Restart Web Application Platform service.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991394 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00094: Enterprise deployment

- page_id: `137991394`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991394/Enterprise+deployment
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:43:45.137+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.

To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The enterprise deployment option for Web Application Platform and its services.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Web Application Platform as well as all of its plugins are provided as separate web applications. Therefore, it is possible to containerize applications and this way achieve better performance and scalability.</p><p>To learn how to containerize Web Application Platform applications using Docker, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="41af73a6-14d0-4578-bdbe-3b0367264fd1" /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="WAP_enterprise_deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The enterprise deployment option for Web Application Platform and its services.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992655 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00095: Exporting comments to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `137992655`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992655/Exporting+comments+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:54:33.013+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to export comments to the PDF/HTML format. This way you can archive the discussions among team members that resulted in specific changes made in the model and thus trace the reasoning behind these changes. The exported files include not only all comments and replies created in the document but also the document and project information and comments statistics as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Cameo Collaborator comments exported to PDF.

To export Cameo Collaborator comments to PDF or HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document containing the comments you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on right side of the app bar and in the open menu select Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Comments .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the comments in the document's open section, which is selected in the Navigation pane.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose from which elements of the active section comments are exported. [ATTACHMENT filename='exporting_form_active_section_peresntation_elements.png']
  - Active view - exports the comments in the open view of the document which is selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='App bar' space=''] .
  - Whole document - exports all the comments in the document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. In the second step of the wizard, choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
7. If needed, enable the following export options:
  - Turn on the Cover page switch to add a cover page to the target document. A cover page contains the Cameo Collaborator document name, export date, project and document information.
  - Turn on the Table of contents switch to add a page with the table of contents to the target document. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_comment_export_properties.png']
8. Click the Finish button.

After the export is complete and the document with comments is generated, you will get a success message with the **Download** button allowing you to download the document. Note that exporting a large number of comments may take a while, so be patient while the document is being generated.

#### TIP: Task Manager

Task Manager

You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing long-running tasks' space=''] to check the comment export status and download the generated document.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992641 space=MCS2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00096: Exporting documents to PDF and HTML

- page_id: `137992641`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992641/Exporting+documents+to+PDF+and+HTML
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:53:51.506+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud allows you to export an open document or a part of it to PDF and HTML formats.

#### TIP: Customizing PDF templates

Customizing PDF templates

PDF templates are customizable and can be modified according to your needs. You can find them in the *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/**shared/conf/data/collaborator/pdf*directory.

#### NOTE: Limitations

Limitations

By default, you can export up to 400 sections to PDF and up to 3000 sections to HTML.

To change the export to PDF/HTML limits:

1. Open the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties file.
2. Do one or both of the following:
  - Add the export.limit.size.pdf property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to PDF as its value (the default value is 400).
  - Add the export.limit.size.html property to the file and specify the maximum number of sections to be exported to HTML as its value (the default value is 3000).
3. Restart Web Application Platform.

To export a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF/HTML

1. Open the Cameo Collaborator document you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on right side of the app bar and select an Export document to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, click the Type drop-down box and select Document .
4. Click the Document scope drop-down box and select one of the following scope options:
  - Active section - exports the open section of the document which is selected in the Navigation pane.
  - Active section's presentation elements - selecting this option allows you to choose which elements of the active section should be exported as displayed below. [ATTACHMENT filename='exporting_active_section_elements.png']
  - Active view - exports the open view of the document selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='App bar' space=''] .
  - Whole document - exports the whole document.
5. Click the Next button.
6. In the second step of the wizard, choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button. [ATTACHMENT filename='export_properties.png']
7. If needed, enable the following export options:
  - Turn on the Cover page switch to add a cover page to the generated document. A cover page contains the document name, export date, and project and document information.
  - Turn on the **Table of contents** switch to add a page with the table of contents to the generated document. You cannot add the table of contents when exporting documents to the HTML format.
8. Click the Image numberin g drop-down box and select one of the following options:
  - Generate automatically - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.
  - Use current numbering - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.
9. Click the Section numbering drop-down box and select one of the following options:
  - Automatic numbering - all sections in the generated document are numbered automatically.
  - None - sections in the generated document are not numbered.
10. Click the **Break page at** drop-down box and select the heading after which page breaks are added in the generated document. You cannot add page breaks when exporting documents to the HTML format.
11. Click the Finish button.

After completing the above steps, the generated PDF or HTML document is downloaded to your computer. Note that exporting a large document may take a while, so be patient while the document is being generated.

##### Installing additional fonts

If your Cameo Collaborator document uses fonts that are different from the default font used in documents, you need to install Microsoft TrueType core fonts as described below. Otherwise, the exported PDF/HTML files may not support these fonts.

Additional fonts must be installed on the server on which the document exporter runs.

To install additional fonts

1. In the command-line interface, execute the following commands:
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting and Stopping Web Application Platform' space=''].

The URLs used in the commands above are subject to change. Always make sure that you install the latest versions of fonts.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992649 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00097: Exporting tables

- page_id: `137992649`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992649/Exporting+tables
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-01-12T13:54:24.881+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Exporting documents to PDF and HTML
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

You can export Generic, Instance, and custom tables in a Cameo Collaborator document to PDF, HTML, or Excel.

##### Exporting tables to PDF or HTML

To export a table to PDF or HTML

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the top right corner of the table and select Export to PDF or HTML .
3. In the open wizard, choose the format of the target document by selecting the **PDF** or **HTML** radio button.
4. Optionally, change the following export options:
  - Turn on the Cover page switch to add a cover page to the generated document. A cover page contains the Cameo Collaborator document name, export date, and project and document information.
  - Turn on the **Table of contents** switch to add a page with the table of contents to the target document. You cannot add the table of contents when exporting documents to the HTML format.
5. Click the Image numbering drop-down box and select one of the following options:
  - Generate automatically - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.
  - Use current numbering - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.
6. Click the Finish button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The wizard for exporting tables to PDF or HTML.

##### Exporting tables to Excel

To export a table to Excel

1. In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='menu.png'] on the top right corner of the table and select Export to Excel . [ATTACHMENT filename='exporting_tables_to_excel.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A cover page contains the Cameo Collaborator document name, export date, and project and document information.</li><li><p>Turn on the <strong>Table of contents</strong> switch to add a page with the table of contents to the target document.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="111ca7e7-0df0-4219-9fee-7538f15b2893"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You cannot add the table of contents when exporting documents to the HTML format.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Image numbering</strong> drop-down box and select one of the following options:<ul><li><strong>Generate automatically</strong> - all images in the generated document are numbered automatically while disregarding any image numbers that were changed manually.</li><li><strong>Use current numbering</strong> - all images in the generated document are numbered the same way as in the Cameo Collaborator document.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_tables_to_pdf_html.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The wizard for exporting tables to PDF or HTML.</h6><h3 style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br />Exporting tables to Excel</h3><p><br /></p><p>To export a table to Excel</p><hr style="" /><ol><li>In a Cameo Collaborator document, navigate to the table you want to export.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="menu.png" /></ac:image> on the top right corner of the table and select <strong>Export to Excel</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_tables_to_excel.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 00099: File locations

- page_id: `166397376`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/166397376/File+locations
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-16T13:37:24.917+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > FAQ and Troubleshooting
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

The default locations for log and configuration files are listed on this page from a standard server installation.

###### Installation paths

| PACKAGE | OS | VERSION | INSTALLATION DIRECTORY |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Teamwork Cloud | Linux | 22xR2, 24x | <install_root>/CATIANoMagicServices |
| 22xR1 and older | <install_root>/TeamworkCloud |  |  |
| Windows | 22xR2, 24x | <install_root>\CATIANoMagicServices |  |
| 22xR1 and older | <install_root>\TeamworkCloud |  |  |
| Magic Collaboration Studio | Linux | All | <install_root>/MagicCollaborationStudio |
| Windows | All | <install_root>\MagicCollaborationStudio |  |

CATIANoMagicServices

TeamworkCloud

MagicCollaborationStudio

<install_root>

###### Web Application Platform

Log files (e.g., *webapp.log*, *collaborator.log*, etc.) 
Linux:*<install_root>**/WebAppPlatform/logs/webappplatform*Windows:*<install_root>**\WebAppPlatform\logs\webappplatform*

Configuration 
Linux: *<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties* 
Windows: *<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties*

###### Magic Collaboration Studio

Log file for 2024x and newer 
Linux*: <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/logs/server.log***Windows:*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\logs\**server.log*

Configuration 
Linux:*<install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf* 
Windows:*<install_root>\TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf*

Log file for earlier versions 
Linux:*<user.home>/.twcloud/<version>/server.log* 
Windows:*C:\Users\<user>\.twcloud\<version>\server.log*

###### Authentication server

Log file for 2022x and newer 
Linux:*<install_root>**/WebAppPlatform/logs/webappplatform/authentication.log* 
Windows:*<install_root>\**WebAppPlatform\logs\webappplatform\authentication.log*

Configuration for 2022x and newer 
Linux:*<install_root>/WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties* 
Windows:*<install_root>\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties*

Log file for earlier versions 
Linux:*<user.home>**/.authserver/<version>/authserver.log* 
Windows:*C:\Users\<user>\.authserver\<version>\authserver.log*

Configuration for earlier versions 
Linux:*<install_root>**/AuthServer/config/authserver.properties* 
Windows:*<install_root>\**AuthServer\config\authserver.properties*

###### Cassandra

Log file 
Linux: */var/log/cassandra/system.log*

Configuration filesLinux:*/etc/cassandra/conf*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
