If Cassandra has not started yet, you will get the error: &quot;No nodes present in the cluster. Has this node finished starting up?&quot; This means that you need to give Cassandra more time to start.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>If Apache Cassandra is not running or if you used installation options other than the one described in this chapter, optionally <ac:link ac:anchor="Configuring Apache Cassandra (optional)"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[configure Apache Cassandra]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h3>Configuring Apache Cassandra (optional)</h3><p>If you used other installation options and not the provided script or if Apache Cassandra does not start, configure it as described below.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54cf37b4-fecc-428a-b542-171d628f71bb"><ac:rich-text-body>Before starting, note that you do not need to configure Apache Cassandra if you installed it using the installation script we provided (<em>install_cassandra&lt;version_number&gt;_&lt;os_version&gt;.sh</em>). It should start without any additional configuration.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To configure Apache Cassandra</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Edit the <em>cassandra.yaml</em> file by executing the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d775661d-8baa-4497-89c2-854a6800b726"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo nano /etc/cassandra/default.conf/cassandra.yaml]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the following parameters related to the Cassandra node IP address and communication settings, and change them as shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1b6a9752-4694-4d9c-ab86-1b951b51e430"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[seeds: "192.168.130.10"
````html
listen_address: 192.168.130.10
broadcast_rpc_address: 192.168.130.10
rpc_address: 0.0.0.0]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><strong>seeds</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">a comma-delimited list containing all of the seeds in the Cassandra cluster. Since our cluster consists of a single node, it contains only one entry - our IP address.</span></li><li><strong>listen address</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the IP address that Cassandra uses to listen for connections.</span></li><li><strong>broadcast_rcp_address</strong> - <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the IP address used to broadcast to other Cassandra nodes in the cluster. This parameter may be commented. In such case, remove &quot;#&quot; and make sure there are no leading spaces.</span></li><li><strong>rcp_address</strong> - when set to <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to </span>0.0.0.0, Cassandra listens <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to rpc requests on all interfaces.</span></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Find the following parameters that <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">control thresholds to ensure that the data being sent is processed properly, and change them as shown below:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4cb4630-dcd0-428b-a2e8-b52de1f74a28"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[commitlog_segment_size_in_mb: 192
read_request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
range_request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
write_request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
cas_contention_timeout_in_ms: 1000
truncate_request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
batch_size_warn_threshold_in_kb: 3000
batch_size_fail_threshold_in_kb: 5000]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To ensure that the default commit log size is 8GB (recommended), uncomment the </span><strong>commitlog_total_space_in_mb</strong> parameter as <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">show as below.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d71783fa-d7d3-4d31-9f6c-2b987cbe5147"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[commitlog_total_space_in_mb: 8192]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cff29604-57d5-48a5-8e32-b1435084a97c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Ensure that the partition where the commit log is installed has enough space to accommodate a commit log of 8GB.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To point the data to the appropriate locations, find the following parameters and change them as shown below:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5657b9ba-2980-4ecc-a772-a8c5642c99c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[data_file_directories:
- /data/data
commitlog_directory: /logs/commitlog
hints_directory: /data/hints
saved_caches_directory: /data/saved_caches]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Start Apache Cassandra by executing the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="489be1f4-0a8b-4c6e-b865-bed3f911251a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl start cassandra]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Check if Apache Cassandra is running by executing the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="592c72ee-1452-4673-b3d6-1db92d1e6336"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[nodetool status]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">If Apache Cassandra is running, you should receive the output displayed below. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If the service is fully operational, the first 2 characters of the last line are &quot;</span>UN<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">&quot;, indicating that the node status is </span>U<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">p, and its state is </span>N<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ormal.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dec272aa-e4fa-47a2-80ca-5fb891e9ae13"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Datacenter: datacenter1
=======================
Status=Up/Down
|/ State=Normal/Leaving/Joining/Moving
--  Address    Load       Tokens   Owns (effective)  Host ID                               Rack
UN  127.0.0.1  128.4 KB   256      100.0%            ea3f99eb-c4ad-4d13-95a1-80aec71b750f  rack1]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Configuring Cassandra JVM node (optional)</span></h3><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you did not use the installation script or want to increase the RAM usage by Cassandra, make the following changes. Otherwise, these configuration changes are set automatically by the Cassandra installation script.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc5f84aa-7fdb-4c4f-aabf-15667fd60303"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Configuration files are located in <em>/etc/cassandra/conf/</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">By default, the maximum RAM usage for Cassandra is 8GB. To change the amount of RAM used by Cassandra, uncomment <strong>-Xms4G (min)</strong> and <strong>-Xmx4G (max)</strong> in the <em>jvm-server.options</em> file and specify their values.</span></li><li>In the <em>jvm11-server.options</em> and <em>jvm8-server.options</em> files, comment all lines from&quot;### CMS Settings&quot; to &quot;### G1 Settings&quot;.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>jvm11-server.options</em> and <em>jvm8-server.options</em> files, uncomment the following lines:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="285ad60f-a05b-4542-8840-20a9bf740553"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#-XX:+UseG1GC
#-XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=500]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <em>jvm11-server.options</em> and <em>jvm8-server.options</em> files, uncomment the following lines and set the values to the physical CPU core count (the values of both parameters should be the same):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="655e3936-4277-4fee-aa05-fccf7d45373d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#-XX:ParallelGCThreads=8
#-XX:ConcGCThreads=8]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>In the <em>jvm8-server.options</em> file, comment all lines from &quot;### GC logging options&quot; to the end of the file.</li><li><p>Synchronize CPU clocks on all Cassandra cluster nodes. Otherwise, you may encounter issues when creating an empty Cassandra cluster.</p></li><li>When using cqlsh, use Python 3.6.0 or a later version. Python 2.7 series is no longer supported.</li><li>In the logback.xml file, comment the &quot;&lt;appender-ref ref=&quot;ASYNCDEBUGLOG&quot; /&gt;&quot; line. This will increase Cassandra's performance by disabling the debug log.</li></ul><h3>Tuning Linux to improve Apache Cassandra performance</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fcce1a17-001e-4c1b-9e30-ef82b8f277d8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you install Magic Collaboration Studio using the <em>install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh</em> script, Cassandra performance is tuned automatically. However, if you plan to use other installation options or if you need to set other parameters after running the script, you can do it manually as described in this section.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To improve Apache Cassandra performance</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open the <em>sysctl.conf</em> file by executing the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c9fbe2b-1f6f-4cab-81bc-7a7e723b9be4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo nano /etc/sysctl.conf]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To configure the TCP settings, add the following tuning parameters to the file:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00737da0-3dba-4b4c-9e5b-dd7b56cba87d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[net.core.rmem_max=16777216
net.core.wmem_max=16777216
net.core.optmem_max=40960
net.core.default_qdisc=fq
net.core.somaxconn=4096
net.ipv4.conf.all.arp_notify = 1
net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_time=60
net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_probes=3
net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_intvl=10
net.ipv4.tcp_mtu_probing=1
net.ipv4.tcp_rmem=4096 12582912 16777216 
net.ipv4.tcp_wmem=4096 12582912 16777216 
net.ipv4.tcp_max_syn_backlog=8096
net.ipv4.tcp_slow_start_after_idle = 0
net.ipv4.tcp_tw_reuse = 1 
vm.max_map_count = 1048575
vm.swappiness = 0
vm.dirty_background_ratio=5
vm.dirty_ratio=80
vm.dirty_expire_centisecs = 12000]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To apply the setting without rebooting, execute the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="401860c9-24af-44d0-b43a-0d1df77494d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo sysctl -p]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>For more information about tuning Linux, see <a href="https://docs.datastax.com/en/dse/6.8/dse-admin/datastax_enterprise/config/configRecommendedSettings.html">DSE 6.8 Administrator Guide</a>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137992376 space=MCS2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00122: Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin

- page_id: `137992376`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137992376/Installing+Cameo+Collaborator+Publisher+plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-12-11T15:18:14.032+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > User Guide > Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud > Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To publish models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin in your modeling tool.

#### WARNING: Compatibility

Compatibility

- The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin is only compatible with the same version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud.
- Make sure the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin version is compatible with the version of your modeling tool. See the compatibility table .

#### NOTE: Cameo Collaborator and 3DEXPERIENCE platform

Cameo Collaborator and 3DEXPERIENCE platform

If [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration in a modeling tool' space='MD2024x'], The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin is disabled in the modeling tool, and you cannot work with Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud.

For information regarding installation, licensing, and system requirements, visit the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='IL2024x'] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To publish models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin in your modeling tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e30a40f-77ee-403b-876b-18c68c81d94f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Compatibility</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin is only compatible with the same version of Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud.</li><li>Make sure the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin version is compatible with the version of your modeling tool. See the <a href="https://www.nomagic.com/support/compatibility#Cameo%20Collaborator%20Publisher%20plugin" style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-blue-bolder,#0c66e4);">compatibility table</a>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d91ba1c8-fbaf-4f6b-8bc8-b511516d007b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Cameo Collaborator and 3DEXPERIENCE platform</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Enabling 3DEXPERIENCE collaboration in a modeling tool" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[collaboration powered by the 3DEXPERIENCE platform is enabled in the Environment Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, The Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin is disabled in the modeling tool, and you cannot work with Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For information regarding installation, licensing, and system requirements, visit the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024x" ri:content-title="Installation, licensing, and system requirements" /></ac:link> page.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=275415345 space=MCS2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00123: Installing Cassandra on Windows

- page_id: `275415345`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/275415345/Installing+Cassandra+on+Windows
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:40:31.564+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio Installation > Installation on Windows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**Downloads**

**[ATTACHMENT filename='CustomTWC_Cassandra4_DockerImg.zip']**

#### WARNING: Important! Cassandra is not supported on Windows!

Important! Cassandra is not supported on Windows!

We do not provide support for Cassandra installations on Windows as those installations are not natively supported by Apache. **We strongly recommend using Linux deployments.**The information below is provided as a courtesy only.

One option for running Cassandra 4.0.3 on Windows is Docker. See [Install Docker Desktop on Windows](https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/windows-install/) for official installation instructions.

Additional resources:

- https://hub.docker.com/_/cassandra/ – official Cassandra Docker image
- https://docs.docker.com/ – Docker documentation
- https://github.com/apache/cassandra/blob/cassandra-4.0/NEWS.txt – Cassandra’s migration notes

##### **Docker Cassandra basics**

This section provides the basics for starting a Docker Cassandra 4.0.3 container, for testing purposes only. For production deployment, we recommend generating a custom Docker image as described in the section .

Start an instance of the official Cassandra 4.0.3 Docker image by executing this command:

```bash

```

| Parameter | Value | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| --name | cassandra4_test | User-defined container instance name. This is an optional parameter. The name must be unique and cannot be the same as the official Docker image name. |
| -v | e:/cassandra/data/:/var/lib/cassandra | Mount an external drive to a path in the Docker container instance. /var/lib/cassandra is the default Cassandra path for data and commit log storage. An external drive must be mounted for permanent data storage. |
| -d |  | Detach and run in the background. |
| -p | 9042:9042 | Publish port 9042 to Windows host. 9042 is the native client port. |
| [image name]:version | cassandra:4.0.3 | Pull the official Apache Cassandra 4.0.3 Docker image for this running instance. |

If this is a new Docker installation, the first **docker run** execution downloads the Cassandra image to the local system.

To access logs:

```bash
]]>
```

To access nodetool (or other tools):

```bash
nodetool status]]>
```

Newer Java versions require the **-Dcom.sun.jndi.rmiURLParsing=legacy** option. If the “Malformed IPv6 address” error occurs, use:

```bash
nodetool -Dcom.sun.jndi.rmiURLParsing=legacy status]]>
```

Docker instances are ephemeral by design. If an instance terminates or is deleted, all data, logs, and configuration files modified inside the instance are lost.

##### **Custom Docker Cassandra image for Magic Collaboration Studio**

Production deployment of Cassandra 4.0.3 on Windows requires modifying system configurations. Therefore, a custom Docker image must be created with the configuration changes. This section covers setting up and running a custom Docker image. Details of the configuration changes are discussed in section .

1. Download and extract the [ATTACHMENT filename='CustomTWC_Cassandra4_DockerImg.zip'] package into an empty working directory.
2. Execute the following command to build the custom Docker image named *cassandra4_twc*: bashDJangotrue
3. Start an instance of the custom Docker Cassandra 4.0.3 image you just built: bashDJangotrue The data and commit log directories are mounted separately, to different paths.

With the custom Docker Cassandra instance running and mounted to permanent storage, the database is ready to connect to your Magic Collaboration Studio installation.

If more configuration changes are needed, the running instance has to be terminated and a new Docker image needs to be built. Edit the configuration file from the working directory in Step 1. Proceed with steps 2-3 to redeploy the Cassandra node on Windows.

##### **Cassandra configuration**

This section is a summary of the configuration changes to optimize Docker Cassandra deployment for Magic Collaboration Studio. If additional configuration changes are needed, the Docker Cassandra image has to be rebuilt. All configuration files are located in */etc/cassandra*.

Synchronize the CPU clocks on all Cassandra cluster nodes. Otherwise, you may encounter issues when creating an empty Cassandra cluster.

###### Modification of the *cassandra.yaml* file

If the new Cassandra 4.x node is going to access existing data from a Cassandra 3.x database, please make a note of the previous **cluster_name** and **num_tokens**settings. These parameters must match in the new Cassandra 4.0.3 *cassandra.yaml* configuration.

The following parameters are for controlling thresholds to ensure that the data being sent is processed properly.

```text

```

Ensure that the default commit log size is 8GB (recommended).

```text

```

Point the data to the appropriate locations.

```text

```

###### JVM configuration for Cassandra Node

To change the amount of RAM used by Cassandra, activate the -Xms4G and -Xmx4g properties in *jvm-server.options* (remove leading ‘#’) and specify their values. By default, Cassandra uses no more than 8 GB of RAM.

- In the jvm11-server.options and jvm8-server.options files, CMS Settings are disabled.
- In the jvm11-server.options and jvm8-server.options files, the parameters below are activated

```text

```

- In the jvm11-server.options and jvm8-server.options files, the two parameters below are set to the physical CPU core count (the values of both parameters should be the same):

```text

```

- In the jvm8-server.options file, disable all GC logging options.
- In the logback.xml file, disable debug.log by commenting out "<appender-ref ref="ASYNCDEBUGLOG" />"

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5def57e-211a-467e-802c-8dd688e3c0a5" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Downloads</strong></p><p><strong><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="CustomTWC_Cassandra4_DockerImg.zip" /></ac:link></strong></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd476f02-b3fd-4b2e-8de2-dbc909dd4aab"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important! Cassandra is not supported on Windows!</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>We do not provide support for Cassandra installations on Windows as those installations are not natively supported by Apache. <strong>We strongly recommend using Linux deployments. </strong>The information below is provided as a courtesy only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">O</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ne option for running Cassandra 4.0.3 on Windows is Docker. See <a href="https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/windows-install/">Install Docker Desktop on Windows</a> for official installation instructions</span>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb6a4db7-8911-4629-a4fd-65794d703b8f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Additional resources:</p><ul><li data-uuid="84c2e79d-858d-4cc7-83f8-fd6e4aa000e9"><a class="external-link" href="https://hub.docker.com/_/cassandra/">https://hub.docker.com/_/cassandra/</a><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">–</span></span> official Cassandra Docker image</li><li data-uuid="f7924188-1a0c-4ab6-822e-536f334d6045"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.docker.com/">https://docs.docker.com/</a><span> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">–</span></span> Docker documentation</li><li data-uuid="96003c21-d73d-4dd4-b443-1f246eb7df24"><a href="https://github.com/apache/cassandra/blob/cassandra-4.0/NEWS.txt" rel="nofollow">https://github.com/apache/cassandra/blob/cassandra-4.0/NEWS.txt</a> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-purple-bolder,#352c63);">–</span> Cassandra’s migration notes</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong>Docker Cassandra basics</strong></h3><p><span>This section provides the basics for starting a Docker Cassandra 4.0.3 container, for testing purposes only. For production deployment, we recommend generating a custom Docker image as described in the section <ac:link ac:anchor="Custom Docker Cassandra image for Magic Collaboration Studio" />.</span></p><p><span>Start an instance of the official Cassandra 4.0.3 Docker image by executing this command:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93b36dcb-6670-47a7-b42a-201e8de1defb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker run --name cassandra4_test –p 9042:9042 –v e:\cassandra\data:/var/lib/cassandra -d cassandra:4.0.3]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""> <col class="" /> <col class="" /> <col class="" /> </colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Parameter</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Value</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>--name</p></td><td><p>cassandra4_test</p></td><td><p>User-defined container instance name. This is an optional parameter. The name must be unique and cannot be the same as the official Docker image name.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-v</p></td><td><p>e:/cassandra/data/:/var/lib/cassandra</p></td><td><p>Mount an external drive to a path in the Docker container instance. <em>/var/lib/cassandra</em> is the default Cassandra path for data and commit log storage. An external drive must be mounted for permanent data storage.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-d</p></td><td><br /></td><td><p>Detach and run in the background.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>-p</p></td><td><p>9042:9042</p></td><td><p>Publish port 9042 to Windows host. 9042 is the native client port.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><p>[image name]:version</p></td><td><p>cassandra:4.0.3</p></td><td><p>Pull the official Apache Cassandra 4.0.3 Docker image for this running instance.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="407abb9f-f047-4bde-b701-4c0df16c5e8c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If this is a new Docker installation, the first <strong>docker run</strong> execution downloads the Cassandra image to the local system.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>To access logs:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ee4f42b-5383-4f6c-8263-8ecdb77c87d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker logs <container name or ID>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>To access nodetool (or other tools):</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61c9a14a-ca73-47d7-aba3-e4fcd7b5db55"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec -it <container name or ID> nodetool status]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Newer Java versions require the <strong>-Dcom.sun.jndi.rmiURLParsing=legacy</strong> option. If the “Malformed IPv6 address” error occurs, use:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d819407-fb99-492e-b896-103aada6fb21"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker exec -it <container name or ID> nodetool -Dcom.sun.jndi.rmiURLParsing=legacy status]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5efaa53d-82fd-4b6e-9307-dd84497bfabc"><ac:rich-text-body>Docker instances are ephemeral by design. If an instance terminates or is deleted, all data, logs, and configuration files modified inside the instance are lost.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong>Custom Docker Cassandra image for Magic Collaboration Studio</strong></h3><p><span>Production deployment of Cassandra 4.0.3 on Windows requires modifying system configurations. Therefore, a custom Docker image must be created with the configuration changes. This section covers setting up and running a custom Docker image. Details of the configuration changes are discussed in section <ac:link ac:anchor="Cassandra configuration" />.</span></p><ol><li data-uuid="4d2a7a09-f9c4-4d40-bf70-29b455539767"><span>Download and extract the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="CustomTWC_Cassandra4_DockerImg.zip" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ZIP file]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> package into an empty working directory.</span></li><li data-uuid="2a5160ff-4e55-4cd4-b61f-7ce9fc446872"><p><span>Execute the following command to build the custom Docker image named <em>cassandra4_twc</em>:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="286a7979-36eb-4e4b-b28e-6e8a59974b1a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker build –t cassandra4_twc:latest .]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="78a11012-3bd8-4b9b-8521-81b648c74a5b"><p><span>Start an instance of the custom Docker Cassandra 4.0.3 image you just built:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3904ca1e-101c-4e70-aa0f-15bb864a354f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[docker run –p 9042:9042 –v d:\data:/data –v e:\log\commitlog:/logs/commitlog -d cassandra4_twc:latest]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4106f393-3cdb-47a7-8d9c-657730c6316c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The data and commit log directories are mounted separately, to different paths.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><span>With the custom Docker Cassandra instance running and mounted to permanent storage, the database is ready to connect to your Magic Collaboration Studio installation.</span></p><p><span>If more configuration changes are needed, the running instance has to be terminated and a new Docker image needs to be built. Edit the configuration file from the working directory in Step 1. Proceed with steps 2-3 to redeploy the Cassandra node on Windows.</span></p><h3><strong>Cassandra configuration</strong></h3><p><span>This section is a summary of the configuration changes to optimize Docker Cassandra deployment for Magic Collaboration Studio. If additional configuration changes are needed, the Docker Cassandra image has to be rebuilt. All configuration files are located in <em>/etc/cassandra</em>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="44bae19f-b953-4a3b-92fe-e38c5a186636"><ac:rich-text-body>Synchronize the CPU clocks on all Cassandra cluster nodes. Otherwise, you may encounter issues when creating an empty Cassandra cluster.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span>Modification of the <em>cassandra.yaml</em> file</span></h4><p><span>If the new Cassandra 4.x node is going to access existing data from a Cassandra 3.x database, please make a note of the previous <strong>cluster_name</strong> and <strong>num_tokens </strong>settings. These parameters must match in the new Cassandra 4.0.3 <em>cassandra.yaml</em> configuration.</span></p><p><span>The following parameters are for controlling thresholds to ensure that the data being sent is processed properly.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a05dd85d-cc29-4cf1-b1ef-a96976e210a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[commitlog_segment_size_in_mb: 192
read_request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
range_request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
write_request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
cas_contention_timeout_in_ms: 1000
truncate_request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
request_timeout_in_ms: 1800000
batch_size_warn_threshold_in_kb: 3000
batch_size_fail_threshold_in_kb: 5000]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Ensure that the default commit log size is 8GB (recommended).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12d1b3ff-b2c4-4df7-99ef-e18aa0f06c49"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[commitlog_total_space_in_mb: 8192]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Point the data to the appropriate locations.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e09c508-0aca-4a7f-9f34-32c24dbc41a4"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[data_file_directories:
- /data/data
commitlog_directory: /logs/commitlog
hints_directory: /data/hints
saved_caches_directory: /data/saved_caches]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>JVM configuration for Cassandra Node</h4><p>To change the amount of RAM used by Cassandra, activate the -Xms4G and -Xmx4g properties in <em>jvm-server.options</em> (remove leading ‘#’) and specify their values. By default, Cassandra uses no more than 8 GB of RAM.</p><ul><li data-uuid="3c50b2b3-538a-45cf-93b9-e83e2a35b52b">In the <em>jvm11-server.options </em>and <em>jvm8-server.options</em> files, CMS Settings are disabled.</li><li data-uuid="a7099dc4-d9f2-42ac-bc70-e7efa834b150">In the <em>jvm11-server.options </em>and <em>jvm8-server.options</em> files, the parameters below are activated</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c10ca81-73af-4413-8d87-84ed98ffffba"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-XX:+UseG1GC
-XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=500]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="108c71e2-82a4-4ccc-96f9-a7f0bda0db60">In the <em>jvm11-server.options </em>and <em>jvm8-server.options</em> files, the two parameters below are set to the physical CPU core count (the values of both parameters should be the same):</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d8bb447-5bfe-41d2-838c-176e83bf2c25"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[-XX:ParallelGCThreads=16
-XX:ConcGCThreads=16]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li data-uuid="6c60659d-f0ff-48fa-ae01-c8e7a641b9e8">In the <em>jvm8-server.options </em>file, disable all GC logging options.</li><li data-uuid="a5d05c80-2253-4a8c-abae-6c4909746238">In the logback.xml file, disable debug.log by commenting out &quot;&lt;appender-ref ref=&quot;ASYNCDEBUGLOG&quot; /&gt;&quot;</li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991291 space=MCS2024x version=5 -->
## PAGE 00124: Installing Magic Collaboration Studio on Windows

- page_id: `137991291`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991291/Installing+Magic+Collaboration+Studio+on+Windows
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:40:38.205+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio Installation > Installation on Windows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section provides instructions on installing Magic Collaboration Studio using either the installer or the zip file. 
Once the installation is complete, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Magic Collaboration Studio license' space=''] before starting to use Magic Collaboration Studio with the modeling tool and Magic Collaboration Studio Admin.

The following sections provide instructions on setting up Magic Collaboration Studio on Windows using the installer or the zip file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This section provides instructions on installing Magic Collaboration Studio using either the installer or the zip file. <br />Once the installation is complete, you need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Magic Collaboration Studio license" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[apply the Magic Collaboration Studio license]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before starting to use Magic Collaboration Studio with the modeling tool and Magic Collaboration Studio <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="22174434-dbe2-421c-a044-d6d784b16392">Admin</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><p><br /></p><p>The following sections provide instructions on setting up Magic Collaboration Studio on Windows using the installer or the zip file.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="06462469-5b89-42ec-9cf9-2e2d6212f709" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991270 space=MCS2024x version=9 -->
## PAGE 00125: Installing services

- page_id: `137991270`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991270/Installing+services
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-03-04T15:09:56.372+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio Installation > Installation on Linux using scripts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter explains how to install Magic Collaboration Studio services by using the *install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh* installation script.

To install Magic Collaboration Studio services

1. Install OpenJDK Java (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Third-Party Component Compatibility' space=''] for version information), if needed. If you used the Apache Cassandra installation script on the same server, then Java is already installed and configured for the system. To install OpenJDK on a new Magic Collaboration Studio server, execute the following command: bashDJangotrue-openjdk]]>
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024x']Download the <*product>_<version_number>_installer_linux64.bin* file and place it in the same location as the install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh script.
3. Install Magic Collaboration Studio by executing the *install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh* script. Example
  1. When executing the installation script, press ENTER until you are asked to configure the machine IP.
  2. Configure the machine IP by entering the local IP address or the host name of the machine (i.e. 192.168.130.10).
  3. Configure the cluster seed node IP by entering the local IP address or the host name of the machine (i.e. 192.168.130.10 ).
  4. Configure the Teamwork Cloud service owner by entering twcloud .
  5. Configure JAVA_HOME by entering /etc/alternatives/jre_11 .
  6. Press **Enter** to choose the default /opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices installation folder or type another installation folder address*.*
4. When the Magic Collaboration Studio pre-installation summary is displayed, follow the instructions on the screen. The summary should look like this: bash In the pre-installation summary, you should see the local IP address of your machine instead of "192.168.130.10" displayed in the example above.
5. Start the twcloud, zookeeper, and webapp services (in this particular order) by executing the following commands: bashDJangotrue
6. To ensure Magic Collaboration Studio services start after the reboot, execute the following commands: bashDJangotrue
7. Make sure that Magic Collaboration Studio is operational by going to *https://<server_address>:8443/webapp.* You should see the Magic Collaboration Studio login screen. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accessing Magic Collaboration Studio web applications' space=''].

When you complete the steps above, the preliminary Magic Collaboration Studio configuration is created. This means that your system is functional and you can login to Magic Collaboration Studio. After making sure that Magic Collaboration Studio is operational, you can optionally perform the described in the section below.

##### Post-installation Magic Collaboration Studio Configuration

The post-installation configuration is an optional step and should only be performed after making sure that Magic Collaboration Studio is operational.

To perform the post-installation Magic Collaboration Studio configuration

1. Edit the <install_root>/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf configuration file and make the following changes:
  - Change the value of the contact-points property to point to the listen_address you set in the cassandra.yaml file (i.e., contact-points = ["192.168.130.10:9042"]). This points Magic Collaboration Studio to the Cassandra database.
  - Use this parameter only if you need to deploy Magic Collaboration Studio in private network but client connect from public network. Change the value of the server-broadcast-host property to the public IP address instead of the local IP address. This way upon the initial connection the client application will know the external IP address to which it must connect (if Magic Collaboration Studio is installed behind a proxy or firewall with NAT).
2. Edit the*<install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties* configuration file and make the following changes:
  - Change the value of the cassandra.contactPoints property to the local IP address.
  - If you are accessing the server by FQDN, change the value of the **authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist** property by adding an the FQDN entry to the whitelist (e.g., **authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=https://FQDN:8443/,https://FQDN:8111/,https://md_redirect**). Make sure to add the public IP and/or FQDN for ports 8443 and 8111 to the whitelist.
3. Edit the <install_root>/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties configuration file and make the following changes:
  - Change the value of the twc.admin.username property to the username of a local account with Administrator privileges (the default value is Administrator).
  - Change the value of the twc.admin.password property to the password corresponding to the Administrator user (the default value is Administrator).
4. Restart the affected Magic Collaboration Studio services.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This chapter explains how to install Magic Collaboration Studio services by using the <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh</span></em> installation script.</p><p><br /></p><p>To install Magic Collaboration Studio services</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Install OpenJDK Java (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Third-Party Component Compatibility" /></ac:link> for version information), if needed. If you used the Apache Cassandra installation script on the same server, then Java is already installed and configured for the system. To install OpenJDK on a new Magic Collaboration Studio server, execute the following command:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0252ed62-d0f8-4cae-8674-479305a87444"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[yum -y install java-<version>-openjdk]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024x" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /><ac:link-body>Download the &lt;<em>product&gt;_&lt;version_number&gt;_installer_linux64.bin</em> file</ac:link-body></ac:link> and place it in the same location as the <em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d1afe86f-cfd7-45b4-b0da-35d0811a5fdb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></em> script.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Install Magic Collaboration Studio by executing the <em>install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh</em> script.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71e20ced-0d6a-4c68-8dfe-59bc9ab41bb7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo ./install_twc_mcs_centos_rhel.sh]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>When executing the installation script, press <strong>ENTER</strong> until you are asked to configure the machine IP.</li><li>Configure the machine IP by entering the local IP address or the host name of the machine (i.e. 192.168.130.10).</li><li>Configure the cluster seed node IP by entering the local IP address or the host name of the machine (i.e. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">192.168.130.10</span>). </li><li>Configure the Teamwork Cloud service owner by entering <strong>twcloud</strong>.</li><li>Configure<strong> </strong>JAVA_HOME by entering <strong>/etc/alternatives/jre_11</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Press <strong>Enter</strong> to choose the default /opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices installation folder or type another installation folder address<em>.</em></p></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the Magic Collaboration Studio pre-installation summary is displayed, follow the instructions on the screen. The summary should look like this:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="684f85c8-1f3e-4178-94de-b0193854bd3e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[===============================================================================
Pre-Installation Summary
------------------------
Please Review the Following Before Continuing:
  
Product Name:
    Magic Collaboration Studio
  
Install Folder:
    /opt/local/CATIANoMagicServices
  
Machine ip:
    "192.168.130.10"
  
Seed node ip:
    "192.168.130.10"
  
JAVA_HOME:
    "/etc/alternatives/jre_11"
  
Disk Space Information (for Installation Target):
    Required:  395,614,661 Bytes
    Available: 31,608,475,648 Bytes]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="810c5e55-11cd-41e6-9411-b659a889e8af"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the pre-installation summary, you should see the local IP address of your machine instead of &quot;<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">192.168.130.10&quot; displayed in the example above.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Start the twcloud, zookeeper, and webapp services (in this particular order) by executing the following commands:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee20ace7-8bc6-4e88-9173-78e241eb1d0d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl start twcloud
sudo systemctl start zookeeper
sudo systemctl start webapp]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To ensure Magic Collaboration Studio services start after the reboot, execute the following commands:</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef8fbc96-ae66-485e-858e-6971778e19ad"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">bash</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="theme">DJango</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="linenumbers">true</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[sudo systemctl enable twcloud
sudo systemctl enable zookeeper
sudo systemctl enable webapp]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Make sure that Magic Collaboration Studio is operational by going to <em>https://&lt;server_address&gt;:8443/webapp.</em> You should see the Magic Collaboration Studio login screen.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60704efe-55f4-467e-9f63-8e3e94932cf5"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Accessing Magic Collaboration Studio web applications" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p>When you complete the steps above, the preliminary Magic Collaboration Studio configuration is created. This means that your system is functional and you can login to Magic Collaboration Studio. After making sure that Magic Collaboration Studio is operational, you can optionally perform the <ac:link ac:anchor="Post-installation Magic Collaboration Studio Configuration"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[post-installation configuration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> described in the section below.</p><h3><br />Post-installation Magic Collaboration Studio Configuration</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2af96a7b-cc5b-4950-ac44-25d70fcffc5b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The post-installation configuration is an optional step and should only be performed after making sure that Magic Collaboration Studio is operational.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To perform the post-installation Magic Collaboration Studio configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Edit the<em> &lt;install_root&gt;/TeamworkCloud/configuration/application.conf</em> configuration file and make the following changes:<br /><ul><li>Change the value of the <strong>contact-points</strong> property to point to the <strong>listen_address</strong> you set in the <em>cassandra.yaml</em> file (i.e., contact-points = [&quot;192.168.130.10:9042&quot;]). This points Magic Collaboration Studio to the Cassandra database.</li><li>Use this parameter only if you need to deploy Magic Collaboration Studio in private network but client connect from public network. Change the value of the <strong>server-broadcast-host</strong> property to the public IP address instead of the local IP address. This way upon the initial connection the client application will know the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">external IP address to which it must connect (if Magic Collaboration Studio is installed behind a proxy or firewall with NAT).</span></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Edit the<em> &lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/authserver.properties</em> configuration file and make the following changes:</p><ul><li>Change the value of the<strong> cassandra.contactPoints </strong>property to the local IP address.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you are accessing the server by FQDN, change the value of the <strong>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist</strong> property by adding an the FQDN entry to the whitelist (e.g., <strong>authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist=<span class="nolink">https://FQDN:8443/,https://FQDN:8111/,https://md_redirect</span></strong>). Make sure to add the public IP and/or FQDN for ports 8443 and 8111 to the whitelist.</p></li></ul></li><li>Edit the<em> &lt;install_root&gt;/WebAppPlatform/shared/conf/webappplatform.properties</em> configuration file and make the following changes:<br /><ul><li>Change the value of the<strong> twc.admin.username</strong> property to the username of a local account with Administrator privileges (the default value is Administrator).</li><li>Change the value of the<strong> twc.admin.password</strong> property to the password corresponding to the Administrator user (the default value is Administrator).</li></ul></li><li>Restart the affected Magic Collaboration Studio services.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=137991269 space=MCS2024x version=4 -->
## PAGE 00126: Installing the FlexNet server

- page_id: `137991269`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/137991269/Installing+the+FlexNet+server
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:40:08.307+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio Installation > Installation on Linux using scripts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Collaboration Studio requires a FlexNet license server to operate. It can be installed on the same system or on a separate machine. This chapter explains how to use the *install_flex_centos_rhel.sh* automated deployment script, which downloads all required components, deploys the server, creates the systemctl service entry to control it, and creates the necessary firewall rules to allow the required traffic. The firewall rules are created for both the internal and public zones. The script may require modification depending on the zone where the interface is located. However, if the firewall is not running when the installation script is executed, the rules are not be created. In addition, the script creates the lmadmin user, which runs the lmadmin service.

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

- The FlexNet server requires the 64-bit Redhat LSB core files and the ld-linux library in order to execute.
- The install_flex_centos_rhel.sh script is configured for CentOS (Red Hat) 7 or Red Hat 8 , but can be modified for a different OS version as well.

To install the FlexNet server

1. Run the *install_flex_centos_rhel.sh* script to start the installation and follow the installer instructions. All the default values presented by the installer should be accepted except for the installation directory which should be changed when prompted (see step 2).
2. When you are prompted to choose the installation directory, change the default directory to <install_root> /FNPLicenseServerManager .
3. After the lmadmin service is installed, start it by executing the following command: bashDJangotrue
4. To check if the service is running, execute the following command: bashDJangotrue

##### Troubleshooting

If the Imadmin service failed to start, it is often because the built-in web server cannot resolve the hostname.

To check if this is the case, execute the following commands:

```bash
/FNPLicenseServerManager/logs
tail web.log]]>
```

You will see output similar to the following where *yourhostname* is the name of the host:

```bash

```

If this is the case, edit the */etc/hosts* file and add the following entry so the webserver could resolve the host:

```bash

```

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=143395518 space=MCS2024x version=7 -->
## PAGE 00127: Installing using the installer file on Windows

- page_id: `143395518`
- space_key: `MCS2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MCS2024x/pages/143395518/Installing+using+the+installer+file+on+Windows
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-11T13:40:49.932+01:00`
- ancestors: Magic Collaboration Studio Documentation > Deployment Guide > Magic Collaboration Studio Installation > Installation on Windows > Installing Magic Collaboration Studio on Windows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The installer file includes both Teamwork Cloud and Web Application Platform with its services in its installation package.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

If the installation path contains any of the following characters, like '[', ']', '(', ')', '!', '@', '#', '$', '#', '%', '{', '}', '+', '=', or ';' the installation procedure cannot be completed successfully and the login page will not load. If, for example, the installation path is *C:\Program Files\TeamworkCloud*, which does not contain any of the special characters, the installation will not fail.

****

To install Teamwork Cloud by using the installer file on Windows

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024x']Download the <*product>_<version_number>_installer_win64.exe* file .
2. Run the installer file as administrator to start the installation wizard.
3. Read the installation introduction and click Next .
4. Select the Advanced Install radio button and click Next .
5. Select the components you want to install and click **Next**. We recommend installing Web Application Platform and Teamwork Cloud on different machines for better performance. If you decide to install Web Application Platform on a separate machine, clear the appropriate checkbox. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Follow the on-screen instructions in the installation wizard and click Next after each step.
7. When the Pre-Installation Summary is displayed, check if all properties are specified correctly and click **Install**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. After successful installation, click **Done** to close the installation wizard.
9. Open the Task Manager and start the following services:
  - Teamwork Cloud service
  - Zookeeper service
  - WebApp service
10. Wait a few minutes until all services have started, then check if the system is working. You should be able to access Teamwork Cloud via https://FQDN:8443/webapp/ .

After completing the steps above, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting Magic Collaboration Studio on Windows' space=''].

##### Post-installation configuration (optional)

The installer created a preliminary configuration. If your system is still not operational, you may need to configure the following parameters manually.

- Edit the configuration file <install_root> \TeamworkCloud\configuration\application.conf . Search for contact-points = , located under the persistence esi.persistence. datastax-java-driver section, and replace localhost with ip_address_of_cassandra_node . Search for url = , located in the esi.auth section. Enter your server's IP address or FQDN (if accessing via FQDN).
- If you are accessing Teamwork Cloud Web UI via its FQDN:
  - Edit the configuration file <install_root> \ WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\authserver.properties . Search for authentication.redirect.uri.whitelist and append the following to the entry: https://FQDN:8443/webapp/
  - Edit the configuration file <install_root> \WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties. Add server.public.host and set it to FQDN.
- Edit the configuration file for Teamwork Cloud Admin: *<install_root>**\WebAppPlatform\shared\conf\webappplatform.properties*.
  - twc.admin.username - Set it to the username of a local account with Administrator privileges (default is Administrator).
  - twc.admin.password - Set it to the password corresponding to the Administrator user (default is Administrator).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
